description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. This is a worker-loop correctness bug independent of the child implementation beads for `ddx-c191e91a`.
title: work: stop retrying execution-ineligible decomposed parents

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go Land() invokes no git fetch and no git push; LandingGitOps interface no longer declares FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, or FetchOriginAncestryCheck methods.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
title: agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_SerializesGitOperations asserts both instruction variants contain a direct prohibition on parallel git/staging/commit operations and require sequential git add/git commit handling.
2. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_RequiresStagedPreCommitGate asserts both instruction variants say lefthook run pre-commit must be rerun after staging when staged files drive hooks and that no-staged-files runs are insufficient evidence.
3. Prompt snapshot or guardrail coverage is updated so future prompt edits preserve both rules.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadInstructions_(SerializesGitOperations|RequiresStagedPreCommitGate)|TestExecuteBeadInstructionsText' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.
- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.
- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change core gitlock recovery.
- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: execute-bead: serialize worker git commands and staged hook gates

No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The current e2e suite still treats `/sessions` as a standalone page, but the frontend has already shifted sessions into the run-layer experience. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.ts` redirects to `/runs?layer=run`, and the fallback Svelte page says the same. That redirect contract is breaking `app.spec.ts` TC-005, `sessions.spec.ts`, and several screenshot fixtures that still assume the old standalone Sessions page.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.ts:1-10` redirects sessions into runs.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte:1-7` documents the redirect fallback.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts:107-289` still exercises the old standalone sessions contract.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts:133-137` still snapshots the old sessions-driven pages, including the agent / project-overview / efficacy coverage that is currently failing.

PROPOSED FIX
Bring the sessions e2e coverage and the screenshot baselines up to the current run-layer redirect contract. Update the tests to assert the redirect into runs, the run-layer session expansion, and any screenshot baselines that changed because sessions are now surfaced in runs. Keep the fix within the frontend/e2e tree and the sessions route files.

NON-SCOPE
- The documents/beads fixture regressions from app.spec.
- The 2k scale fixture bead.
- The broader Store API migration.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts` passes against the current redirect contract.
2. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` passes for the affected pages.
3. `cd cli && bun run test:e2e -- --grep "TC-005|screenshots"` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The drain-queue golden tests are failing because the workers page now exposes more than one heading named `Workers`, and the Playwright spec is using a non-unique role query. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` calls `getByRole('heading', { name: 'Workers' })`, but the page also renders `Reported workers`, which makes the selector ambiguous in strict mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts:306-410` uses a heading locator that collides with the page’s section heading.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:1-120` and its rendered output define the page structure that exposes both the main page heading and the workers subsection heading.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the drain-queue test target the unique page heading or otherwise disambiguate the query so the golden flow can verify the worker dispatch and terminal-state assertions. Keep the fix confined to the drain e2e spec and any minimal accessibility label adjustment required to make the heading unique.

NON-SCOPE
- Worker dispatch semantics.
- GraphQL or backend worker state transitions.
- Other e2e specs.

ACCEPTANCE
1. Both drain-queue golden tests in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` pass.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/drain.e2e.ts` passes.

acceptance:
1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

16. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still exist.

Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

acceptance:
1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

acceptance:
1. `TestLefthookPreCommitIncludesDDxRootAudit` passes and asserts `lefthook.yml` wires the DDx-root audit into `pre-commit`.
2. `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` fails when a staged Go file reintroduces a hardcoded `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)` outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Even after production packages are migrated, the acceptance grep will keep failing because test files and non-test helpers still use literal `.ddx` joins, and `lefthook.yml` does not yet enforce the audit in pre-commit.

ROOT CAUSE
Representative test/helper hits remain in `cli/cmd/run_test_helpers.go:206`, `cli/cmd/bead_workspace_test.go:18`, `cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go:95`, `cli/internal/server/workers_test.go:311`, and many other `_test.go` fixtures. The current `lefthook.yml` pre-commit block has no rule running `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace remaining test/helper `.ddx` joins with shared helpers/constants so the full-tree audit can go green without changing fixture meaning.
- Add a pre-commit hook command in `lefthook.yml` that runs the exact audit and fails on any remaining hardcoded join outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/`.
- Add a config test that asserts the hook block is present and names the DDx-root audit.

NON-SCOPE
- Production-package migrations owned by sibling decomposition beads.
- Worktree registry CLI behavior.

DEPS
- Depends on the production audit children from this decomposition because the final full-tree grep cannot pass until they land.
title: ddx home: clear test .ddx joins and wire pre-commit audit

acceptance:
1. Default rules/vocabulary are present in the asset path chosen by TD-036 and include DDx terminology accepts plus unsupported filler rejects.
2. Focused tests run the rules against the fixture/golden cases and prove expected positives and negatives.
3. Project vocabulary override behavior is covered by a test using accept and reject entries.
4. No output includes an AI-authorship score or detector-bypass claim.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
CONTEXT
DDx needs deterministic default prose rules and vocabulary so the CLI can flag common AI-writing tropes without relying on subjective model judgment. The rules must be tuned for DDx technical documents and configurable by projects.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- Default prose rule/vocabulary assets under the path selected by TD-036
- Config schema/defaults for prose settings if TD-036 places them in this slice
- cli/internal/docprose tests that evaluate rules against the fixtures from the fixture bead

RULE REQUIREMENTS
- Detect unsupported generic praise and vague claims such as robust, seamless, comprehensive, industry-leading-style phrases when not backed by specifics.
- Detect repeated formulaic openings or filler transitions where practical.
- Support project vocabulary accept/reject lists.
- Support at least technical and planning modes; public mode may share defaults if TD-036 says so.
- Keep findings advisory metadata-ready: rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- ddx doc prose command wiring.
- Review integration.
- Any AI-authorship score.
title: Add default prose rules and DDx vocabulary

acceptance:
1. 5 fixture beads chosen and listed in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/comparison-fixtures.md with: bead ID, base-rev, original closing commit, characterization (trivial/refactor/test/multi-file/docs).
2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.
4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.
5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.
6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.
7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.
8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.
9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.
10. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.
notes: Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.
title: study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)

acceptance:
1. `ddx doc prose docs` before cleanup records the baseline finding count.
2. High-signal findings are edited without changing DDx commands, paths, IDs, frontmatter, headings, tables, or acceptance criteria.
3. `ddx doc prose docs` after cleanup records the final finding count.
4. Word-count delta is recorded, and any increases are justified by added specificity.
5. `ddx doc audit` passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After the Vale-backed checker, skill, and workflow hook work, DDx docs need one measured cleanup pass that applies high-signal prose findings and records the before/after result.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:361-369 says full-doc cleanup must wait until checker, skill, and workflow evidence paths are working. The cleanup needs to record finding count and word-count delta instead of silently rewriting docs.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Corpus-scale editorial cleanup is high-judgment. The agent must preserve DDx technical meaning, commands, paths, IDs, acceptance criteria, and rationale while reducing filler and AI-slop constructions where safe.

PROPOSED FIX
- Run the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` pass across `docs/**`.
- Apply only high-signal findings.
- Preserve DDx terminology, commands, paths, headings, tables, frontmatter, IDs, AC, and useful technical density.
- Record before/after finding count and word-count delta in the bead evidence or close notes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not tune checker rules in this bead.
- Do not rewrite code comments outside docs.
- Do not pursue low-confidence findings.

DEPS
Depends on workflow-hook evidence so cleanup uses the final integrated prose workflow.

description: Delete DDx's synthetic no-op compaction breaker after Fizeau removes the stale no-op compaction policy. Pure no-op compactions are silent in Fizeau, and DDx must not infer runtime failure from internal compaction telemetry. In-scope files: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go no-op compaction constants/streak/detection, agent_runner_service_test.go no-op compaction tests, session_index_test.go fixtures as needed. Out of scope: DDx queue-level no-progress cooldowns based on DDx-owned attempt outcomes.
title: Delete DDx no-op compaction breaker

description:
PROBLEM
  The conflict-recovery state machine (auto-merge + focused-resolve + close-or-park) is partially extracted but the description cites only the pre-extraction loop lines, leaving the executor without a clear picture of the current API boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:118 defines RunConflictRecovery(ctx, ConflictRecoveryInput) ConflictRecoveryOutput — the C4 extraction already landed. The bead description still refers to the old inline branch. The caller site is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 (loop branches on ShouldAttemptConflictRecovery, then calls RunConflictRecovery). The try.Attempt API (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:67, func Attempt(ctx, store, beadID, AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error)) does not yet call RunConflictRecovery; conflict recovery remains loop-owned.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Migrate RunConflictRecovery invocation from execute_bead_loop.go:847-878 into try.Attempt (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go) so Attempt's return Outcome already encodes conflict-recovery disposition.
  - Add try/conflict_recovery.go: thin adapter from ConflictRecoveryOutput to Outcome (Disposition=Merged → OutcomeSuccess, Park/NeedsHuman → OutcomePark).
  - Loop caller (execute_bead_loop.go:847) delegates to try.Attempt instead of calling RunConflictRecovery directly; removes the inline branch.
  - Add TestAttempt_ConflictRecovered_ReturnsMergedOutcome and TestAttempt_ConflictUnresolvable_ReturnsPark to cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Deleting the attempted/hookFailed maps (C7, ddx-06eb05d8).
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: fail-open at the layer boundary when conflict recovery is not applicable or cannot complete cleanly.
- P2: conflict recovery owns only its own conditions; the loop should delegate rather than infer recovery policy.
- P3: the worker panel should surface conflict-recovery transitions and any degraded path.
- P4: a bad bead X must not spill into bead Y; conflict recovery stays per-bead.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx does not have a written contract that separates broad interactive queue stewardship from worker execution. An agent opened in the repo can interpret queue questions as permission to start edits, while ddx work is meant to pick up execution-ready beads.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy without declaring a default interactive queue-steward mode. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work layers but not the interactive mode boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work", "what's on the queue", and "what's ready" through the ddx skill without specifying whether those prompts mean human intervention triage or worker execution.

PROPOSED FIX
Update AGENTS.md, FEAT-010, and FEAT-011 with an explicit mode taxonomy: queue_steward for broad interactive DDx questions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead worker prompts, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Define precedence so bead_execution supersedes only the queue-steward default, while tracker, merge-policy, and safety instructions remain load-bearing.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI behavior beyond static guidance tests.
- Changing tracker status enums or storage.
- Starting or managing workers automatically from interactive sessions.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: none.
title: agent-modes spec: document interactive stewardship modes

description:
PROBLEM
  The execute-bead subsystem files use execute_bead_* naming that conflates the orchestrator, the attempt, the review, and the loop under one prefix, making package boundaries and import graphs ambiguous. The refactor proposal was stored at /tmp/ and is unreadable post-session.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (drain loop), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (attempt), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (land coordinator), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (landing), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (review), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go (review triage), cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (disposition), and cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go (mirror) all share a flat package with unrelated concerns. The ddx try CLI command named in FEAT-010 does not exist (cli/cmd/try.go absent).

PROPOSED FIX
  Rename per the following table (no behavior change):
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go             -> cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go (already exists; merge or verify)
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/land.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/coordinator.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/review.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go -> cli/internal/agent/try/review_triage.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go       -> cli/internal/agent/try/disposition.go
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go         -> cli/internal/agent/work/drain.go
    cli/internal/agent/executions_mirror.go         -> cli/internal/agent/try/mirror.go

  Rename identifiers: ExecuteBeadWorker -> work.Worker, ExecuteBeadWorker.Run -> work.Worker.Drain, ExecuteBead -> try.Attempt (FEAT-006 + FEAT-010).

  Add cli/cmd/try.go with cobra command "ddx try <bead-id>" invoking try.Attempt directly (FEAT-010 surface).

  Update FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 spec docs to reflect new package paths. Keep ExecuteBead* shims as typedefs; deletion is C14.

NON-SCOPE
  - Behavior changes to the drain loop or attempt logic.
  - Deletion of ExecuteBead* typedefs (C14).
  - ddx-dc157075 file-path reference migration if that bead has not yet landed.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: file and identifier names should reflect the subsystem boundary and keep responsibilities separated.
- P5: ddx try as a first-class CLI surface makes the worker/attempt state easier for operators to see.
- P7: the refactor aligns names and prompt surface with the bead-as-prompt contract.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:dep, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

description:
PROBLEM
The execution/runtime packages still build project-state paths with literal `.ddx` joins, so run-state, evidence, worker liveness, and exec attachments will not follow convention-mode roots even after the CLI layer is fixed.

ROOT CAUSE
Representative direct joins remain in `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:57`, `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:216`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1790`, `cli/internal/exec/store.go:54`, `cli/internal/exec/bead_runtime.go:421`, `cli/internal/workerstatus/liveness.go:42`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:667`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go:235`, and `cli/internal/persona/loader.go:77`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add package-local helpers that route runtime/project-state paths through `ddxroot.Path` for `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, `internal/persona`, `internal/workerstatus`, `internal/processmetrics`, and `internal/agentmetrics`.
- Replace the remaining literal `.ddx` joins in those packages.
- Add focused tests proving run-state/evidence and exec attachment paths resolve against the DDx root chosen by `ddxroot.Path`.

NON-SCOPE
- `cli/cmd`, config/git, and server/graphql packages.
- Test-only files and the final full-tree hook gate.

DEPS
- No deps.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive queue stewardship needs a higher-level surface than ddx bead ready. Ready beads are worker-actionable and should usually be left to ddx work; operators need a command that exposes work ddx ready/work will not pick up plus a conservative signal when the ready queue is deep enough to justify starting another worker.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:7-16 lists ready, blocked, status, and list as queue inspection commands but has no human-intervention view. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 answers "What should I work on next?" with ddx bead ready. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:119-160 prints execution plan rows and skipped epics, but not a first-class intervention queue or worker-capacity recommendation.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a read-only command surface, proposed as ddx work focus, that summarizes human-required intervention separately from worker-ready work. It should show needs_human/review-blocked beads, blocked beads that require planning or dependency decisions, malformed or underspecified beads if lifecycle signals are available, ready-depth summary, active-worker/in-progress signals when observable, and unknown capacity hazards when not observable. It may recommend starting another worker only from observable queue depth/capacity signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Automatically starting workers.
- Closing, updating, or splitting beads.
- Replacing ddx work plan or ddx bead ready.

PARENT AND DEPS

description:
PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `work` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent work` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "work is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent work`, and deprecation text still names work rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `work` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `work-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent work` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "work|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent work`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `work-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent work` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.

title: agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature

1. TestBeadAutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock (or equivalent) runs two or more concurrent bead update/create operations with auto_commit=always against the same repo and proves all complete without .git/index.lock errors.
2. The same regression asserts commit history is audit-correct: each auto-commit either contains only the operation named in its subject or is an explicitly named combined commit that lists every included tracker mutation.
3. Existing stale-lock recovery tests TestBeadCreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, TestBeadUpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock, and TestBeadCloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock remain green.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestBead(AutoCommitSerializesThroughMainGitLock|CreateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|UpdateAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock|CloseAutoCommitRecoversStaleIndexLock)' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/gitlock/... ./internal/agent/... passes.

description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, a worker executing ddx-bdc0065e cancelled several stale beads. Multiple ddx bead update commands ran close together from the execute-bead context. The tracker ended clean, but the auto-commit audit trail was noisy: one commit subject said it updated ddx-387a0178 while it also picked up additional bead-row changes, another update failed with .git/index.lock, and another commit reported nothing to commit. That makes tracker history misleading and is the same class of dirty/index-lock noise the operator wants eliminated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:134-154 builds bead auto-commit config and calls bead.AutoCommitFilesWithRecovery directly. cli/internal/bead/git_autocommit.go:10-17 wraps git add/commit with gitlock.RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery, but that only recovers from stale/transient .git/index.lock; it does not serialize the whole add/commit pair under the process-shared main git lock. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:98-118 documents and implements withMainGitLock/withTrackerLock for primary-checkout git writes, and cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:22-24 says DDx writers serialize through that lock, but the bead CLI auto-commit path is outside it.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route bead CLI auto-commit add/commit operations through the same process-shared main git lock used by landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
- Preserve existing stale index.lock recovery inside the lock, so stale locks are still repaired and live owners still fail or wait according to policy.
- Add a concurrent regression where multiple bead update/create auto-commits run against one repo and produce either deterministic serialized commits or one explicitly combined commit; no index.lock failures, no misleading commit subjects that omit paths actually included.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change .ddx/beads.jsonl format.
- Do not remove auto-commit recovery for stale index locks.
- Do not squash, amend, or rewrite existing execute-bead history.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: bead: serialize auto-commit tracker git writes

description:
PROBLEM
The foundation bead for TD-027 split the bead storage migration into caller-facing and package-local slices, but the current `ddx-464feba2` child still bundles the Store API migration together with a repo-wide caller sweep. The local `cli/internal/bead` package work is still a valid, self-contained slice: `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` already declares ctx-first interfaces, while `cli/internal/bead/store.go` still exposes the legacy zero-arg methods (`Init`, `ReadAll`, `Get`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, `GenID`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:74-100` declares the ctx-first foundation interfaces.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:140-1181` still exposes the legacy no-ctx Store methods.
- `ddx-464feba2` was blocked because the broader caller migration exceeds that child slice.

PROPOSED FIX
Update only the core bead package surface so the Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` accept `ctx context.Context` as first parameter for the foundational non-event methods, and keep the verification local to `cli/internal/bead/...`. Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead. Update or add the store/package tests needed to prove the local API still behaves correctly after the signature change.

NON-SCOPE
- Caller migration in `cli/cmd`, `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/server`, `cli/internal/escalation`, and metrics packages.
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`).
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. The foundational Store methods in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` take `ctx context.Context` first: `Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, and `GenID`.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
4. Any tests added or updated in `cli/internal/bead/` are named and deterministic, with no caller-package changes in this bead.

reason=bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885): 1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.
2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.
3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare" -count=1` passes.
5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.
title: execute-bead: prevent worker attempts from mutating primary git config

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or a comparable suite) exists, parameterized over [Convention, InTree] modes and the nine listed scenarios.
2. The suite passes for every (mode, scenario) cell.
3. TestModeMatrix_TwoClonesShareXDG asserts two project worktrees pointing at the same git remote share the XDG project dir AND register both in worktrees.json with one auto-pinned as master.
4. TestModeMatrix_OperatorExpectationDocumented asserts the suite's package godoc clearly states the cross-mode equivalence invariant.
