description:
PROBLEM
The two principles of epic ddx-602ae31a (ddx home resolution + skills/personas install topology) must behave identically across both modes (in-tree and convention). Today there is no single test suite that drives the same scenarios through both modes; coverage is per-mode-ad-hoc and divergence between modes is invisible until operators hit it in production.

ROOT CAUSE
The two parent beads (ddx-06cbaa90 and ddx-937de9fc) each carry per-feature tests. Neither owns the cross-mode equivalence guarantee.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go (or equivalent suite under a new package) that parameterizes every behaviour-equivalence scenario across both modes:

  for mode in [Convention, InTree]:
    setup_fixture(mode)
    for scenario in [
      "bead-lifecycle":           create/show/update/close
      "bead-execution":           ddx try a bead, observe attempt evidence and state mutation
      "plugin-skill-resolution":  ddx-side plugin lookup honors precedence; agent-tier symlinks resolve to plugin content
      "persona-binding":          role → persona resolves correctly per binding config
      "worker-drain":             ddx work --watch picks beads and lands them
      "meta-prompt-sync":         CLAUDE.md (or equivalent) gets the right content
      "auto-commit-routing":      bead create/update auto-commit lands in the right git repo (project's vs XDG's)
      "attachments":              per-attempt event files written to the right anchor
      "index-lock-recovery":      gitlock recovery fires correctly in both modes
    ]:
      run scenario in current mode
      assert behaviour identical to the other mode modulo storage path

2. Fixture setup must be deterministic and isolated:
   - Convention mode fixture: tempdir as project root, no .ddx/; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed at a per-test tempdir; git remote set to a deterministic URL.
   - In-tree mode fixture: tempdir as project root WITH .ddx/ pre-created; XDG_DATA_HOME pointed elsewhere (must not be consulted).
   - Two clones of the same project share an XDG dir — explicitly tested as a separate sub-scenario.

3. Document the test-mode invariant in cli/internal/agent/test_mode_matrix_test.go's package doc: "Any behavior that diverges between Convention and InTree modes (other than the on-disk anchor) is a regression."

4. SPEC UPDATES — propagate the resolver/mode model to every doc that references .ddx/ paths or project state. Each spec update added in this bead's commit cites principle-numbered tags so future readers see the link:
   - TD-031 bead state machine — "Storage anchors via ddxroot.Path(); see ddx-06cbaa90."
   - SD-019 multi-project-server-topology — Project identity from `host/owner/repo`; reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - SD-020 multi-machine-coordinator-topology — same.
   - ADR-021 operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path — reference ddx-06cbaa90.
   - workers-sessions-operator-surface.md — worker status paths through ddxroot.Path().
   - plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture — FEAT-010 paths through resolver.
   - library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md — install precedence per ddx-937de9fc.
   - library/README.md — install topology overview.

NON-SCOPE
- New behaviours (every scenario in the matrix is already covered by ddx-06cbaa90 or ddx-937de9fc per-feature tests; this is the cross-mode equivalence layer).
- Migration tooling for existing in-tree repos (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
ddx-937de9fc (skills/personas/plugins).
title: config: TestModeMatrix — both modes pass the same scenario suite, spec citations updated

acceptance:
1. TestTry_ForceClaimBypassesCooldown asserts that ddx try <id> --force-claim --reason "operator unblock" picks up a bead with a future work-retry-after timestamp and executes it.
2. TestTry_ForceClaimRequiresReason asserts that ddx try <id> --force-claim without --reason exits non-zero with a clear error message.
3. TestTry_ForceClaimEmitsAuditEvent asserts that the bead's event log gains a kind=force-claim entry with the operator's reason text and timestamp.
4. TestWork_IgnoreCooldownDrainsCooledQueue asserts that ddx work --ignore-cooldown --reason "..." in a queue of 5 cooled beads picks them up in priority order.
5. TestForceClaim_PreservesCooldownFieldOnFailure asserts that if a force-claimed attempt fails, the bead's work-retry-after Extra value is unchanged (we override the gate, not the policy).
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When an operator wants to retry a bead currently in cooldown (work-retry-after set in the future), the only way today is to manually run ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after before each ddx try / ddx work invocation. With 14 beads in cooldown today the operator ran 14 such commands in sequence (and even then a single ddx bead clear-cooldown --all doesn't exist yet — that's ddx-63688d7b). There is no CLI surface for "I know this bead is cooled; run it anyway because I just resolved the underlying issue."

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/work.go and cli/cmd/try.go both honor work-retry-after via the standard ready-queue filter. There is no override flag. The escalation ladder and route-exclusion machinery is correct (and ddx-63688d7b will narrow when cooldowns even apply), but operators still need a per-invocation escape hatch for the cases where cooldown was set by a now-resolved condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --force-claim to ddx try <id>: ignore work-retry-after for the specified bead during this attempt. Emit a kind=force-claim event with actor=<assignee> and the timestamp the override was used. The cooldown field is NOT cleared; if the attempt fails again, the original cooldown remains in effect for queued workers.
- Add --ignore-cooldown to ddx work: drain mode, ignore work-retry-after across the queue for this worker session. Same event semantics.
- Both flags emit a structured event so the audit trail shows "operator forced through cooldown at time T".
- Both flags require an explicit --reason "<text>" string (operator must explain why). The reason is captured in the event body.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the cooldown-setting policy (ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Do not auto-clear cooldowns after a successful forced attempt (the bead's success implicitly invalidates any stale cooldown via its own state, but we don't mutate the field).
- Do not expose this flag in passthrough constraints; it's an operator override only.

DEPS
ddx-63688d7b (the cooldown-policy rework). The --ignore-cooldown semantics depend on what cooldowns mean post-63688d7b.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

description:
PROBLEM
Hidden lifecycle model calls in `ddx work --watch` can mutate the operator's project checkout before a bead attempt starts. Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.
`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.

PROPOSED FIX
Run all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.
title: work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.
- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.
- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.
- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.
- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.
- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.
- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).

NON-SCOPE
- Caching for resolvers other than documents.
- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).
title: perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation

description:
PROBLEM
The runs/[runId] page (built in S16.1) has no Evidence tab. Users cannot browse or download execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json, stderr logs, etc.) from the run-detail view. Whitelisting logic for safe inline rendering must be enforced to avoid serving binary or executable content inline.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (added by ddx-f04faa86) has no Evidence tab.
- No bundleFiles[] or bundleFile(path) resolver exists yet for the run-detail page (that resolver is ddx-6d132f62, a dep).
- No whitelist enforcement is implemented; any file could be rendered inline without size or extension checks.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add Evidence tab to runs/[runId]/+page.svelte (as a sibling tab to Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session).
- List every file in run's bundle with: path, size, mimeType.
- Whitelist: *.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB — render inline as raw text.
- Non-whitelisted or > 64KB: download link only. No Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview.
- Evidence tab lazy-loads on activation.
- Large files (> 64KB): show size + download button only.
- Bundle-root confinement enforced server-side on bundleFile(path) resolver.

NON-SCOPE
- Markdown rendering (deliberate: whitelist is raw-text only).
- Syntax highlighting.
- Hex preview of binary files.
title: Story 16.2: Evidence tab — bundle-file list + download + whitelist inline view

description:
PROBLEM
DDx should run prose checks during normal docs-changing attempts without users reminding the agent. Findings need to be captured as evidence and fed back while the attempt is still editable.

docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires `ddx try` / `ddx work` attempts that change Markdown under `docs/` to run `ddx doc prose --changed`, attach evidence, and keep prose advisory. No workflow hook currently implements that contract.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Workflow-hook policy is high-judgment because it touches execution finalization and evidence. A weak implementation could block correct work on advisory prose findings or run too late for agents to repair obvious prose issues.

PROPOSED FIX

NON-SCOPE

description:
PROBLEM
TD-037 prohibits numeric retry floors on beads. Retry escalation currently stores numeric `MinPower` floors in bead `Extra` through `TriagePowerHintKey`, and later reads them as dispatch input.

Delete `numericPowerFloorHint` and every call to it. Remove numeric floor writes from no-changes retry, route-exclusion retry, ladder-exhaustion handling, and any related tests. Keep retry escalation as execution policy/evidence inside the current attempt path; do not replace the removed behavior with another bead `Extra` key.

- `cli/cmd/try.go`

description:
PROBLEM
DDx pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0. Fizeau just shipped v0.14.1 with three routing-reliability fixes that together resolve the queue-stall problems we hit all session:

1. Bounded TCP dial timeout (5 s) for openai-compat clients — closes the gap that v0.14.0 left open (the IsDialError short-circuit was wired but the dial itself was still ~30s). This addresses fizeau-b5757624.

2. Policy-aware scoring under MinPower/MaxPower bounds — `--min-power 8` now correctly prefers cheaper in-bounds subscription candidates over metered higher-power candidates. Without this, ladder-aware routing in ddx-8a7a6843 (landed) does not consistently pick the right provider when both are eligible.

3. Proactive endpoint aliveness probing + ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — startup TCP-connect probe and background re-probe loop record per-provider reachability. The routing gate filters known-unreachable providers with FilterReasonEndpointUnreachable instead of dialing them every request. PersistRouteHealth (optional, path-based) persists probe results across ddx process boundaries, so a fresh ddx-try does not re-discover that bragi is dead. This addresses fizeau-7cd66e6b.

Until this bump lands, autorouting on this machine still pays ~5s per dead-provider dial on every new ddx-try, the cheap subscription is not always picked when in-power-bounds, and provider health is rediscovered fresh on each invocation. With the bump, the routing chain is end-to-end correct.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/go.mod line: `github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0`. Needs to be v0.14.1.

PROPOSED FIX
- `go get github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.14.1` from cli/, regenerate go.sum.
- `go build ./...` green.
- `go test ./internal/agent/... -run "Cooldown|Retry|Routing|Provider|Land|Lock" -count=1` green (smoke).
- Single commit, `chore(deps): bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1`.
- DOES NOT wire ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — that requires the DDxRoot epic (ddx-602ae31a) to settle on a path. Follow-up bead can wire it once DDxRoot lands. The bump itself unblocks the dial-timeout and policy-scoring fixes immediately; PersistRouteHealth defaults to off in v0.14.1 so the bump is safe without DDx-side wiring.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring PersistRouteHealth.
- Any DDx-side routing/escalation changes.
- Changes to provider configuration.

DEPS
None. This is a pure dependency bump.

TOP PRIORITY because every running drain on this machine pays the v0.14.0 dial-timeout cost on every dead-provider attempt, and the in-flight epic (ddx-602ae31a) child beads will reference the new behaviour as the baseline.
title: deps: bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1 — dial timeout, policy-aware scoring, endpoint probing

description:
# PROBLEM
The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:axon, area:migration, kind:feature, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore must exist), MIG bead (Migrator interface must exist).
title: IMP: JSONL→Postgres importer for production AxonStore migration (replaces legacy MigrateToAxon)

acceptance:
1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with exported Path(ctx, projectRoot) function.
2. TestDDxRoot_DefaultsToProjectRootDotDDx asserts Path returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx") in the default (no XDG fallback yet) case.
3. Static check: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. This is the regression guard for the audit. (The check is wired into lefthook as a pre-commit rule.)
4. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. All existing tests pass without modification.
5. The audit covers at minimum the 12 path-writers enumerated in the description. The bead's no_changes_rationale.txt (if used) MUST list every file modified by the audit, grouped by package.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes, including the new "no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths" rule.
description:
PROBLEM
Every place in the codebase that writes to .ddx/<something> today hardcodes filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...). This makes it impossible to relocate the DDx state to an XDG location for convention-mode projects without auditing and rewriting every call site. A leaked path means the convention-mode invariant ("no files written to project tree without a .ddx/ folder") breaks the moment that code path runs.

The full enumeration of touched paths is at minimum:
- .ddx/config.yaml
- .ddx/beads.jsonl
- .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
- .ddx/attachments/<id>/
- .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
- .ddx/run-state/<id>.json
- .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl
- .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
- .ddx/plugins/<name>/
- .ddx/workers/<id>/
- .ddx/agent-logs/
- .ddx/scratch/
- and likely 10+ more across cli/internal/server/, cli/cmd/, library/checks/, scripts/

ROOT CAUSE
There is no single accessor that returns "the DDx root for this project". cli/internal/config/ has GetMetaPrompt() and similar config getters, but no DDxRoot(ctx) function. Every path-writer reaches into the file system directly.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a new package cli/internal/ddxroot with a single public function: `Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string`. Today returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"); a follow-up bead (E2) extends it to fall back to XDG when projectRoot/.ddx does not exist.
2. Audit every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment. Replace `filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...)` with `filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), ...)`. The audit must cover cli/cmd/, cli/internal/, library/checks/ (where applicable to ddx-side paths).
3. Add a build-time linter check (or a pre-commit hook lint rule) that fails the build if a new `filepath.Join.*"\\.ddx"` literal appears outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Use go vet via a custom analyzer OR a simple ripgrep-based pre-commit hook (lefthook).
4. The resolver MUST NOT change behaviour in this bead: for any projectRoot today, ddxroot.Path returns exactly filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"). This bead is a pure refactor — no functional change.
5. Existing tests must continue to pass without modification. If any test fails, it has hardcoded .ddx in a way that bypassed the resolver — fix that test.

NON-SCOPE
- XDG fallback path (E2 bead).
- Convention mode bootstrap (E2 bead).
- `ddx init --global` (E3 bead).
- Manifest overlay (E5 bead).
- Migration of existing data (none required; behaviour is identical).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor that the rest of the epic builds on.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: DDxRoot resolver + audit every .ddx/ path writer

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteOnService_PromptFeaturesReachServiceRequest|TestExecuteOnService_EstimatesPromptTokensWhenRuntimeOmitsEstimate|TestPreClaimIntakeHook_DispatchesWithStandardProfileNoStrongPowerTrick' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestFizeauAutoRoutingDefaultWorkRequestLeavesRouteUnpinned|TestExecuteOnService_MinMaxPowerReachServiceRequest' -count=1` passes.
3. `rg -n 'EstimatedPromptTokens:|RequiresTools:' cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` shows both fields populated in the `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest` path used by `RunWithConfigViaService`.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Readiness and other profile-driven DDx executions can reach Fizeau route selection with missing prompt feature inputs. The observed failure was `ResolveRoute: no live provider supports prompt of 0 tokens with tools=false at policy smart`; the prompt text was present, but DDx did not reliably forward `EstimatedPromptTokens` and `RequiresTools` into `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:66` has `AgentRunRuntime.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `AgentRunRuntime.RequiresTools`, so the call site has the data available.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244` constructs the `agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest` passed to Fizeau; this request must include prompt feature fields for routing.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:253` builds a real readiness prompt, so a zero-token request at route-selection time is a DDx request-envelope bug, not an empty readiness prompt.

PROPOSED FIX
Forward `AgentRunRuntime.EstimatedPromptTokens` and `AgentRunRuntime.RequiresTools` into `ServiceExecuteRequest`. When runtime omits an estimate but DDx has prompt text or a prompt file, compute a conservative nonzero estimate before dispatch so Fizeau never receives `EstimatedPromptTokens=0` for a nonempty prompt. Keep concrete model/provider selection in Fizeau; DDx only forwards request facts.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/passthrough_envelope_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `triage.estimated_difficulty` parsing or power inference.
- Do not pin provider/model/harness/profile to work around routing.
- Do not persist prompt token estimates on beads.
- Do not add alternate routing paths in DDx.
title: routing: forward prompt feature hints to Fizeau requests

acceptance:
1. All 15 children closed.
2. ExecuteBeadWorker.Run replaced by work.Worker.Drain (~30 lines).
3. 18 store-error-as-loop-exit smells eliminated; transient store error during outcome-commit logs and continues.
4. attempted/hookFailed maps deleted; pick derives exclusion from drain.result.
5. Routing preflight runs once at Drain startup, never per iteration.
6. ddx work mode semantics preserved: default is drain-until-no-ready-work; --once runs one ready bead then exits; --watch stays alive and sleeps for the idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist; --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error; no public compatibility alias remains for the removed polling flag.
7. ddx try <bead-id> CLI command exists.
8. File/identifier rename per package split complete; ExecuteBead* shims deleted.
9. JSONL event shapes (bead.result, execute-bead, review, routing, triage-decision, loop-error) unchanged - verified by C0 fixture baseline.
10. Server worker integration (cli/internal/server/) compiles and behaves identically except for the explicit once/drain/watch mode contract; tests green at every phase.
11. ddx-c6e3db02, ddx-b24e9630, ddx-cfedee8e, ddx-a8718bec, ddx-dc157075, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, and ddx-dc300be9 behaviors preserved per C5/C9/C10 absorption ACs.
12. FEAT-006 + FEAT-010 cross-refs updated to reflect new package paths and the mode + idle_interval worker-loop vocabulary.
description:
ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. The execute-bead -> try/work package-split epic still routes through cli/internal/agent and preserves removed ddx agent compatibility assumptions, so it must not dispatch.
title: REFACTOR: execute-bead surface → try / work packages aligned to FEAT-010

acceptance:
1. Add `TestCommitTrackerConventionModeCommitsXDGTracker` proving `CommitTracker(projectRoot)` commits `beads.jsonl` in the XDG ddxroot repository when `projectRoot` has no `.ddx/`, and does not try to stage `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in the project worktree.
2. Add `TestAttemptMetricsUseDDxRootPath` proving `attemptmetrics.AppendRow`, `LoadRows`, and `LoadAttemptIDs` read/write `${ddxroot.Path(projectRoot)}/metrics/attempts.jsonl` in convention mode.
3. Add `TestWorkPlanUsesDDxRootPath` and `TestWorkClearCooldownsUsesDDxRootPath` proving those commands read the convention-mode bead store via `ddxroot.Path` rather than `projectRoot/.ddx`.
4. Harden the `ddxroot-path-audit` pre-commit/CI rule so a production Go file containing `projectRoot + "/.ddx"`, `WorkingDir + "/.ddx"`, or an equivalent DDx-managed state root bypass fails outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/` and documented test fixtures.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/attemptmetrics ./cmd -run 'TestCommitTrackerConventionModeCommitsXDGTracker|TestAttemptMetricsUseDDxRootPath|TestWorkPlanUsesDDxRootPath|TestWorkClearCooldownsUsesDDxRootPath' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The landed zero-footprint refactor introduced `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` and a pre-commit audit, but the audit only catches `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)`. Production code still has direct `.ddx` state-root consumers that will keep reading or committing the project worktree instead of the XDG state repository in convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1791-1837` stats the tracker via `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "beads.jsonl")`, but then checks/stages/commits literal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in `projectRoot`. In convention mode that never commits the XDG tracker repo.
`cli/internal/attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:19-101` defines `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` as a relative constant and joins it directly under `projectRoot`, bypassing `ddxroot`.
`cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-87` and `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go:46-47` open `projectRoot + "/.ddx"` directly, so convention-mode projects show empty or wrong queues.
The new audit command in `lefthook.yml` only searches for `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx")`, so these regressions are invisible to pre-commit.

PROPOSED FIX
Route the concrete residual state-root consumers above through `ddxroot.Path` / `ddxroot.JoinProject`. When committing tracker state in convention mode, run git in the actual DDx state root repository and stage `beads.jsonl` relative to that repo; preserve existing in-tree behavior and execute-bead merge-policy commits. Update the audit so it fails on production direct state-root patterns such as `projectRoot + "/.ddx"`, `WorkingDir + "/.ddx"`, and constants joined under projectRoot for DDx-managed state, while still allowing relative artifact path strings used inside worker prompts and git pathspec filters.

NON-SCOPE
Do not migrate this repository out of in-tree mode. Do not rewrite prompt text that intentionally tells agents where evidence appears in an execution worktree. Do not change the bead storage format or attachment externalization policy.
title: ddx home: route residual state-root consumers through ddxroot

acceptance:
1. All 5 child beads (E1-E5) closed.
2. Convention mode end-to-end test: clone a fresh ddx-using project (no .ddx/), run `ddx bead list`, verify (a) no files written to project tree, (b) XDG project dir created with git repo, (c) bead list returns convention defaults.
3. In-tree mode end-to-end test: run `ddx init` in a fresh git repo, verify .ddx/ created in worktree, verify subsequent commands route there.
4. Override precedence test: place .ddx/config.yaml with a non-default value, verify it overrides global + convention.
5. Path-audit lint: `rg "filepath\.Join\([^)]*\\.ddx" cli/` returns zero hits outside the DDxRoot package (regression guard).
description:
EPIC

Eliminate the .ddx/ footprint from arbitrary git repos using DDx, while keeping today's in-tree behavior available for projects that want it (DDx itself, HELIX, etc.). Make init effectively touchless: zero ceremony for the common case.

DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Single rule: presence of <project>/.ddx/ determines the DDx root.
- If <project>/.ddx/ exists → in-tree mode. Root = <project>/.ddx/. Operator commits or gitignores per their own .gitignore. DDx never manipulates .gitignore.
- If <project>/.ddx/ does NOT exist → convention mode. Root = ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/). The XDG directory is itself a git repo containing all DDx state for that project; the project's own git repo stays pristine.

PROJECT ID is the human-readable remote URL host/owner/repo triple, parsed from `git remote get-url origin`. SSH URLs (`git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`) and HTTPS URLs both resolve to the same path. Projects without a remote fall back to `local/<basename>-<abs-path-short-hash>/`. The slashes are filesystem-real, so the XDG path is browsable: `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx/`.

OVERRIDE PRECEDENCE at command time:
1. In-tree (<project>/.ddx/config.yaml) — top priority
2. Global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml) — installed by `ddx init --global`
3. Convention defaults — baked into the binary

Each layer is a delta on the one below. A project with no overrides commits zero new files and writes zero files into its worktree.

ENGINEERING IMPERATIVE

EVERY path in the codebase that today reads or writes under .ddx/ must route through a single DDxRoot(ctx) resolver. No filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) calls outside that resolver. A regression linter asserts this invariant. The audit is the hard part of this epic.

KNOWN-HARD QUESTION (worth solving but not blocking):

When multiple worktrees of the same project (same remote URL) exist on one machine — e.g. `~/Projects/ddx` and `~/work/ddx-experiment` — they share the same XDG state. SHAs in events are unambiguous, but operator UX for "where do I cd to inspect this" is. Solution direction: each XDG project keeps a worktrees.json registry. One entry is marked master (operator pin or first-seen). Cross-references in audit logs and bead show output cite the master path.

CHILD BEADS

- E1: config: DDxRoot resolver + audit every .ddx/ path writer
- E2: config: convention mode bootstrap (XDG git repo + worktree registry)
- E3: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas
- E4: config: ddx init creates .ddx/ in project, switches to in-tree mode
- E5: config: optional .ddx/config.yaml overlay for per-project overrides

NON-SCOPE
- Migration of existing in-tree repos (today's DDx repo, HELIX) is a separate concern. They stay in-tree mode by virtue of having .ddx/ already.
- Server-backed state (federation) is orthogonal. XDG state can be a git repo with a remote; pushing that remote IS the team-share mechanism.
- Manifest-only mode is rejected — the convention/in-tree split makes a separate manifest file unnecessary.
title: config: XDG-rooted DDx state — convention mode + in-tree mode + touchless init

description:
PROBLEM
The work applies bead-scoped cooldowns to infra-class failures, contradicting documented policy and violating the forward-progress invariant. Observed today: 14 beads all routed to a temporarily-unreachable LM Studio host received 15-min cooldowns in sequence, then the worker went idle with the entire queue gated for 15 minutes despite cloud providers being reachable. A separate worker also hit a 2nd-strike preclaim cooldown after .ddx/beads.jsonl was momentarily staged between two parallel workers' tracker commits — recoverable in milliseconds, but the policy parked the bead for minutes anyway. Both cases produced the failure the operator named: "cooling down every bead in the queue when we are failing to find a provider, how is that going to fix anything?"

ROOT CAUSE — code violates existing spec
The forward-progress invariant and the right cooldown policy are already documented; the code does not honor them.

- reliability-principles.md P6 (docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md:35): "Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race -> no cooldown, return to ready." Today's code violates this for `provider_connectivity` and `no_viable_provider`.
- ADR-024 §Infrastructure And Rate-Limit Fallback (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:96): "Infrastructure failures are not capability failures... do not consume escalation budget... apply a retry-after cooldown only when time passing could plausibly fix the same attempt class." Loose "or" wording lets the current implementation skirt the intent.
- TD-031 §3.1 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:75): "Continuous forward progress is the default. Before a bead can move to `status=proposed`, the drain loop MUST exhaust every applicable automatic path in priority order." Says MAY set retry-after for `no_viable_provider`; the code unconditionally sets 15 min regardless of whether alternative routes exist.

Implementation sites that violate the spec:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2283-2284 — provider_connectivity sets bead cooldown unconditionally after applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion already recorded the failed route. With ddx-71629938 + fizeau-8134d6db landed, the exclusion is the correct mechanism; the cooldown is now redundant deadweight.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2294-2306 — no_viable_provider sets bead cooldown unconditionally; should transition the WORKER to a paused state (like paused-quota), not penalize the bead.
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:96-107 — preclaim-hook-failed sets bead cooldown on 2nd strike regardless of whether the cause is bead-specific (real divergence) or systemic (transient tracker race / index lock). The existing isSystemicPreClaimError path correctly avoids cooldown for global failures; staged-tracker / index-lock races belong in the same class.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:~968 (NoReadyWork branch) — when every ready bead is in cooldown for the same infra reason, the worker goes idle instead of transitioning to a paused-infra state and resuming when the condition clears. Violates "forward progress in all possible scenarios."

PROPOSED FIX
1. Restore code-to-spec conformance for infra-class outcomes:
   - provider_connectivity: drop SetExecutionCooldown call; the work-failed-routes entry + RouteExclusionWindow TTL is the correct mechanism. Bead is immediately reclaimable; next attempt routes around the failed (provider, model).
   - no_viable_provider: drop SetExecutionCooldown; instead transition the WORKER to paused-infra (new state), poll every 2 min, leave every bead immediately reclaimable.
   - preclaim-hook-failed: classify staged-.ddx/beads.jsonl-only and index-lock-races as systemic-transient (existing isSystemicPreClaimError path); skip cycle without cooldown. Only bead-specific persistent failures (across distinct attempts) trigger cooldown.
2. Add paused-infra worker state to TD-031 §4 with entry/exit conditions mirroring paused-quota. Surface in workers panel (P5 operator-visible state) with resume-at timestamp.
3. Worker idle-check (execute_bead_loop.go:~968): before returning NoReadyWork, scan whether every active cooldown shares an infra-fault status. If yes, transition to paused-infra with the earliest cooldown.until as the resume target. Worker sleeps until then, then re-evaluates the full queue.
4. Tighten loose spec wording:
   - TD-031 §3.1 line 73 — "MAY set retry-after (15 min)" for no_viable_provider becomes "MAY set retry-after only when no alternative routing path exists and the condition is purely time-dependent; when alternate routes exist, leave bead immediately reclaimable."
   - ADR-024:96 — replace ambiguous "or" with a crisp rule: cooldown applies only when no alternate route exists AND the condition is purely time-dependent.
5. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead clear-cooldown [--all] [--reason <class>]` clears work-retry-after on matching beads. Today this requires per-bead `ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after` (14 invocations today).

NON-SCOPE
- Escalation ladder behavior (ddx-8a7a6843 already in progress).
- Fizeau-side route health (fizeau-8134d6db already shipped in v0.14.0).
- Cooldown for genuinely bead-faulting outcomes (no_progress, no_changes with rationale, repair_cycle_exhausted) stays unchanged — those are documented bead-fault cases where time passing may help.
title: work loop: rework cooldown policy — code violates documented forward-progress invariant

description:
PROBLEM
DDx service-run code still understands too much of Fizeau's inner event stream. It should forward/store Fizeau ServiceEvents opaquely and adapt only the final data needed for DDx Result bookkeeping.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:243-355 switches on raw service-event names and reconstructs transcript/progress/tool-call records.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go also owns DDx-specific fallback policy for internal Fizeau events that should not drive DDx runtime outcomes.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go still exposes service event handling surfaces that make it easy for DDx callers to couple to Fizeau internals.

PROPOSED FIX
Collapse DDx's Fizeau service consumption to two responsibilities: store/forward opaque ServiceEvents unchanged, and decode only final-result/routing/session artifact fields required to populate DDx Result and indexes. Prefer Fizeau typed decoders such as agentlib.DecodeServiceEvent where decoding is necessary; remove local stringly-typed event constants and custom partial structs that duplicate Fizeau schema.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau event schema changes.
- Deleting the legacy direct Claude parser; ddx-64c24626 handles that after opaque forwarding is in place.
- Deleting session-log tailing; ddx-f948b7a4 handles that after opaque forwarding is in place.
title: Forward Fizeau events opaquely and keep only final-result adapter

description:
PROBLEM
DDx still contains a legacy direct Claude stream-json execution/transcript path. After DDx runs agent work through Fizeau, this alternate code path violates the boundary and can produce divergent transcript semantics from Fizeau.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go contains runClaudeStreamingFn, parseClaudeStream, claudeStreamArgsUnsupported, and Fizeau-style event synthesis from Claude stream-json.
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go and tests still allow DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH / legacy fallback routing to select DDx-owned parser behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete the DDx-owned Claude stream parser and any execution fallback that can route transcript-producing agent work through it. DDx should invoke Fizeau for agent execution and consume Fizeau events/results through the opaque/final-result adapter.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx final Result projection from Fizeau final events.
- Fizeau harness internals.
- Historical artifact readability for already-created logs.
title: Delete legacy direct agent execution transcript handling

description:
PROBLEM
  The DDx skill tree is still managed through parallel bootstrap copies instead of a single package-owned source. The governing plan at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:16-23 requires the default `ddx` plugin package to own the DDx skill, requires `ddx init` and `ddx plugin install ddx` to share one package installer path, and keeps home-directory skill installs out of scope.

ROOT CAUSE
  Current state is split across several bootstrap paths: cli/cmd/init.go:256-287 copies embedded skills with `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, registers `.ddx/skills/ddx` separately, then installs the default plugin through `registry.InstallPackage`; cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 handles package skills by calling `skills.Install` directly instead of applying manifest skill mappings; cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 has a separate local symlink implementation; cli/Makefile:65-84 keeps copied skill mirrors in sync. The plan requires these paths to collapse into package install semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
  Break the migration into child beads that first make package install semantics correct, then route init through the embedded package installer, then move the skill source into the default package layout, and finally update docs/specs/help.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not execute the child implementation work in this epic bead.
  - Do not restore global or home-directory skill installs.
  - Do not change public plugin CLI names beyond the separate CLI rationalization work.

PARENT + DEPS
  Root epic. No deps.
title: skills package: migrate DDx skill ownership to the default plugin package

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` still reports readiness decode failures and then fail-opens into execution. Two observed shapes are now known:
- `waivers_applied` may be emitted as a flat string list, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied of type agent.preClaimReadinessWaiver`.
- `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` may be emitted as a single string, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.suggested_child_beads.acceptance of type []string`.

Both failures make the readiness model unavailable even though the model produced a semantically usable classification. This keeps readiness from contributing route/difficulty signals and produces noisy fail-open logs during normal `ddx work --watch` operation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:60-72` decodes the readiness classification payload directly into strict Go structs. `preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.SuggestedChildren` is `[]preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild`, while `preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.Acceptance` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:148-152` requires `[]string`. `WaiversApplied` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:71` requires structured objects. `decodeReadinessClassificationPayloadWithMode` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:584-588` fails the whole readiness classification on any JSON shape mismatch. The prompt text at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:424-431` asks for `suggested_child_beads` and `waivers_applied` but does not give enough defensive handling for common model scalar/list variants.

PROPOSED FIX
Add small JSON normalization types for readiness model-only fields so decoding accepts the observed variants without creating new execution paths. Specifically: normalize `waivers_applied` from omitted/null/empty array/flat string list/object list, and normalize `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` from either a string or a string array. Keep the internal readiness result behavior unchanged unless existing code already consumes these fields. Tighten the readiness prompt so it documents the preferred shapes and explicitly says scalar fallbacks are tolerated only by the decoder.

NON-SCOPE
Do not persist readiness waiver or child-bead suggestion data to bead metadata. Do not add numeric difficulty/score metadata. Do not implement automatic decomposition behavior here. Do not change the easy/medium/hard routing contract.
title: readiness: accept model-output waiver and child-acceptance variants

description:
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:archive, area:interchange, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

acceptance:
1. FEAT-011 no longer describes `skills/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, `.ddx/skills/ddx`, and `cli/internal/skills/ddx` as five maintained checked-in shipped paths.
2. FEAT-015 states that `ddx init` installs the default DDx package through the embedded package installer, registry installs write real project files, and local installs create project-local symlink overlays without updating pins or auto-committing.
3. SD-011 states that canonical DDx skill source lives under `library/skills/ddx` and that `.agents/skills/ddx` plus `.claude/skills/ddx` are package installer outputs.
4. Generated AGENTS/CLAUDE guidance and relevant command help no longer instruct users to commit `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a DDx skill mirror.
5. `rg -n 'skills/ddx/|cli/internal/skills/ddx|\.ddx/skills/ddx|five shipped|copy-skills' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-011-agent-skills.md AGENTS.md cli/cmd` returns only intentionally historical or migration-plan references.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The governing specs still describe the old copied-bootstrap skill layout and need to be aligned with the package-owned DDx skill model so future workers do not reintroduce copied mirrors or home skill installs.

ROOT CAUSE
  FEAT-011 documents the five-copy model at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:214-244 and acceptance checks across five paths at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:295-317. FEAT-015 still says `ddx init` copies bootstrap skills to `.ddx/skills`, `.agents/skills`, and `.claude/skills` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:128-166, even though its 2026-05-12 amendment at lines 16-34 already requires no home skill installs and local symlink overlays. SD-011 still says skill source lives in `cli/internal/skills/ddx` and `ddx init` writes `.ddx/skills/ddx` at docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-011-agent-skills.md:91-112.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Update FEAT-011 to name `library/skills/ddx` as the canonical package-owned skill source and describe generated/install outputs instead of five checked-in mirrors.
  - Update FEAT-015 init and plugin-install sections so `ddx init` uses the embedded default package installer and local overlays link package skill children from manifest mappings.
  - Update SD-011 to match the package-owned source, embedded package copy, and project-local output paths.
  - Update generated AGENTS/CLAUDE guidance or command help snippets that still describe `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a committed bootstrap mirror.

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementing the installer behavior; sibling code beads own implementation.
  - Reopening legacy compatibility guidance for old skill names.
  - Adding home-directory skill installation instructions.

PARENT + DEPS
  Parent: ddx-6862f2ac. Depends on implementation beads so docs can describe the final behavior, not an intermediate state.

description:
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

acceptance:
1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
2. TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
- TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
While validating the landed `ddx-7eb97e4e` side-worktree reporting fix on 2026-05-15, the broad agent package gate failed once in `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded`. The failure closed only 4 of 5 seeded beads and `git show HEAD:bead-0005.txt` failed with `fatal: path 'bead-0005.txt' does not exist in 'HEAD'`. A direct rerun of the same test immediately passed, so this is either a real concurrent worker race or a nondeterministic integration-test assumption. Either way, this test currently cannot serve as a trustworthy gate for concurrent bead landing safety.

Observed failing command:
`cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1`

Observed focused rerun:
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded -count=1 -v` passed in 0.483s.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:408-423` starts 5 workers concurrently with `Once:false` against one store and shared project root.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:432-451` asserts all 5 beads are closed and all bead files are reachable from HEAD, but the failure showed one bead missing from both the tracker terminal state and landed git state.
- The current test does not dump per-worker `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, claimed bead IDs, terminal statuses, or preserved refs on failure, which makes it impossible to distinguish an actual claim/land race from a test harness timing assumption using only CI output.

PROPOSED FIX
Reproduce under stress, then either fix the underlying concurrent claim/land behavior or make the test harness deterministic without weakening the invariant. Add failure diagnostics that print each worker result, bead lifecycle summary, and git refs when the invariant fails. The invariant must remain: five concurrent workers over five ready beads eventually close and land all five distinct beads exactly once.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not skip, delete, or loosen the concurrent landing invariant.
- Do not hide the test behind short mode.
- Do not change production queue ordering or retry policy except where a real race is proven and fixed.

DEPS
None.
title: agent: stabilize concurrent script-harness landing test

4. Existing bead.result/review event consumers remain compatible; if durable field names are retained for compatibility, the test documents those retained names explicitly.
