description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

description:
PROBLEM
After `ddx-c9b6fb51` landed, the next live readiness check still produced a warn-only decode failure on 2026-05-15 while evaluating `ddx-5cc92777`: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied.criteria of type []string`. The previous fix tolerated scalar `waivers_applied` and flat string arrays, but not a waiver object whose `criteria` field is a scalar string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:223-229` defines `preClaimReadinessWaiver.Criteria` as `[]string`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:272-279` unmarshals object entries directly into that strict struct before `normalizeReadinessStringList` can run, so legacy/model output like `{"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"..."}]}` fails the entire readiness check. Existing coverage at `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go:291-304` only covers object waivers with `criteria` already encoded as an array.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small custom unmarshal path for waiver object entries so `criteria` accepts either a JSON string or a JSON string array and normalizes to `[]string`. Keep `criteria` requested as an array in prompts and skill text; this is compatibility-only tolerance for legacy/model output. Do not loosen decisive readiness fields.

NON-SCOPE
Do not use waiver metadata for routing, power inference, ordering, or bead metadata. Do not add another readiness schema path. Do not change warn-only readiness policy, timeout behavior, or canonical readiness classifications.

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestTryResult_PrintsLandingCoordinates|TestTrySideWorktreeLanding_PrintsNoRewriteRescueCommand' -count=1` passes. The tests prove a successful side-worktree `ddx try` output includes `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `project_root`, and a `git merge --no-ff <branch>` rescue command.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLandResult_CarriesResolvedTargetBranch|TestApplyLandResult_PropagatesTargetBranchToReport' -count=1` passes and proves the resolved branch from `Land()` reaches `ExecuteBeadReport` / `agent/try.Report`.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestLandConflictUnresolvableEventIncludesRescueCommand -count=1` passes and proves the parked-conflict event body includes `preserve_ref` plus a no-rewrite rescue command using `git merge --no-ff`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-05-15 Fizeau grendel-off routing incident, the operator had to run a targeted `ddx try fizeau-a54e0299` from a clean side worktree because the active fizeau checkout was dirty. The worker succeeded and printed `✓ fizeau-a54e0299 → merged (0d70176c)`, but that success was branch-local to the side worktree branch `ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516`. The active fizeau `master` checkout later advanced independently and still did not contain the route-time health fix. The operator had to inspect git history manually, notice that `master` lacked the landed branch, create a rescue branch, and run `git merge --no-ff ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516` to make the successful bead reachable from current project history.

This is not a model/routing bug. It is DDx operator UX and audit-safety around side-worktree landings. A successful `ddx try` must make the landing coordinates and recovery command unambiguous when the code lands in a worktree branch that is not the operator's active target branch.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/try.go:503-514` writes the terminal try result but only prints `bead`, `status`, `detail`, `result_rev`, and `preserve_ref`. It omits existing `ImplementationRev` / `LandedRev` fields and has no branch/worktree/rescue summary, so `merged` looks complete even when it landed on a side branch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1056-1068` resolves the target branch internally, and `LandRequest.TargetBranch` documents the branch contract at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:88-90`, but `LandResult` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:111-130` does not carry the resolved target branch back to reports.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-430` already has `ImplementationRev` and `LandedRev`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-47` already mirrors them. The data exists but the CLI result surface does not expose enough to distinguish worker commit, landed branch tip, and branch-local landing.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:212-220` records unresolvable land conflicts with `preserve_ref`, but it does not attach an operator-ready rescue command such as creating a clean worktree and merging the preserve ref with `--no-ff`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add resolved landing metadata to the success path: `LandResult.TargetBranch`, and propagate it through `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, `agent/try.Report`, and the try adapter.
- Update `ddx try` output to always print `implementation_rev` and `landed_rev` when they differ, and to print `landed_branch` / `project_root` for landed attempts.
- When `ddx try` is run from a side worktree or non-default branch, print a clear follow-up block naming the branch-local landing and the no-rewrite rescue command: `git merge --no-ff <landed-branch>` from the desired target worktree, or a `git worktree add ... && git merge --no-ff ...` variant when the active checkout is dirty.
- For `land_conflict_unresolvable` / `land_conflict_operator_required`, include a rescue command in the bead event body and terminal detail using the preserve ref, not just the ref name.
- Keep merge-policy intact: do not squash, rebase, or rewrite execute-bead histories. The rescue path must preserve the worker commits and tracker/evidence commits.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider health, or grendel-specific behavior.
- Do not auto-merge side branches into another checked-out dirty worktree.
- Do not change `ddx work` queue ordering or bead priority policy.
- Do not rewrite existing execute-bead branch history.

INTERSECTIONS
- `ddx-06cbaa90` will eventually add a per-project worktree registry. This bead is narrower: expose/rescue the landing coordinates for today's in-tree mode.
- `ddx-c191e91a` covers stale worktree cleanup. This bead is not cleanup; it is successful-result discoverability and manual rescue guidance.
notes: Additional rescue observation: after creating rescue branch rescue/a54e0299-route-health-235101, the committed file /tmp/fizeau-a54e0299-rescue.235101/.ddx/beads.jsonl contains fizeau-a54e0299 with status=closed and closing_commit_sha=0d70176c, but running ddx bead show fizeau-a54e0299 from that rescue worktree reported status=open because the installed ddx command resolved state from the active/canonical fizeau checkout instead of the command worktree's committed .ddx file. The fix should print the state root/worktree used by try/bead commands, or otherwise make side-worktree state resolution explicit; this reinforces the need for branch/worktree landing coordinates in the terminal result.
title: try: surface side-worktree landings with rescue commands

Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92 and ddx-3c6ca63f.

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:441 sets DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000
  (3 hours). This is the per-attempt absolute wall-clock cap on a
  harness invocation. Two drain wedges observed in the 2026-05-09
  drain consumed the full 3 hours before timing out:

    ddx-c113a116: silent from 16:21 to 19:13:54 (3h0m0.018s),
                  then "do response complete 8472 tok in 3h0m0.018s,
                  0.8 tok/s" → timeout: context deadline exceeded.

    ddx-94a368b6: silent from 19:36 onward (drain killed before
                  natural timeout fired).

  3 hours is too long: a wedged worker burns operator time and budget
  before the timeout fires. 1 hour catches the same wedge class
  before they accumulate substantial cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441
        DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
  - Comment at line 438: "Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout
    — so normal long tasks still complete while genuinely hung workers
    free themselves."
  - The 1.5x ratio against the 2h idle timeout (DefaultTimeoutMS at
    line 434) is preserved by halving both proportionally OR by
    halving wall-clock alone (idle timer protects against true silence
    independently).

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441: change DefaultWallClockMS to
    3600000 (1 hour).
  - Update the comment at line 438 to reflect the 1h cap and explain
    the wedge-detection rationale (citing this bead).
  - DO NOT change DefaultTimeoutMS (idle timer); it remains at 2h,
    which now exceeds wall-clock — but that's fine, idle and
    wall-clock are separate triggers (whichever fires first wins).
    Document this in the comment.
  - Verify nothing in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45-46 or
    cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:158-159 hardcodes the 3h value
    elsewhere; they should pull from DefaultWallClockMS.
  - Add a regression test: TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget
    in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go (or extend an existing
    test) that asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured budget.

NON-SCOPE
  - Per-tool-call timeout (sister bead handles that as a structural
    prevention; this bead is the quick symptom guard).
  - Loop detection via stream-json watcher (separate concern).
  - Removing the underlying source of long Bash calls (sister beads
    ddx-1fda33d2, ddx-150758e7, ddx-f1c476c3 reduce per-push test
    cost; ddx-1f774edf removes per-bead pushes entirely).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent: per-tool-call wall-clock for
    Bash..." — structural prevention; this bead is the immediate
    symptom mitigation.
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf — once landed, drain stops pushing per
    bead, eliminating the 168s test tax that fuels wedges.
notes: Cancelled per user direction 2026-05-09: wall-clock cap is wrong policy. Reducing 3h to 1h would kill legitimately-hard-but-still-active work along with wedges. Right policy: detect 'no real progress' (loops + true idle) rather than capping absolute wall-clock. See sister bead ddx-1c3cad6f (reshaped to cover loop detection + meaningful-event idle reset).
title: fix(agent): reduce DefaultWallClockMS from 3h to 1h (faster wedge detection)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
- TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
- `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
Worker persistence and runtime execution still use a server-local ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, so fields present at cobra or wire boundaries can be lost before spec.json or runWorker consumes them.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec without MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, or SpecVersion. cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-845 builds CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime from that incomplete spec, so runWorker cannot honor those fields on server-spawned workers.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, or make ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec a temporary type alias to executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec if needed for a mechanical migration.
- Persist the fully resolved unified spec to .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json after ApplyDefaults has already run.
- Update lifecycleStartDetail, StartExecuteLoop, buildRoutePreflight, runWorker, and test literals to consume the unified spec.
- Ensure runWorker passes MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev into config/loop/runtime paths, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD used only as the zero-value fallback.
- Keep disk reads tolerant of unknown legacy fields such as min_tier and max_tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change queue picking, route preflight policy, landing behavior, or watchdog behavior.
- Do not implement REST/GraphQL decode changes here except compile fixes.
- Do not change LabelFilter semantics.

acceptance:
1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.

description:

The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a live GraphQL subscription path so DDx can subscribe to Axon changeEvents for bead and bead-event updates. The current test suite in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go covers CRUD, deps, events, and import/export, but there is no subscription smoke test yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:131-179 verifies the split event collection, but nothing in the package opens a WebSocket subscription and asserts that changeEvents are delivered in order. That leaves the live-update path unexercised.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an integration test in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens the subscription endpoint, performs a small bead mutation sequence, and asserts the expected changeEvents arrive.
- Reuse the generated Axon client and subscription transport from the client-scaffolding bead.
- Keep the test isolated and deterministic so it can run in the normal package suite.

NON-SCOPE
- Core backend CRUD logic.
- Config selection.
- Package-wide backend matrix work.

acceptance:
1. Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
2. Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
3. `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
- Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
- `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 4

description:
PROBLEM
The frontend accessibility suite is failing on the dashboard and agent views because the primary accent buttons use contrast combinations that do not meet WCAG AA. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts` reports `color-contrast` violations for `.bg-accent-fulcrum` on light mode and `.bg-accent-fulcrum` plus `.dark\:bg-dark-accent-lever` on dark mode. The visible button styles on the project dashboard and worker detail pages are the source of the failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte:50-72` defines the project dashboard action buttons, including the `bg-accent-lever` and `bg-accent-fulcrum` variants.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte:440-490` renders the live response controls that reuse the same low-contrast accent colors in dark and light modes.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts:55-70` is the failing coverage that catches the contrast regression.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust the button color tokens or local component classes so the dashboard and worker pages meet WCAG AA contrast in both themes without changing the interaction model. Keep the fix localized to the shared accent button styles or the two affected page components.

NON-SCOPE
- Copy or layout changes unrelated to contrast.
- Other page families that are not currently failing axe.
- Backend data or routing changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts` passes for the dashboard and agent pages in both light and dark modes.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/a11y.spec.ts --grep "dashboard|agent"` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: restore dashboard and agent contrast to WCAG AA

description:
# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which adds `BeadReader` and the LifecycleSubscriber ctx-aware signature).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- FEAT-008 spec updated with Run-detail-page subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist.
- FEAT-010 spec updated with extended Run resolver contract + drain-time persistence note + ToolCallEntry definition.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)
- ADR for the in_progress-eligibility bug (separate concern)
- Cross-project skill packaging (deferred per FEAT-015)
- Schema changes to bead JSON (waivers via labels; no new fields)
title: ADR-023 + FEAT-004/010/011 amendments: bead-lifecycle quality enforcement policy

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.

description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 (filed as ddx-XXXX): a sub-skill is needed at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ (and 4 other shipped paths) carrying the rubric, examples, and judge prompts for lint + triage + refine modes. ddx try will compose this sub-skill via the runner library.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md (existing) is the precedent for nested workflow sub-skills under the parent ddx skill. No equivalent exists for bead authoring quality. The bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md is the canonical template (per codex review) but only has 1 strong example + 1 weak example (line 98 of template).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create sub-skill directory bead-lifecycle/ under each of 5 shipped paths (codex amendment): skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/. SKILL.md + supporting files identical across all 5 (or symlinked if the build process supports it; check current bead-breakdown/ packaging).

2. SKILL.md frontmatter: name=bead-lifecycle, description="Score, classify, and refine ddx beads. Used by ddx try hooks pre-dispatch (lint mode) and post-attempt (triage mode); operator-invocable for refine mode."

3. SKILL.md body sections:
   - LINT MODE: input=bead JSON; output=JSON {score 0-8, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied}. References the 8-criterion rubric from bead-authoring-template.md.
   - TRIAGE MODE: input=bead + outcome event + session log excerpt; output=JSON {classification ∈ {already_satisfied, ambiguous, investigated_no_path, decomposed, operator_input_needed, routing, quota, transport, tests_red, merge_conflict, review_block, timeout}, recommended_action ∈ {refine_bead_and_retry, retry_with_more_context, file_children_and_supersede, escalate_to_operator, close_as_already_done, wait_and_retry, give_up}, rationale, suggested_amendments, suggested_followup_beads}.
   - REFINE MODE: input=bead + (optional) prior triage output; output=YAML diff suggesting amendments to bead description + AC.
   - WAIVER TABLE: per-bead-type criterion skips (doc-only skips named-tests + test-name; epic skips concrete-implementation; deletion skips wired-in-assertion). Codex: rubric-first, per-bead label override second.
   - 5-10 CURATED EXAMPLES under bead-lifecycle/examples/: code bug, feature, doc-only, epic, deletion/rename, no-op investigation, upstream/external work. Operator (epic owner ddx-a9d130d0) curates from existing closed beads; LLM does not pick.

4. Mode selection: invocation prompt MUST include "MODE: lint|triage|refine" as first line; SKILL.md instructs the LLM to read mode and apply only that mode's contract.

NON-SCOPE
- LLM judgment baked into Go (this bead is data + markdown only; no code)
- ddx try hook integration (separate child bead)
- Tooling to validate skill output JSON (deferred; LLMs are usually well-behaved with structured-output prompts)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first per FEAT-015)

acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
9. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
10. ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.
11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
13. Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and ddx plugin commands honor both

description:
PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.

description:
PROBLEM
Current deadcode RTA on 2026-05-15 still reports residual preclaim/profile/ratelimit/recovery helpers as unreachable from the production graph, so `ddx-83440482` cannot close from current-tree evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` reports residual dead symbols across `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:368`, `profile_select.go:72`, `ratelimit_retry.go:73`, `recovery_decompose.go:107`, `recovery_reframe.go:40`, `repair_prompt.go:24`, `service_run.go:581`, and `triage_dispatch.go:25`.

PROPOSED FIX
Resolve every symbol currently listed for these files in the committed artifact. WIRE recovery/profile/ratelimit behavior that is required in the live worker path; DELETE orphaned or superseded helpers that no runtime surface still exercises. If any symbol still needs deferred investigation after ~15 minutes, annotate it with `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch candidate-cycle, compare, or execute-bead residuals; those clusters are tracked separately.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.

acceptance:
1. Child bead for root SIGINT/SIGTERM context and user-facing shutdown message exists and is closed.
2. Child bead for interrupted attempt cleanup, unclaim, liveness cleanup, and regression tests exists and is closed.
3. `ddx work` and `ddx try` both print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` on first Ctrl-C/SIGTERM.
4. Interrupted non-terminal beads are open/re-claimable after cleanup and are not parked behind `work-retry-after`.
5. Second-signal hard-abort behavior is documented and tested or explicitly documented as best-effort.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestSignal|TestInterrupt|TestCancel|TestShutdown|TestDisrupted|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators routinely stop long-running `ddx work` / `ddx try` with Ctrl-C. The governing specs now require the first SIGINT/SIGTERM to cancel work cooperatively, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, preserve available evidence, release non-terminal claims, and leave unfinished beads re-claimable. The implementation beads need to land as one coherent interrupt story.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls cmd.Execute, and cli/cmd/root.go:31 calls rootCmd.Execute without a signal-backed context, so terminal SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the process instead of driving cooperative cancellation.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 documents signal as a stop condition, but there is no root signal handler that turns Ctrl-C into that condition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 can classify context cancellation as disrupted, but normal Ctrl-C does not currently reach this path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 now define the intended first-signal behavior and user-facing message.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate two implementation slices: root CLI signal context/user messaging, and work/try attempt cleanup/reclaim tests. When both children close, verify `ddx work` and `ddx try` behave consistently under Ctrl-C.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement resumable worktrees by default. Interrupted work may be preserved as evidence, but the safe default is cleanup and retry from scratch.
- Do not change queue ordering, routing policy, review policy, or cost-cap policy.
- Do not remove legacy work/execute-bead compatibility commands.
notes:
RELATED QUEUE ALIGNMENT
- ddx-9228a484 (StopCondition enum) now depends on this epic so Signal semantics are implemented after first-signal cooperative cancellation is defined.
- ddx-9f20a4bd (external claim liveness) intersects with interrupted heartbeat/liveness cleanup; do not reintroduce beads.jsonl heartbeat writes while implementing the cleanup child.
- ddx-eccc6efb (server-spawn ddx work subprocess) should rely on the same root signal behavior once server workers exec ddx work; keep server-specific SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation as a supervisor layer, not a second queue-drain implementation.
title: work: graceful interrupt shutdown for work and try

description:
PROBLEM
Review-block and post-review escalation still use the old power-hint metadata path. TD-037 requires escalation decisions to be execution policy/evidence, not durable bead difficulty or power metadata.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:633` writes `TriagePowerHintKey` for `ActionEscalatePower`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:9` aliases `TriagePowerHintKey` to the old escalation hint key.
- Review triage tests still assert the old hint is present or cleared.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove review-block and post-review writes to `TriagePowerHintKey`. Keep operator-required cleanup of stale legacy hint keys if needed, but do not create or rely on bead power hints for escalation. Record escalation decisions in events/evidence and let dispatch policy use current attempt state, not bead metadata.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review verdict taxonomy.
- Do not add new durable review escalation fields to bead `Extra`.
- Do not modify Fizeau provider/model routing.
title: review: remove post-review power hint persistence

acceptance:
1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.
2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptCommitsTrackerCleanup proves a cancelled loop context unclaims the bead and commits the resulting .ddx/beads.jsonl mutation, leaving no unstaged tracker diff in a temp git repo.
2. TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotCommitRunState proves the interrupt cleanup commit excludes .ddx/run-state.json and .ddx/run-state/** even when those files are dirty.
3. TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttemptDoesNotRecordTerminalOutcome proves the bead remains open/retryable, is not closed, and no success/already_satisfied/no_changes terminal outcome is recorded for the cancelled attempt.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteLoopInterruptedAttempt" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When a ddx work watcher receives SIGINT/SIGTERM or a cancelled root context during an active attempt, it can release the bead claim and append routing/release metadata but leave .ddx/beads.jsonl modified in the main worktree. Live monitor repro on 2026-05-15: Fizeau watcher cancelled during fizeau-4cf91f64, printed "interrupted attempt released fizeau-4cf91f64 without recording terminal outcome: context canceled", exited, and left .ddx/beads.jsonl dirty until the operator manually committed the single event append.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/root.go:49-55 converts SIGINT/SIGTERM into a cancelled command context. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2064-2085 handles that cancellation by marking the report disrupted, unclaiming the bead, logging the release, and returning ctxErr immediately. That early return bypasses the normal outcome/evidence finalization path below cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2096-2103 and does not stage/commit DDx-managed tracker mutations produced during the attempt, such as routing events appended before cancellation.

PROPOSED FIX
On interrupted-attempt cleanup, keep the existing no-terminal-outcome semantics, but make DDx-managed tracker cleanup durable before returning: unclaim the bead, append a structured interrupted-cleanup event if needed, and route the resulting .ddx/beads.jsonl/attachment mutation through the same bounded auto-commit/index-lock path used for other execute-loop bookkeeping. The main repo should not be left dirty solely because the operator stopped a watcher.

NON-SCOPE
Do not commit .ddx/run-state.json or .ddx/run-state/**; that live-state pollution is owned by ddx-108f145b. Do not close, cooldown, or mark success for interrupted attempts. Do not change OS signal handling or make a cancelled implementation attempt look like model failure. Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.
title: work: auto-commit interrupted attempt cleanup tracker mutations

acceptance:
1. Fixture Markdown files cover positive and negative examples for generic AI-like prose, technical DDx prose, project vocabulary, and at least two prose modes.
2. Expected findings are stored in a machine-readable golden format with file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, and suggested edit fields.
3. A focused test such as `cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/... -run TestFixtures` passes, or if the package does not exist yet, the bead creates the minimal package/test harness needed for fixture validation.
4. The fixtures include at least one negative case proving technical lists/headings/domain terms are not automatically findings.

description:
CONTEXT
The prose checker needs fixture coverage before rule and CLI work so agents can distinguish useful findings from noisy false positives. This bead adds test inputs and expected outputs only; it should not implement checker logic unless a tiny schema helper is already required by existing test conventions.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/docprose/testdata/ or the fixture path selected by TD-036
- cli/internal/docprose/*_test.go only if needed to lock fixture loading/golden comparison conventions
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md only if the selected fixture path needs a minor correction

FIXTURE CASES
- Generic AI-like prose that should be flagged: unsupported robust/comprehensive/seamless claims, vague importance language, filler transitions, repeated openings.
- Technical DDx prose that should not be over-flagged: headings, lists, ADR/spec terminology, precise implementation references.
- Vocabulary behavior: accept DDx, bead, Helix, Fizeau-like project terms when configured; reject known filler when unsupported.
- Mode differences: technical, planning, and public prose.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI command implementation.
- Default skill text.
- Review integration.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx execution paths still call ResolveRoute as a preflight even though Fizeau owns service routing for agent execution. That keeps DDx coupled to route-preview semantics during runtime execution and risks alternate behavior between status/debug surfaces and actual work execution.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:609-653 defines buildRoutePreflight and calls ResolveRoute before executing worker tasks.
- cli/internal/server/workers_route_preflight_test.go asserts this preflight behavior, so tests currently lock in the boundary violation.
- cmd routing lint currently checks command execution paths but does not cover server worker execution in cli/internal/server/workers.go.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove ResolveRoute preflight from worker execution paths. Keep ResolveRoute only on explicit status/debug/preview surfaces such as ddx agent route-status. Add lint/test coverage so non-status execution paths fail if they reintroduce ResolveRoute.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing route-status or provider/model inventory features.
- Changing Fizeau routing APIs.
- Changing final actual-route recording from Fizeau results.
title: agent: remove ResolveRoute preflight from work execution paths

acceptance:
1. A bead whose primary route fails mid-execution with provider_connectivity retries on the next route in the readiness fallback chain at the same power class.
2. If all same-power alternates fail with provider_connectivity, escalate power class.
3. If all power classes exhaust with the same connectivity failure pattern, mark the bead failed.
4. Reproduce: simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus without operator intervention.
description:
When a bead's readiness check passes on a route, then the route's provider goes down mid-execution, ddx work logs 'ladder exhausted at power 5' and unclaims the bead. It does NOT retry on an alternate provider at the same power class even when one is available.

Repro from fizeau bead fizeau-e08d8228 (around 2026-05-16T05:56:28Z):

  readiness: route fiz/bragi-club-3090/qwen3.6-27b-autoround
    -> passed (bragi was reachable for the readiness probe)
  do: route fiz/bragi-club-3090/qwen3.6-27b-autoround power=5
    -> agent: provider error: openai: Post "http://bragi:8020/v1/chat/completions": dial tcp 100.127.38.115:8020: i/o timeout
  kind:execution-escalation-aborted source:ddx work
    summary: 'escalation aborted: ladder exhausted at power 5 provider=bragi-club-3090 model=qwen3.6-27b-autoround'

The bead was marked execution_failed with outcome_reason=provider_connectivity and unclaimed. claude/opus was available the entire time — the very next bead (PR 1c) in the queue ran successfully on claude/opus when its readiness check failed on bragi.

Expected: when a route fails with outcome_reason=provider_connectivity (transient, not a capability issue), retry the bead on the next route in the readiness fallback chain (e.g. claude/opus) before declaring escalation exhausted.

Likely fix: distinguish escalation-exhausted (no stronger route at higher power) from same-power-alternates-exhausted (no alternate provider at same power class). connectivity-class failures should consume the alternates list, not the power ladder.

Impact: flaky local endpoints cause beads to be marked failed even when a healthy fallback is reachable. Forces operator to manually re-queue. With watch=true and a queue of ~9 beads, this added ~5 minutes of wasted re-attempts.

reason=ac-quality:needs-refinement — fix requires design decision and verified repro before code change Investigation summary: The bead's "likely fix" (distinguish escalation-exhausted from same-power-alternates-exhausted) names a real seam, but the implementation choice is non-obvious and the empirical gap is not fully explained by an obvious read of current code. Operator design input is needed before committing routing/escalation changes. Relevant code paths examined in this worktree: 1. cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:299 — runEscalatingPowerAttempts short-circuits on report.Disrupted=true and never retries at the same power. provider_connectivity sets Disrupted=true (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2715-2718), so the in-execution path never attempts the alternate at the same power class. 2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4798 — applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower) which only inspects power classes STRICTLY > actualPower (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:97). When the failed route is at the top power and same-power alternates exist (e.g. claude/opus at the same power class as bragi/qwen3.6-27b-autoround), the ladder emits kind=execution-escalation-aborted at power N even though a viable alternate remains. 3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4733 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions (called on the NEXT claim, cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:449) builds ExcludedRoutes from the failed-route list and re-runs ResolveRoute at the same minPower. In theory this should pick up the alternate on the next iteration; in the user's repro, it did not. Root cause for that gap is not visible from static read of the code (could be Available/AutoRoutable catalog state, worker iteration ordering, or a pausedInfraUntil interaction) and requires reproduction before fixing. 4. AC4 ("simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus") needs integration-level scaffolding that doesn't exist for this code path. The existing tests at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go cover unit-level behavior of applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion but cannot simulate a real mid-execution drop end-to-end. Open design questions for operator: - Should the in-execution executor retry on the alternate at the same power class (modify runEscalatingPowerAttempts to track session-local failed routes and reject Disrupted-as-terminal), OR should it remain "next claim retries via failed_routes exclusion" (and we instead fix whatever blocked the empirical retry)? - Should applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion be made aware of same-power alternates (consult a resolveRoute callback) so it suppresses the misleading escalation-aborted event when alternates remain? - What is the test scaffolding strategy for AC4? Stub fizeau ResolveRoute that returns bragi on first call and claude/opus on second, and exercise the worker loop end-to-end? Or a tighter unit test that asserts the alternate is consulted? Suggested decomposition once design is settled: (a) Repro test: extend route-exclusion tests to assert the failed bead is reclaimed and dispatched on the alternate within the same drain iteration. (b) Implementation: route-exclusion path consults same-power viability before emitting escalation-aborted; OR in-executor retry on Disrupted+connectivity. (c) Integration: end-to-end worker test with stubbed provider drop mid-execution. No code changes attempted in this attempt — committing speculative escalation logic without a verified repro risks regressing existing routing behavior.
