reason: ac-quality:needs-refinement — fix requires design decision and verified repro before code change

Investigation summary:

The bead's "likely fix" (distinguish escalation-exhausted from same-power-alternates-exhausted) names a real seam, but the implementation choice is non-obvious and the empirical gap is not fully explained by an obvious read of current code. Operator design input is needed before committing routing/escalation changes.

Relevant code paths examined in this worktree:

1. cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:299 — runEscalatingPowerAttempts short-circuits on report.Disrupted=true and never retries at the same power. provider_connectivity sets Disrupted=true (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2715-2718), so the in-execution path never attempts the alternate at the same power class.

2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4798 — applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower) which only inspects power classes STRICTLY > actualPower (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:97). When the failed route is at the top power and same-power alternates exist (e.g. claude/opus at the same power class as bragi/qwen3.6-27b-autoround), the ladder emits kind=execution-escalation-aborted at power N even though a viable alternate remains.

3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4733 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions (called on the NEXT claim, cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:449) builds ExcludedRoutes from the failed-route list and re-runs ResolveRoute at the same minPower. In theory this should pick up the alternate on the next iteration; in the user's repro, it did not. Root cause for that gap is not visible from static read of the code (could be Available/AutoRoutable catalog state, worker iteration ordering, or a pausedInfraUntil interaction) and requires reproduction before fixing.

4. AC4 ("simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus") needs integration-level scaffolding that doesn't exist for this code path. The existing tests at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go cover unit-level behavior of applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion but cannot simulate a real mid-execution drop end-to-end.

Open design questions for operator:

- Should the in-execution executor retry on the alternate at the same power class (modify runEscalatingPowerAttempts to track session-local failed routes and reject Disrupted-as-terminal), OR should it remain "next claim retries via failed_routes exclusion" (and we instead fix whatever blocked the empirical retry)?

- Should applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion be made aware of same-power alternates (consult a resolveRoute callback) so it suppresses the misleading escalation-aborted event when alternates remain?

- What is the test scaffolding strategy for AC4? Stub fizeau ResolveRoute that returns bragi on first call and claude/opus on second, and exercise the worker loop end-to-end? Or a tighter unit test that asserts the alternate is consulted?

Suggested decomposition once design is settled:

  (a) Repro test: extend route-exclusion tests to assert the failed bead is reclaimed and dispatched on the alternate within the same drain iteration.
  (b) Implementation: route-exclusion path consults same-power viability before emitting escalation-aborted; OR in-executor retry on Disrupted+connectivity.
  (c) Integration: end-to-end worker test with stubbed provider drop mid-execution.

No code changes attempted in this attempt — committing speculative escalation logic without a verified repro risks regressing existing routing behavior.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-037 now requires exactly one optional bead metadata field, `triage.estimated_difficulty`, with values `easy`, `medium`, and `hard`. The implementation still reads `triage.power_hint` and route-shaped values (`cheap`, `standard`, `smart`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/escalation/powerinfer.go:10` defines `BeadPowerHintKey = "triage.power_hint"`.
- `cli/internal/escalation/powerinfer.go:24` exposes `BeadPowerHint` as the production bead inference helper.
- `cli/internal/escalation/execution_hint.go:88` still treats a smart bead hint as requiring `SMART JUSTIFICATION`.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the bead hint parser with `triage.estimated_difficulty`. Add an estimated-difficulty enum/parser for `easy`, `medium`, and `hard`; map `easy -> cheap`, `medium -> standard`, and `hard -> smart` inside `InferPowerClass`. Delete production `BeadPowerHint`/`TriagePowerHint` inference paths. Keep lint focused on durable concrete route pins only.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/escalation/powerinfer.go`
- `cli/internal/escalation/execution_hint.go`
- `cli/internal/escalation/powerinfer_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change retry escalation behavior in this bead; numeric retry-floor cleanup is a separate TD-037 bead.
- Do not change readiness prompt/schema in this bead; readiness difficulty is a separate TD-037 bead.
- Do not add compatibility reads for `triage.power_hint` or `power:*` labels.

description:
CONTEXT
After the skill, rules, and advisory CLI command exist, DDx needs user-facing documentation that explains Prose Quality Support without overclaiming. The docs must be clear that DDx provides quality guidance and deterministic checks, not AI detection.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/prose-quality.md or the docs path chosen by TD-036
- README.md or docs index only for a concise link
- CHANGELOG.md if project convention requires an entry for user-facing CLI behavior

REQUIRED DOC CONTENT
- What Prose Quality Support does and does not do.
- How to run `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Example finding output.
- Config examples for mode, vocabulary accept/reject, severity, includes/excludes, and advisory/blocking policy if supported.
- Guidance on when to use the human-writing-support skill.
- Explicit statement: not an AI detector and not detector bypass.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- New checker behavior.
- Review integration.
title: Document DDx Prose Quality Support

CostUSD is ALREADY reported by Fizeau on Final events for every call (no upstream change needed — it's been there). acceptance:

2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.

5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

description:
Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract

description:
PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)

description:
PROBLEM
DDx currently has a worker queue and an in-progress claim state, but no first-class operator-attention lane for beads that require human review. When review triage decides needs_human, the tracker can leave beads in in_progress while worker execution skips needs_human labels, making them neither worker-actionable nor clearly operator-actionable.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:39-41 defines ready/status as open/ready/blocked counts only. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:343-352 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:156-170 say review/spec gaps block with needs_human, but they do not define the operator lane, CLI discovery surface, resolution semantics, or claim transition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1060-1090 counts a failed pre-close review but does not unclaim the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Create a spec-first implementation queue that defines and implements needs_human as a first-class operator-attention lane: open + needs_human + structured reason metadata, excluded from ddx work, discoverable by agents/TUIs via ddx bead needs-human, and resolvable through explicit supported actions.

NON-SCOPE
- Full interactive artifact review chat UI.
- Changing the bd/br canonical status enum.
- Removing existing retry/escalate review-block behavior for fixable gaps.

CHILD QUEUE
- Spec update bead defines state model and CLI contract.
- Store/count helpers support the lane.
- CLI list/show/status/ready surfaces make it agent-friendly.
- Resolve command performs explicit retry/split/obsolete/defer actions.
- Execute loop transitions terminal needs_human review blocks to open+needs_human.
- Reconcile repairs historical stale rows.
title: operator lane: define needs-human review workflow

acceptance:
1. TestDDxRoot_ConventionModeXDGPath asserts that for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`, ddxroot.Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx/.
2. TestDDxRoot_ConventionModeNoRemoteFallsBackToLocal asserts the no-remote fallback path matches `local/<basename>-<sha[:12]>/`.
3. TestDDxRoot_URLParsingCanonicalForms covers https, ssh, git@, custom-host URL inputs.
4. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapInitsGitRepoInXDG asserts that on first invocation, the XDG project dir is created, `git init` runs there, and a HEAD commit exists.
5. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapIdempotent asserts that two concurrent calls to ddxroot.Path on the same project do not race-create the directory (atomicity test via temp dir + sync.WaitGroup).
6. TestWorktreeRegistry_RegistersOnFirstAccess asserts worktrees.json is created and lists the calling worktree on first ddxroot.Path call.
7. TestWorktreeRegistry_AutoMarksFirstAsMaster asserts the master field equals the first registered path.
8. TestWorktreeRegistry_LateRegistrationDoesNotChangeMaster asserts subsequent worktrees register without overwriting master.
9. `ddx project worktree list` and `ddx project worktree master <path>` commands exist with appropriate help text.
10. End-to-end test: run `ddx bead list` in a clone of this repo with .ddx/ temporarily removed (or in a fresh repo), assert (a) project tree unchanged afterwards, (b) XDG project dir created at the expected path, (c) bead list returns convention defaults.

description:
PROBLEM
With the DDxRoot resolver from ddx-564ea1cf in place, the next step is to actually use an XDG path when no in-tree .ddx/ exists. Convention mode: the project's worktree stays untouched; all DDx state lives in ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/.

ROOT CAUSE
ddxroot.Path today returns filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx") unconditionally. It needs to fall back when the in-tree path does not exist.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Extend ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot):
   - If <projectRoot>/.ddx is a directory → return <projectRoot>/.ddx (in-tree mode, unchanged).
   - Else → resolve XDG path from `git -C <projectRoot> remote get-url origin`. Parse the URL into host/owner/repo (handle SSH, HTTPS, custom-host forms). Return ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/.
   - If no remote, fall back to local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>.
   - First call on a project that has no XDG dir yet: create the directory, run `git init` in it (with main as default branch), commit an empty .gitkeep or seed config. This makes the XDG state itself version-controlled and pushable.

2. Worktree registry:
   - Each XDG project root maintains a worktrees.json registering every absolute worktree path that has accessed it. Schema: {paths: [{path, first_seen_at, last_seen_at, hostname}], master: <path or null>}.
   - The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master. Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
   - `ddx project worktree list/master <path>` operator commands to inspect or pin the master.
   - Audit logs and bead show output cite the master path when reporting commit SHAs from project space.

3. Bootstrap idempotency: any ddx command may trigger XDG bootstrap. The bootstrap MUST be atomic and idempotent. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create two XDG dirs.

4. URL parsing:
   - https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
   - git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
   - https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 cancel/cooldown methods (`RequestCancel`, `IsCancelRequested`, `MarkCancelHonored`, `SetExecutionCooldown`, `ClearCooldowns`, `IncrNoChangesCount`). Cancel signals cooperative shutdown; cooldown parks beads from re-execution after failures. Per TD-027 §11.5 + §14 these belong in `ops/cancel/` and `ops/cooldown/` helpers + typed `Operation` values. Callers are in `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and the operator CLI.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. The drain loop's cooldown policy (TD-031 §2 outcome mapping) writes via `SetExecutionCooldown`; the escalation policy in `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go` reads + manipulates cooldown state. Cancel signals are written by operators via the CLI and read by the worker's mid-attempt check.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §11.5 + §14 for cancel + cooldown:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `SetCancelRequested{At time.Time}` — sets `cancel.requested=true` + `cancel.requested_at`.
   - `ClearCancelRequested{}` — clears both fields.
   - `MarkCancelHonoredOp{At time.Time}` — sets `cancel.honored=true` + `cancel.honored_at`.
   - `SetCooldown{Until time.Time; Status, Detail, BaseRev string}`.
   - `ClearCooldownOp{}` — clears cooldown fields.
   - `IncrNoChangesCount{}` — atomic increment via `Apply`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/cancel/cancel.go`:
   - `Request(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `SetCancelRequested{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `ClearCancelRequested{}`.
   - `MarkHonored(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `MarkCancelHonoredOp{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `IsRequested(b *Bead) bool` — pure predicate.
3. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/cooldown/cooldown.go`:
   - `Set(ctx, l, id, opts SetOptions) error` — wraps `SetCooldown`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `ClearCooldownOp{}`.
   - `ClearAll(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, q BeadQueries, filter func(*Bead) bool) (int, error)` — iterates with `Apply`.
   - `IncrNoChanges(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `IncrNoChangesCount{}`.
   - `IsActive(b *Bead, now time.Time) bool` — pure predicate (per TD-027 §11.2 invariant 6: `cooldown.until` in past → no-op).
4. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — cancel checks + cooldown setting.
   - `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go` — cooldown set/clear.
   - `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go` — operator clear-all.
   - Any test files asserting cancel/cooldown workflow behavior → use helpers.
5. Remove `*Store.RequestCancel`, `IsCancelRequested`, `MarkCancelHonored`, `SetExecutionCooldown`, `ClearCooldowns`, `IncrNoChangesCount` (6 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Cooldown retry-window policy (15-minute ceiling, which outcomes may set retry-after) — TD-031 §2.1 owns that; storage exposes the field.
- Cancellation acknowledgment semantics — workflow concern; helper provides the field-level mutation.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, claim, etc.) — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 cancel/cooldown Operation types. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Tests: `TestOperation_SetCancelRequested_SetsFields`, `TestOperation_MarkCancelHonored_RequiresPriorRequest`, `TestOperation_IncrNoChangesCount_Increments`, `TestOperation_SetCooldown_PersistsAllFields`.
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/cancel/` and `cli/internal/bead/ops/cooldown/` packages exist with helpers + tests: `TestOpsCancel_Request_RoundTrip`, `TestOpsCancel_IsRequested_Predicate`, `TestOpsCooldown_Set_PersistsAllFields`, `TestOpsCooldown_ClearAll_FiltersCorrectly`, `TestOpsCooldown_IsActive_RespectsPastUntil`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 6 cancel/cooldown methods.
4. Caller files no longer reference these on `*Store` (verified by grep).
5. Escalation flow continues to set/clear cooldowns correctly. Test: `TestEscalation_NoChangesUnverified_SetsCooldown_ViaHelper`.
6. TD-027 §11.2 invariant 6 verified: `cooldown.until` in past → queue derivation ignores cooldown. Test: `TestCooldown_PastUntil_QueueIgnoresIt`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:execution, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx worker and live-output paths still derive inner agent progress from Fizeau-adjacent session logs or harness-native records. After SD-011, canonical progress belongs to Fizeau ServiceEvents; DDx should consume those fields for display and keep DDx-owned worker lifecycle events separate.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:83-85 formats Fizeau-style session JSONL records into DDx transcript/progress lines.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1655-1704 reads latest agent-*.jsonl logs and calls agent.FormatSessionLogLines for live worker output.
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:451-456 still writes Fizeau-style progress/session records from a direct parser.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:243-355 drains service events and reconstructs progress/tool-call entries rather than treating canonical progress as the source of truth.

PROPOSED FIX
Use Fizeau canonical progress ServiceEvent payloads for DDx worker/live output. DDx may format DDx-owned worker lifecycle events, but inner agent action/target/timing/output excerpts must come from canonical Fizeau progress fields when present. Historical fallback handling must be explicit and not used when canonical events exist.

NON-SCOPE

- Parsing Claude stream-json directly.
- Fizeau schema changes.

acceptance: 1. Execute-bead prompt requires verification_command OR explicit needs-investigation marker in any no_changes_rationale.txt. 2. Loop verifies the verification_command before closing as already_satisfied. 3. Implementation aligns exactly with TD-031 NoChangesContract (per ddx-673833f4) — uses LABELS not new statuses (e.g., 'triage:needs-investigation', 'triage:no-changes-unverified', 'triage:no-changes-unjustified'). **bd/br round-trip via ddx bead export must produce a bd-importable record after this bead lands.** 4. New events use canonical names from TD: no_changes_unverified, no_changes_unjustified, no_changes_verified. 5. Tests cover all paths: verified→close, unverified→stay-open+label, unjustified→stay-open+label, needs-investigation→stay-open+label. 6. Existing beads currently in 'closed via no_changes' state are not retroactively reopened. 7. Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the triage:* labels per TriageContract. 8. **Verify schema_compat_test.go passes.**
description:
Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.
notes:

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestDDxRoot_BootstrapInitsGitRepoInXDG asserts the XDG dir is created with `git init` on first bootstrap and a HEAD commit exists.
- TestDDxRoot_BootstrapIdempotent asserts two concurrent calls do not race-create.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_FirstWorktreeIsMaster asserts auto-master on first access.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 3

description:

Bead review should be able to use normalized Vale-backed prose findings without confusing prose advice with correctness review.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:264-283 requires prose findings and correctness review to stay separate. The plan also lists `ddx bead review --prose` reuse after the command engine lands at lines 356-359.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Review integration is policy-sensitive. A weak implementation could turn advisory prose checks into blocking correctness gates or mix style findings into acceptance-criterion verdicts.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add or update the `ddx bead review --prose` path to reuse normalized prose findings.
- Attach prose evidence separately from acceptance/correctness review evidence.
- Keep prose findings advisory by default.

NON-SCOPE

- Do not run prose checks for non-doc changes.
- Do not alter reviewer model routing.

DEPS
Depends on the Vale-backed public `ddx doc prose` command.

description:
PROBLEM
During the current DDx monitor on 2026-05-15, ddx-b6859802 landed successfully, then the next watch iteration immediately reported: preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260515T221109-774cab8b; released ddx-bb8e7d07 and continuing watch. dirty paths: cli/cmd/run_test_helpers.go. A primary-checkout git status immediately afterward was clean, and the retry of ddx-bb8e7d07 proceeded. The reported path was the rename/delete path from the just-landed bead, not operator WIP. This creates exactly the dirty-file complaint loop operators do not want: DDx preserves and reports its own transient post-land state even though there is nothing for the operator to commit or clean.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:738-759 treats any non-allowed pre-dispatch dirty path as implementation dirt. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1994-2008 then preserves those paths under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch and continues watch mode. That path does not distinguish stable operator WIP from transient just-landed files still visible while the previous attempt finalization/checkpoint commits are settling. The result is a false dirty-tree preservation event between two worker claims.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a regression that simulates a successful landed attempt with a rename/delete path and immediately starts the next watch iteration; the next bead must not be released because of the just-landed path.
- Make pre-dispatch dirty handling require a stable dirty snapshot before preserving implementation paths in watch mode, or serialize next-claim pre-dispatch after the prior attempt finalization under the same project git lock so just-landed files cannot appear as operator WIP.
- Keep the real operator-WIP behavior from ddx-ff7a7207: if an actual implementation file remains dirty after the stability check, stop or preserve according to the existing operator-attention policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not auto-commit arbitrary operator implementation files.
- Do not remove the pre-dispatch dirty-tree guard.
- Do not change execute-bead merge policy or rewrite historical attempt commits.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: work: suppress false pre-dispatch dirty preservation after land

description:
PROBLEM
The execute-bead loop body (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036) has 18 store-error exit paths — every bead outcome write can hard-exit the drain loop. This violates the loop's contract: a single bead's store failure should never kill the entire drain worker.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 contains ~14 direct error returns triggered by store write failures, all routing through handleOutcomeStoreError. Each returns a hard error to Drain instead of scheduling a cooldown and continuing. The helper handleOutcomeStoreError itself is also a terminal-exit path. The 18 exit paths (14 store-error sites + handleOutcomeStoreError variants) are the dominant smell: 18 of 24 returns in the loop body are store-error paths.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go defining commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID, op func() error).
- commitOutcome calls op(); on error, calls SetExecutionCooldown with 'loop-error' ParkReason and returns nil (never hard-errors to Drain).
- Delete handleOutcomeStoreError.
- Replace all 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor coordinates: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) with commitOutcome.

NON-SCOPE
- Logic changes to what's stored (only how store errors are handled).
- execute_bead_loop.go file rename (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: store-write failure must not terminate the whole drain loop.
- P3: loop-error fallback should be observable as a structured degradation event.
- P4: a write failure on one bead must not affect later beads.
title: C6: single commitOutcome helper deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError

description:
PROBLEM
The exact `.ddx` audit still matches the CLI command layer and config/git helpers, so convention-root projects keep bypassing `ddxroot.Path` in the entrypoints that create stores, read config, and resolve workspaces.

ROOT CAUSE
Representative call sites still hardcode `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)` in `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:154`, `cli/cmd/config.go:246`, `cli/cmd/try.go:150`, `cli/internal/config/loader.go:69`, and `cli/internal/git/git.go:107`. Those joins assume an in-tree `.ddx/` and prevent the command/config layer from following the new presence-based root resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add shared helpers in `cli/cmd/` and `cli/internal/config/` that route project-state lookups through `ddxroot.Path` instead of direct `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)` calls.
- Replace the remaining direct `.ddx` joins in `cli/cmd/*.go`, `cli/internal/config/*.go`, and `cli/internal/git/git.go`.
- Add focused tests proving command/config lookup uses the resolved DDx root for convention-mode projects.

NON-SCOPE
- `cli/internal/agent`, `cli/internal/exec`, and server/graphql packages.
- Test-only files and the final full-tree hook gate.

DEPS
- No deps.

description:
CONTEXT
This bead creates the technical design for the Prose Quality Support pipeline authorized by FEAT-027. The design should decide the implementation boundary for deterministic prose checks, including whether DDx shells out to Vale, embeds a small checker, or wraps a pluggable runner. The design must keep the first executable surface advisory and must define behavior when optional tooling is missing.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md only for depends_on/status updates if the local convention requires it

REQUIRED DESIGN CONTENT
- Checker boundary: Vale vs embedded checker vs wrapper, with install/runtime behavior and missing-tool behavior.
- Rule/vocabulary asset layout in the default DDx plugin.
- Config schema sketch for mode, severity, vocabulary accept/reject, includes/excludes, and advisory/blocking policy.
- CLI shape for ddx doc prose --changed and future ddx doc prose <paths>.
- Finding schema: file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit.
- Fixture/golden test approach.
- Sequencing: skill/rules/doc command first; bead review integration later.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Implementing CLI code.
- Adding rule files.
- Wiring bead review.
title: Design TD-036 prose quality pipeline

description:
PROBLEM
`cd cli && go test ./cmd -run '^TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue$' -count=1` currently fails because plain `ddx work --json` waits longer than 5 seconds on an empty queue. Default drain mode must scan once, report `no_ready_work`, and exit quickly; only explicit `--watch` should sleep on the worker idle interval.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go:534` captures the regression: `ddx work --json` in default drain mode should not wait for the long-running worker poll interval.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1024` delegates empty-queue termination to `work.StopCondition`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1061` still reaches `sleepOrWake(ctx, idleInterval, runtime.WakeCh)` after `no_ready_work` when default drain is not classified as terminal.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the default `ddx work` mode a true drain-and-exit mode: after one empty ready-queue scan, return the JSON `no_ready_work` result without sleeping. Preserve explicit `--watch` behavior, where an empty queue is idle and the worker sleeps until the next scan or wake signal. Keep `--once` semantics unchanged.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change ready-queue ordering or claim policy.
- Do not change `ddx work --watch` idle interval behavior.
- Do not weaken the elapsed-time assertion by merely raising the test threshold.
title: work: make default drain exit immediately on empty queue

3. `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` explain that current-bead lifecycle transitions are orchestrator-owned during `ddx work` / `ddx try` attempts.

description:
PROBLEM
During live Fizeau work on 2026-05-15, the execute-bead prompt let the worker run `ddx bead update fizeau-ae932179 --claim` and then `ddx bead update fizeau-ae932179 --status in_progress` from inside the isolated attempt. The claim failed, but the status update succeeded and dirtied the primary project tracker mid-attempt. Worker attempts must not mutate lifecycle state for the bead they are currently executing; the orchestrator owns claim/status/unclaim/close.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2135-2143` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2166-2179` render worker instructions that describe how to work and how to decompose, but they do not explicitly forbid current-bead lifecycle mutations. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go:82-84` currently asserts decomposition commands (`ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, `ddx bead update`) are present, which accidentally protects a broad `ddx bead update` allowance without a current-bead lifecycle carve-out. `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:119-125` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:119-125` document that non-closing outcomes should be unclaimed, but they do not distinguish orchestrator-owned lifecycle operations from worker-safe child-bead decomposition notes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the execute-bead instruction text in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` so both Claude-style and Agent-style prompt variants state that the worker must not run `ddx bead update --claim`, `--status`, `--unclaim`, or `ddx bead close` on the bead currently being executed.
- Preserve the existing split-first path: workers may still create child beads, add dependencies, and add parent decomposition notes when Step 0 says the bead is too large.
- Add guardrail tests in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go` that render both prompt variants and assert the current-bead lifecycle prohibition is present alongside the safe decomposition allowance.
- Update `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md` with the same orchestrator-owned lifecycle boundary.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove Step 0 decomposition support.
- Do not change the bead CLI permission model or make `ddx bead update` globally unavailable inside workers.
- Do not add harness/provider/model pins or numeric power hints.

PARENT / DEPS
No parent. No deps.
title: execute-bead: forbid current-bead lifecycle mutations in worker prompts

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 12 query methods (`List`, `ListWithArchive`, `Ready`, `ReadyExecution`, `ReadyExecutionBreakdown`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `NeedsHuman`, `Blocked`, `ExternalBlocked`, `DependencyWaiting`, `BlockedAll`, `Status`) that compute derived views over the bead corpus. Per TD-027 §6.1 these compose into `BeadQueries` interface; per AC #1 every interface method takes `ctx context.Context`. Several callers (`cli/cmd/work_focus.go`, `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go`) are clean ISP narrowing candidates — they use queries only.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` (and related files for derivation logic) defines these 12 methods without ctx. The Foundation bead declared `BeadQueries` interface (ctx-aware) but the `*Store` methods don't yet match.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update all 12 query methods on `*Store` to take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter.
2. Add compile-time assertion: `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through every caller of these methods. Many callers can also narrow their `*bead.Store` field type to `bead.BeadQueries` (ISP win) — opportunistic narrowing in this bead; full narrowing pass in BL.
4. No method removal — these methods stay on `*Store`; they ARE the storage primitives for derived views (no Operation pattern needed; pure reads).

# NON-SCOPE
- Queue derivation logic (TD-027 §2.1) — not modified.
- Pagination / cursor support for List — deferred (TD-027 §25 open question).
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. All 12 query methods on `*Store` take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ BeadQueries = (*Store)(nil)` compiles.
2. Caller files updated to pass ctx. Test: `TestBeadQueries_*_AcceptsContext` (parameterized over each method).
3. At least 3 caller files narrowed from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadQueries` field type. Concrete:
   - `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` (uses `BlockedAll`, `List`, `ProposedOperatorAttention`, `ReadyExecution` — pure queries).
   - `cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go` (uses `ReadyExecution`).
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go` (uses `DependencyWaiting`, `ExternalBlocked`, `Ready`, `Status` — pure read-side resolver).
   - `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` (uses `ReadAll` — strong reader narrowing candidate; BeadReader, not Queries, but related).
4. Existing query tests in `cli/internal/bead/` continue to pass.
5. Ctx cancellation propagates: if ctx is cancelled mid-query, the method returns `ctx.Err()`. Test: `TestBeadQueries_RespectsContextCancellation`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:queries, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (Foundation — BeadQueries interface declared).
title: Q: BeadQueries — add ctx to 12 query methods on *Store; opportunistic caller narrowing (TD-027 §6.1)

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 lifecycle-transition methods (`TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed`) that encode HELIX-flavored state-machine policy directly in the storage layer. Callers in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/` hold `*Store` concretely and invoke these methods. Per TD-027's storage-vs-workflow separation, the state-machine validation belongs in a workflow helper package, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, op Operation)` plus typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes lifecycle methods that mutate status with HELIX-specific transition validation embedded. `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, and `cli/cmd/bead.go` call these methods directly. The new `Operation` pattern (added in Foundation bead `ddx-c6317784`) provides the primitive but the workflow helpers don't exist yet.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §14 helper package `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/`:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`: `SetStatus{Status}`, `SetLifecycleStatus{Status, Options}`, `SetCloseEvidence{SessionID, CommitSHA}`, `ReopenOp{Reason, Notes}`, `ParkToProposedOp{Reason}`, `AppendNotes{Notes}`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/transition.go` with helpers:
   - `Transition(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, r BeadReader, id, newStatus string) error` — validates against TD-027 §2 transition matrix; calls `l.Apply(ctx, id, SetLifecycleStatus{Status: newStatus})`.
   - `CloseWithEvidence(ctx, l, id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error`.
   - `Reopen(ctx, l, id, reason, notes string) error`.
   - `ParkToProposed(ctx, l, id, reason string) error`.
   - `AppendNotes(ctx, l, id, notes string) error`.
   - `SetStatus(ctx, l, id, status string) error` — for callers that don't need state-machine validation.
3. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` — `store.Reopen(...)`, `store.CloseWithEvidence(...)`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(...)` → `lifecycle.Reopen(ctx, store, ...)` etc.
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go` — `store.TransitionLifecycle`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `store.Reopen` → helper calls.
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — lifecycle transition usages → helpers.
   - All test files that use these methods (where they assert workflow behavior) → helpers; tests that assert storage primitive behavior stay using `Apply` directly.
4. Remove `*Store.TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Other concerns (claim, cancel, cooldown, queries, dependency, archive) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Changes to the state machine itself (statuses, transitions) — defined in TD-027 §1–§2; not modified here.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 lifecycle Operation types listed in PROPOSED FIX. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Test: `TestOperation_LifecycleOps_AllImplementApply` (reflection-enumerated).
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/` package exists with the helper functions. Each helper has at least one test against `*Store`: `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_HappyPath`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_RejectsInvalidTransition`, `TestOpsLifecycle_CloseWithEvidence_RecordsProvenance`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Reopen_FromClosedFails` (closed is terminal), `TestOpsLifecycle_ParkToProposed_AppendsTriageEvent`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 lifecycle methods. Compile-time check: `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (TransitionLifecycle|SetLifecycleStatus|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus|CloseWithEvidence|AppendNotes|Reopen|ParkToProposed)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returns nothing.
4. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store`. Verified by `grep -rln 'store\.\(TransitionLifecycle\|SetLifecycleStatus\|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus\|CloseWithEvidence\|AppendNotes\|Reopen\|ParkToProposed\)' cli/` outside the bead package and test fixtures, which returns zero matches.
5. Existing test behavior preserved: `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go`, `chaos_test.go`, `backend_conformance_test.go` continue to pass (they test storage primitives, not the workflow helpers).
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (Foundation bead — depends on Operation pattern + BeadLifecycle interface + *Store.Apply existing).

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work plan` currently surfaces `ddx-f088a3fd` as a completed epic closure candidate, but the no-agent/routing-boundary cleanup still needs a final closure audit. The parent AC requires the `ddx agent` / `cli/internal/agent` / `harness=agent` search to return only audited historical or literal external-agent hits, and stale related beads still encode plans that conflict with the remove-not-deprecate decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/work_plan.go:149-151` prints epic closure candidates when all direct children are closed; that is a queue visibility signal, not proof the epic AC is satisfied.
- `cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go:295-319` documents the same structural rule.
- `ddx bead show ddx-387a0178` records `needs-human-reason`: the bead is superseded by `ddx-f088a3fd` / `ddx-b194d20f`, preserves deprecated `ddx agent` aliases in AC #7, and targets `cli/internal/agent/try`, so it must not dispatch as written.
- `ddx bead show ddx-3ee4ddcf` still describes a compatibility-alias cleanup and depends on `ddx-387a0178`, so the surrounding dependency graph can keep stale no-agent work alive even after the direct children of `ddx-f088a3fd` closed.
- A current audit command, `rg -n 'ddx agent|cli/internal/agent|Harness: "agent"|harness=agent' cli docs .agents/skills .claude/skills`, still returns hits that must be classified before the epic can close.

PROPOSED FIX
Run a final no-agent/routing-boundary closure audit as a normal child bead. Write `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no-agent-boundary-audit.md` classifying every remaining hit as live violation, retained compatibility/durable key, historical doc, test fixture, or literal external-agent reference. Use `ddx bead update`, `ddx bead dep remove`, and `ddx bead create` to make stale conflicting beads non-dispatchable or to file focused residual cleanup work. Keep DDx from adding routing defaults or model matching; Fizeau owns that.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement Fizeau model/provider/harness routing in DDx.
- Do not add compatibility aliases for `ddx agent`.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; all tracker mutations must use `ddx bead` commands.

description:
PROBLEM
No chaos or integration test suite validates federation behavior under failure conditions. Unit tests cover individual components but no test exercises multi-node failure scenarios (spoke crash, hub crash, network partition, version skew, duplicate node_id). Shipping federation without chaos coverage risks silent data loss or incorrect routing under common failure modes.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/: unit tests cover fanout and registry separately but no test spins up multiple in-process httptest.Server instances to simulate a real federation topology.
- The test patterns from ddx-13e19750 / ddx-e59bff83 (closed tests) established the two-node httptest setup; this bead extends to chaos scenarios.
- ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first as the chaos tests exercise the full resolver + routing stack.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/federation/chaos_test.go (or federation_integration_test.go):
  - Test fixture: spin up N in-process httptest.Server instances (hub + N spokes).
  - Chaos scenarios (each as a t.Run):
    1. Spoke crash: spoke goes offline mid-operation; hub fan-out returns partial results.
    2. Hub crash: spoke loses hub connection; spoke enters degraded mode.
    3. Network partition: simulated by closing httptest.Server; requests timeout gracefully.
    4. Version skew: spoke reports different SpecVersion; hub logs warning and continues.
    5. Duplicate node_id: second spoke with same node_id is rejected at registration.

NON-SCOPE
- Load testing or performance benchmarks.
- External network; httptest only.
title: federation: backend chaos/integration suite

2. The test uses deterministic latency (channels or synctest), not wall-clock sleeps.
3. The test uses a per-test sandbox via testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir().
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  After the sister implementation bead removes network I/O from Land(),
  Land() is local-only: worktree-merge + update-ref + evidence commit
  under a brief local lock, with no fetch or push. A worker that crashes
  mid-Land (between UpdateRefTo and the next bead's claim) can leave the
  tracker lock held by a dead process. The next worker that starts must
  break the stale lock and recover cleanly. There is no test for this
  local-only recovery path today.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:196-218 breakStaleTrackerLock removes
    the lock if the owner PID is dead.
  - No test exercises the scenario where the dead owner had completed
    UpdateRefTo (advancing local main) but did not have a chance to
    perform any follow-up tracker writes before exiting.
  - With network I/O removed from Land(), the only stale-lock-recovery
    scenario worth covering is the local-only one: worker died after
    advancing local ref; next worker breaks lock, sees local-ahead state,
    proceeds.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add one chaos test in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (or a new
  file cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go):

  TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand:
    - Simulate a worker that acquired withMainGitLock, called
      UpdateRefTo successfully (local main advanced), then was killed
      without releasing the lock.
    - Next worker arrives, calls breakStaleTrackerLock at
      tracker_lock.go:196-218, observes the lock is held by a dead PID,
      breaks it.
    - Next worker proceeds with its own Land() and asserts it sees the
      local-ahead state (does not re-merge the previous worker's work,
      does not roll back).

  Use a per-test sandbox (testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir()) and
  channel-based barriers / synctest for deterministic timing — no
  wall-clock sleeps in CI.

NON-SCOPE
  - The sister implementation bead handles the network-I/O removal.
  - Push-race or fetch-race scenarios are obsolete after the sister
    implementation bead lands; they no longer exist in single-node mode.
  - Multi-machine coordination chaos coverage is a future federation
    concern.

acceptance:
1. TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.
2. TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.
3. TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
4. TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
5. `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.
6. `ddx init` (or the equivalent template-update path) writes .gitignore entries for `.ddx/attachments/`, `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/`, `.ddx/workers/`.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1 passes.
8. CHANGELOG.md notes the reaper + retention behavior + env knobs.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy: `.ddx/executions/` entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS (default 14) are archived to `.ddx/executions-archive/<year>/` or deleted. Run on `ddx work` startup or via `ddx doctor` subcommand.
4. Audit .gitignore guidance for ddx-init: ensure `.ddx/attachments/`, `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/`, `.ddx/workers/` are recommended ignored (they're ephemeral per-machine state, not source-of-truth).
5. Add a `ddx doctor cleanup` (or extend existing `ddx doctor`) that prints what would be reaped and offers a `--apply` flag for one-shot cleanup.
6. Make `ddx try` / `ddx work` defer worktree cleanup to a process-exit handler that survives Go panics and signal handlers (currently the cleanup only runs on the happy path).

NON-SCOPE
- Don't change worktree-creation paths or naming.
- Don't change beads.jsonl/beads-archive.jsonl retention (separate concern).
- Don't change CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence-pile semantics — execution evidence is still valuable, just on a retention policy.
- Don't depend on a cron/daemon — keep the reaper synchronous in ddx work/try startup.

DEPS
None. This is purely housekeeping in the ddx-cli surface.

description:
PROBLEM
The specs define interactive-steward as the default queue-steward conversation workflow, but the shipped ddx skill still routes broad prompts directly to status/work/review/beads references, the existing status/work references still steer interactive users toward worker-ready work, and the route fixture/eval surface still only understands the legacy expected_reference/expected_cli shape. A green embedded-copy sync can also leave .agents, .claude, and .ddx project skill copies stale.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:136-179 requires reference/interactive.md, route precedence, phase output contracts, consent-gated adversarial review, negative fixtures, and a richer routing fixture schema consumed by the eval driver. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:228-238 states that make copy-skills syncs only skills/ddx to cli/internal/skills/ddx and that project-local copies also need verification. skills/ddx/SKILL.md:102-109 routes queue/work/review prompts directly to existing references and has no interactive-steward first stop. skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" to ddx bead ready, which exposes worker-ready work rather than the human intervention/planning loop. scripts/eval-skill.sh:115-128 reads only expected_reference and expected_cli, so it cannot validate mode, mutation permissions, queue commands, or next action.

PROPOSED FIX
Add the interactive-steward skill reference and deterministic route fixtures, and fold the older broad-prompt queue-steward reference cleanup into this bead. Update the source ddx skill frontmatter/router to send broad conversational DDx prompts to reference/interactive.md before delegating to status.md, review.md, beads.md, or work.md. Update status/work/review references so broad interactive prompts inspect focus/status and plan rather than manually implementing ready beads. Update routing.jsonl and scripts/eval-skill.sh so the richer fixture schema is validated and consumed. Sync and verify all shipped skill copies.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md
- skills/ddx/reference/interactive.md
- skills/ddx/reference/status.md
- skills/ddx/reference/work.md
- skills/ddx/reference/review.md
- skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl
- scripts/eval-skill.sh
- cli/cmd/skill_interactive_steward_test.go (create if absent; colocate with existing skill/routing tests if a better local test file exists)
- cli/internal/skills/ddx/**, .agents/skills/ddx/**, .claude/skills/ddx/**, .ddx/skills/ddx/** as generated/copied skill outputs

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Implementing ddx work focus.
- Changing ddx work/try execution behavior.
- Updating AGENTS.md generation; that is a separate child bead.
- Editing tracker storage by hand.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. Dependencies: ddx-377ebf92. Absorbs the implementation scope formerly tracked by ddx-847df529.
title: ddx skill: add interactive-steward routing and fixtures

description:
# PROBLEM
The conformance suite (`ddx-958b8fc3`) parameterizes tests across `*Store` and `AxonStore`, but `AxonStore` is tested against an httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint — a mock. Production AxonStore deployment requires verifying behavior against an actual Postgres-backed Axon service. Without real-wire tests, semantic differences (timestamp precision, transaction isolation, NULL handling, etc.) can silently diverge between the mock and prod.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/axon/` and the conformance suite use mocked transports. No CI job runs AxonStore against real Postgres. Per `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`'s audit, "Zero tests run against Postgres" was a gap. The current state remains gap-ful for production verification.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a real-wire integration test package under `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/`. Each test:
   - Provisions a Postgres instance (via Docker testcontainer, `dockertest`, or similar in-CI Postgres).
   - Starts an Axon service against that Postgres (or uses Axon's standalone test mode).
   - Constructs `AxonStore` via the real GraphQL transport pointing at the Axon service.
   - Runs the existing conformance suite + Axon-specific tests against the real backend.

2. Test cases (in addition to the parameterized conformance):
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_ClaimCAS_AtomicUnderConcurrency` — 10 concurrent claims; assert exactly one succeeds, others get `ErrAlreadyClaimed`.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_HeartbeatNativeUpdate` — assert `SetClaimHeartbeat` produces a single `UPDATE` SQL statement (no full-row read-modify-write); verify via Postgres query logs.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_EventAppend_Monotonic` — concurrent event appends preserve TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8 (monotonic per bead).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TransactionIsolation` — `Apply` operations are atomic under SERIALIZABLE / READ COMMITTED (whatever Lakebase default is).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_NullPreservation` — `omitempty` JSONL fields map correctly to Postgres NULL/JSONB representation.
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_TimestampPrecision` — RFC3339Nano roundtrips with Postgres `TIMESTAMPTZ` (nanosecond truncation behavior documented).
   - `TestAxonStore_RealPostgres_SchemaVersionDefault` — beads with `schema_version` absent default to v1 on read (TD-027 §11.4).

3. CI job: `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` runs the real-wire tests on a Postgres service container. Gated to manual trigger initially (Postgres setup is slow); converted to nightly once stable. Not on every PR.

4. Documentation: `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/README.md` explains how to run locally (`docker-compose up postgres-test` + `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Production Lakebase setup — separate ops/infra concern.
- Performance benchmarks — separate work; this bead is correctness.
- Migrating the existing conformance suite to use real Postgres — would slow every PR. The httptest-served suite stays as the fast inner-loop test; real-wire is a gated outer-loop verification.

# Acceptance criteria

1. New package `cli/internal/bead/integration/axon/` with at least 7 real-wire tests listed above.
2. Tests run against a real Postgres instance (testcontainer or service container in CI). Test infrastructure documented in package README.
3. Build tag `//go:build integration` separates these tests from the default test run. `go test ./...` (no tag) doesn't run them; `go test -tags=integration ./internal/bead/integration/axon/...` does.
4. CI workflow `.github/workflows/integration-axon.yml` provisioned. Initial trigger: `workflow_dispatch` (manual). Validation: at least one successful run end-to-end before merge.
5. The full parameterized conformance suite from `ddx-958b8fc3` runs against the real Postgres backend (re-using the same test cases). Mock vs. real-wire produces identical pass/fail signal for every test.
6. Real-wire-specific findings documented as new tests (concurrency CAS, native heartbeat update via Postgres logs, transaction isolation, NULL/JSONB handling, timestamp precision, schema_version defaults).
7. Docs link added to TD-027 §28.4 (downstream acceptance) once landed.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:axon, area:testing, kind:test, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore must exist), IMP bead (importer used in test setup to populate Postgres with fixtures), ddx-958b8fc3 (conformance suite to re-use).
title: WIRE: Real-wire Axon integration tests against actual Postgres (CI nightly)

acceptance:
1. All five new files (`operation.go`, `id.go`, `errors.go`, `context.go`, `backend.go` expansion) exist and compile.
2. `Operation` interface declared with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter implements `Operation`. Test: `TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip`.
3. ID generation: tests `TestValidateID_AcceptsValidIDs`, `TestValidateID_RejectsInvalidCharset`, `TestValidateID_RejectsLengthBounds`, `TestRandomHexIDGenerator_ProducesValidIDs` (100 generations all pass `ValidateID`), `TestSequentialIDGenerator_ProducesUniqueOrdered` (1000 generations all distinct + lexicographically increasing).
4. Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
5. Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
6. Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.
7. `*Store.Apply` method exists; type-asserts to `OperationApplier`; falls back to generic load-mutate-save. Test: `TestStore_Apply_DelegatesToOperationApplier_WhenAvailable`, `TestStore_Apply_GenericFallback_WhenNotAvailable`.
8. `JSONLBackend` implements `OperationApplier` (empty switch + generic fallback — heartbeat-specific cases added in C bead). Compile-time assertion: `var _ OperationApplier = (*JSONLBackend)(nil)`. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_FallsThroughForUnknownOps`.
9. Bead data model invariants (TD-027 §11.2) hold across `*Store`. Test: `TestBeadDataModel_InvariantsHold` asserts the 12 universal invariants on freshly-created and mutated beads.
10. Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
11. Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
12. `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.
13. Existing tests in `cli/internal/bead/` (store_test.go, chaos_test.go, backend_conformance_test.go) continue to pass unchanged. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` is green.
14. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
15. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel errors.
4. `cli/internal/bead/context.go` — typed accessors `WithIdentity`/`IdentityFromContext`/`WithTrace`/`TraceFromContext`. Package docstring per TD-027 §9 enumerating allowed ctx values.
5. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` (expanded) — declare `BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle` (Create + Apply only at this stage), `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter` interfaces. All ctx-first. Event interfaces are declarations only in this bead; no `*Store` event compile-time assertions until E. Composite `Backend` is not added yet (other sub-interfaces missing until per-concern beads land). `OperationApplier` optional interface declared. `RawBackend` retained with new docstring warning new backends off whole-corpus shape.

## Modify *Store

6. Add `*Store.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` method per TD-027 §7.2: type-asserts wrapped `RawBackend` to `OperationApplier` and delegates; otherwise generic `WithLock + ReadAll + op.Apply + WriteAll`.
7. Add ctx as first parameter to `*Store.Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, plus `GenID`. Do not change `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind` here; E owns event ctx threading.

## Modify JSONLBackend

8. Add `JSONLBackend.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` — satisfies `OperationApplier`. Empty type-switch + generic fallback (the per-concern Claim bead adds the `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` sidecar-optimization cases later).

## Caller migration (ctx threading only — scoped to foundational non-event methods)

9. Update every caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that calls the foundational non-event methods (Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID) to pass `ctx` through. Use `cmd.Context()` for cobra commands; existing request ctx for HTTP/GraphQL handlers; `context.Background()` only where no ctx is naturally available.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) — E bead `ddx-f39b41b3`.
- Helper packages (`ops/<concern>/`) — per-concern beads handle these.
- Removing any `*Store` workflow methods (Claim, Heartbeat, TransitionLifecycle, RequestCancel, SetExecutionCooldown, etc.) — per-concern beads handle removal as they migrate callers.
- AxonStore implementation — separate bead `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- Caller narrowing from `*bead.Store` field types to `bead.Backend` interface — happens in BL bead `ddx-74452926` after per-concern beads complete.
- Module boundary lockdown (relocation to `internal/storage/`) — BL bead.
- Schema-versioning migration framework — deferred until first breaking change.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:feature, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: none — this bead is the gate.

# Cross-references
- TD-027 §5 (storage vs. workflow separation)
- TD-027 §6 (sub-interface taxonomy)
- TD-027 §7 (Operation pattern)
- TD-027 §7.2 (Apply routing through OperationApplier)
- TD-027 §8 (ID generation)
- TD-027 §9 (context discipline)
- TD-027 §10 (sentinel errors)
- TD-027 §11 (bead data model)
- TD-027 §13 (event vocabulary — controlled list)
- TD-027 §23.1 (gating bead acceptance criteria)

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defines Phase enum (Queueing | Running | Terminal) + emitPhase function.
2. Four emission sites in execute_bead_loop.go (:577, :596, :1076-1091, :1093) replaced with emitPhase calls.
3. JSONL on-disk shape byte-identical to C0 baseline (automated fixture diff asserts this in test).
4. TestEmitPhase_Terminal_WritesCorrectEvent verifies terminal event has Disposition and ParkReason fields.
5. TestEmitPhase_Running_BeforeTerminal verifies event ordering.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Phase events (queueing, running, terminal, bead-result JSONL) are emitted from 4 separate sites in execute_bead_loop.go. Adding a new phase or changing event shape requires coordinated edits across all 4 sites, introducing subtle divergence. Today the JSONL-on-disk shape is only verified by manual inspection against a baseline fixture.

ROOT CAUSE
The 4 emission sites in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:
- :577 — queueing phase event
- :596 — running phase event
- :1076-1091 — terminal phase event (with Disposition/ParkReason)
- :1093 — bead.result JSONL write

Each site constructs and emits events independently with no shared Phase enum or emitPhase abstraction. The C0 fixture diff approach (byte-identical JSONL baseline) is the only correctness gate, but it is not automated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defining Phase enum: Queueing | Running | Terminal.
- Add emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error called by state transitions.
- Replace the 4 current emission sites with calls to emitPhase.
- Verify JSONL on-disk shape is byte-identical to the C0 baseline (automated fixture diff in test).

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the JSONL event format or adding new event types.
- Renaming execute_bead_loop.go (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: terminal phase emission should fail open rather than silently diverge across call sites.
- P3: the emitted phase stream must stay observable and structured.
- P4: a phase-event bug on one bead must not affect other beads.
- P5: the phase stream supports operator-visible state.
title: C12: single phase-event emission (replaces 4 emit sites with state-machine-driven emitPhase)
