acceptance: 1. 429 errors from Fizeau trigger per-bead wait + retry per TD-031 RateLimitRetryContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Per RateLimitRetryContract: NO persisted status change before budget exhaustion; provider stays in rotation. bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
description:
Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).
title: ddx work: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)

description:
CONTEXT
This bead creates the governing feature spec for DDx Prose Quality Support. The desired user outcome is not AI-detection evasion; it is clear, specific, voice-preserving prose in DDx governing artifacts and user-facing docs. The feature must define how DDx targets common AI-writing tropes with deterministic, explainable findings.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-027-prose-quality-support.md
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md only if needed to add a short feature reference

REQUIRED SPEC CONTENT
- Problem: vague/generic prose weakens document-driven development.
- Primary users: DDx maintainers, project maintainers, and agents writing/reviewing docs.
- Content modes: technical, planning, and public prose.
- Product surfaces: default skill, deterministic checker/rules, ddx doc prose --changed, later review integration.
- Non-goals: no AI detector, no detector bypass, no blocking by default, no rewriting authorial voice away.
- Measurable acceptance criteria using structural findings: file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit; project vocabulary; advisory default.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Implementing the checker.
- Choosing the final tool boundary beyond naming deterministic prose checks.
- Adding CLI flags or plugin assets.
title: Define FEAT-027 Prose Quality Support

PROBLEM
execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 handles no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict inline in the loop body. These are distinct outcome behaviors that belong in try.Attempt so the loop only sees Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036: the block that follows a bead attempt contains outcome-specific branches (no_changes, decomposition, push_failed, push_conflict) as raw loop logic rather than Disposition values from try.Attempt.
- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): adjudicateNoChangesContract function exists in the codebase — confirm it is called from try.Attempt's no_changes branch (not merely defined). If wired: preserve. If unwired: this child must wire it.
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): EvaluateRetryPolicy (or similar) must be called from try.Attempt's rate-limit detection path — confirm actual wiring before this refactor moves it.
- try/attempt.go:67 is the candidate location for the relocated no_changes adjudication logic.
- The dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must close before this child to establish which functions are genuinely wired vs. only defined.

PROPOSED FIX
- Remove lines 875-1036 from execute_bead_loop.go; replace with: read Disposition + Cooldown + ParkReason + RecordEvents from try.Attempt result.
- Move no_changes adjudication (verification_command runner + triage label logic) into try.Attempt's no_changes branch near try/attempt.go:67.
- Move rate-limit retry (Retry-After honor + exponential backoff + budget cap) into try.Attempt's rate-limit detection.
- Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation on no_changes; stays in canonical state 6; uses triage:no-changes-unverified label per ADR-004.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not modify the Drain loop contract beyond replacing lines 875-1036 with Disposition/Cooldown reads.

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: no_changes, decomposition, push-failed, and push-conflict must fail open instead of wedging the loop.
- P2: try.Attempt owns the outcome classification; the drain loop should not own per-outcome policy.
- P3: emit structured events so operators can see the exact degradation instead of a silent retry loop.
- P4: the handling must stay per-bead.
- P6: auto-retry should remain limited to transient classes only.

description:
PROBLEM
Live `ddx work --watch` readiness checks still reject semantically usable readiness results and fail open instead of applying the intended readiness outcome. Observed failures:
- `ambiguous` should mean `operator_required`.
- `safely_refinable` should mean `needs_refine`; this was observed in Fizeau on 2026-05-15 as `readiness check unavailable: unknown classification "safely_refinable"`.
- `split` should mean `needs_split`; this was observed in DDx on 2026-05-15 as `readiness check unavailable: unknown classification "split": expected one of ready, needs_refine, needs_split, operator_required, system_unready` while evaluating `ddx-c191e91a`.
- `score` may arrive as a decimal such as `0.86`; older decoding failed with `json: cannot unmarshal number 0.86 into Go struct field .score of type int`.
- `waivers_applied` may arrive as a scalar string; on 2026-05-15 `ddx work --watch` logged `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied of type agent.preClaimReadinessWaiver` while evaluating `ddx-edd8085b`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:126-139` normalizes some legacy readiness classifications but does not cover `ambiguous`, `safely_refinable`, or `split`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:60-72` decodes classification-shaped readiness output into strict Go field shapes; `Score` must be tolerant of any present non-null JSON value, and `WaiversApplied []preClaimReadinessWaiver` currently rejects scalar or flat-string legacy/model outputs before classification can be applied. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:584-601` feeds decoded classifications through `ClassifyReadinessWithMode`, so a shape mismatch becomes an unavailable-readiness warning instead of a usable result. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:720-747` has a legacy decoder path that rejects unknown classifications before readiness outcome handling can apply.

PROPOSED FIX
Normalize legacy classification `ambiguous` to `operator_required`, `safely_refinable` to `needs_refine`, and `split` to `needs_split` for classification-shaped readiness output. Keep canonical prompt guidance saying new prompts must emit only `ready`, `needs_refine`, `needs_split`, `operator_required`, or `system_unready`; document aliases only as tolerated legacy/model-output compatibility. Make readiness JSON decoding tolerant where the value is non-decisive metadata: `score` should accept integer, float, string, object, array, bool, or null without preserving numeric meaning, and `waivers_applied` should accept null, an array of waiver objects, a flat string array, or a scalar string without failing the readiness check. Update mirrored bead-lifecycle skill text and tests so the model-facing contract, decoder, and prompt coverage agree.

NON-SCOPE
Do not make `ambiguous`, `safely_refinable`, or `split` canonical classifications. Do not use numeric `score` for routing, power inference, ordering, or bead metadata. Do not persist extra readiness metadata. Do not make loose decoding apply to decisive fields such as `classification`, `readiness_checks`, or `difficulty.estimated_difficulty`. Do not change warn-only readiness policy, timeout behavior, routing power policy, or post-run triage classifications.
title: readiness: normalize legacy classifications and loose JSON fields

description:
PROBLEM
  A 2026-05-09 alignment review of the bead lifecycle (spec TD-031, types.go,
  lifecycle.go, store.go, reconcile.go, plus all CLI/server/agent mutation
  call sites) found the state machine is well-specified but has six concrete
  alignment, duplication, and test-coverage gaps. The implementation funnels
  ~all status writes through transitionLifecycleInPlace + ValidateLifecycleTransition
  (good), but: reconcile-close bypasses ClosureGate without the spec saying so;
  Ready() filters status twice with conflicting answers; the backend conformance
  suite never exercises the transition matrix; "park to proposed" is duplicated
  6x in agent/*.go; claim-Extra key cleanup is duplicated in Reopen + Unclaim.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:97 ValidateLifecycleTransition is the single
    transition gate.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:619 transitionLifecycleInPlace is the single
    chokepoint for status writes.
  - cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:236-242 closes beads via UpdateWithLifecycleStatus
    directly, bypassing the ClosureGate enforced by store.go:1245.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1763 readyFiltered re-filters Status==StatusOpen
    after the lifecycle module already classified the bucket.
  - cli/internal/bead/backend_conformance_test.go:53-166 covers create/update/
    claim/deps/events but no status transitions.
  - 6 "park to proposed" call sites in cli/internal/agent/{execute_bead_loop.go,
    execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_review_terminal.go,
    execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go} duplicate the same boilerplate.
  - Reopen (store.go:1306-1317) and Unclaim (store.go:823-831) maintain
    parallel lists of claim-Extra keys to clear.

PROPOSED FIX
  Decompose into 6 children, executed in dependency order:
  - B1 (doc-only): amend TD-031 to declare reconcile-close vs evidence-close.
  - B2 (code): align reconcile.go with amended TD-031 and add comment at
    store.go:471 noting Create-time status default is the lone exception.
  - B3 (code): drop redundant Status==Open filter at store.go:1763; let
    EvaluateLifecycleQueue be the single source of truth for "ready".
  - B4 (test): extend backend_conformance_test.go with the TD-031 transition
    matrix against both backend_jsonl and axon_backend.
  - B5 (refactor): extract Store.ParkToProposed helper covering the 6 agent
    duplicates.
  - B6 (refactor): consolidate claim-Extra key list so Reopen + Unclaim share
    one definition.

NON-SCOPE
  - File-level reorganization of store.go (carving into claim.go/closure.go/
    queue_view.go). Pure mechanical and not in scope here; can be filed later.
  - Adding new persisted statuses or changing the canonical-status enum.
  - Changes to bd/br interchange (ADR-004 contract is fixed).

INTERSECTIONS
  - TD-031: B1 amends it; B2-B6 implement against amended spec.
  - ADR-004: out of scope (interchange contract is fixed).
  - ADR-023 (lifecycle quality policy): adjacent; not changed by this epic.
title: bead-lifecycle alignment review: spec, dedupe, and conformance

description:
PROBLEM
DDx readiness can currently turn operator-accepted open work back into status=proposed, which interrupts continuous forward progress and can create a downgrade loop after an operator promotes a bead back to open. The queue should keep moving unless DDx has a hard, evidenced operator-decision blocker.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:219-224 and :485-493 allow readiness to move ambiguous or underspecified work to status=proposed, while docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:85-99 says lifecycle quality defaults to WARN-ONLY. The policy boundary is underspecified.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 parks terminal readiness outcomes to proposed without checking whether an operator already promoted the same bead back to open.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 appends intake.blocked with outcome/detail only; it has no stable rule fingerprint or operator-override signal.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate child beads that define and implement a forward-progress readiness policy: open is the execution lane; proposed is the hard operator-decision escape; proposed -> open is a durable operator acceptance signal; readiness warnings are observable but do not block by default.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Root epic. Child beads under this epic carry the execution details and dependencies.
title: bead-lifecycle: coordinate forward-progress readiness policy

description:
PROBLEM
DDx is document-driven: PRDs, FEAT specs, technical designs, ADRs, bead descriptions, review prompts, and close notes are governing artifacts. When those documents drift into generic AI-like prose, vague importance language, or terminology inconsistency, agents receive weaker execution prompts and reviewers lose checkable design intent.

GOAL
Add DDx Prose Quality Support: DDx should help users and agents write clear, specific, voice-preserving project prose and flag common AI-writing tropes through advisory guidance plus deterministic checks. This is not an AI detector and must not be framed as detector bypass.

PROPOSED SLICES
1. Define the FEAT product contract for prose quality support.
2. Define the technical design for a deterministic prose quality pipeline.
3. Add fixtures/golden expected findings to keep false positives under control.
4. Ship a default-plugin human-writing-support skill.
5. Add default rules and DDx vocabulary.
6. Add ddx doc prose --changed as the first advisory CLI surface.
7. Document configuration and non-goals.
8. Later, integrate prose findings into bead review after noise is known.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement AI-authorship detection. Do not make prose findings blocking by default. Do not wire this into ddx work or bead review until the advisory doc command has landed and has fixture coverage.
title: EPIC: Add DDx Prose Quality Support

description:
PROBLEM
With the DDxRoot resolver routing through XDG when no in-tree .ddx/ exists (ddx-ad338ec3), the project-level `ddx init` command needs to do exactly one thing: create <project>/.ddx/. The presence of that directory is the signal that flips the project to in-tree mode for all subsequent ddx commands.

ROOT CAUSE
Today's `ddx init` does a lot: writes config.yaml, installs plugins into the project, optionally injects CLAUDE.md meta-prompt, optionally commits. With the new XDG-rooted design, most of that becomes optional or handled elsewhere.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Simplify `ddx init` (no --global flag) to:
   - Check that the current dir is the root of a git repo.
   - mkdir <project>/.ddx (if it does not already exist).
   - Optionally write a minimal <project>/.ddx/config.yaml with project-id (parsed from origin) and a single comment line "# DDx in-tree mode. See ddx-15c71970 for global tier."
   - Initialize <project>/.ddx/ with the bare minimum — no plugin install (plugins lazy-install on first use via the global path).
   - Print one line: "DDx initialized in <project>/.ddx (in-tree mode)."

2. `ddx init` MUST NOT touch <project>/.gitignore. Whether to commit .ddx/ is the operator's choice.

3. `ddx init --force` re-creates .ddx/ even if present. (Already exists today; behavior preserved.)

4. `ddx init --no-git` skips the git-repo check. (Already exists today; behavior preserved.)

5. Remove the meta-prompt injection from `ddx init`. Move it to a separate explicit command `ddx prompt sync` (or to a follow-up bead). Init should be one job — create the .ddx dir.

6. The XDG bootstrap from ddx-ad338ec3 happens transparently before `ddx init` runs. If the operator has already used ddx commands in the project, the XDG project dir exists. `ddx init` does not migrate that XDG state into the new .ddx/; the operator decides whether to do that via a separate `ddx project migrate-from-xdg` command (out of scope here).

NON-SCOPE
- Migrating state from XDG to in-tree on `ddx init` (out of scope; separate command).
- CLAUDE.md meta-prompt injection (separate command).
- Plugin install (lazy on first plugin use, sourced from global per ddx-15c71970).

description:
PROBLEM
GraphQL worker dispatch has the same parallel-struct smell as REST, so fixing only the REST handler would leave another transport able to drop future work fields.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 defines a GraphQL-local request struct and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127-141 manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. It also parses worker-loop mode and idle interval separately from REST, bypassing a shared spec lifecycle and risking a stale public alias.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode rawArgs directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Preserve workers.default_spec behavior from cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:83-104 by applying defaults onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
- Route GraphQL through the same server-side helper used by REST so ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, Mode/IdleInterval semantics, and StartExecuteLoop are not reimplemented.
- Delete the GraphQL-local request struct and the parallel StartExecuteLoop literal.
- Do not accept or emit the removed polling flag alias on the GraphQL worker-dispatch path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change GraphQL schema or worker kind names beyond the rawArgs decoded into the shared spec.
- Do not change workers.max_count semantics.
- Do not change REST handler behavior; that belongs to the REST child.
notes: Removed the stale GraphQL poll field requirement. GraphQL now follows the same Mode plus IdleInterval contract as REST/server worker dispatch.

description:
PROBLEM
The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.
- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).
- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:
  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.
  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.
  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.
- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).
- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Specs listed under SPEC IMPACT are updated in the same commit as the code refactor. Each updated spec references this bead ID.
- cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. Existing tests pass without modification.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

2. Add `TestDoctorLegacySkillSymlinkDirsFlagsDDxSymlink` proving `.agents/skills/ddx` or `.claude/skills/ddx` as a symlink still reports the corresponding parent directory.
3. Add or update `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_IgnoresNonDDxProjectSkillSymlink` so `checkProjectRuntimePreflight` does not populate `LegacySymlinkDirs` and `emitPreflightWarning` stays silent when bead-lifecycle exists and only non-DDx project skills are symlinks.
4. Replace the over-broad arbitrary-symlink expectation in `TestProjectRuntimePreflight_LegacySkillSymlinkSuggestsUpdateForce` with a DDx-skill symlink fixture, or rename/split the test so arbitrary non-DDx symlinks are explicitly ignored.
5. Running `/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx doctor` from `/Users/erik/Projects/helix` no longer exits with `legacy skill symlinks detected` solely because `.agents/skills/helix` and `.claude/skills/helix` are symlinks. Non-critical Vale warnings may remain.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run "TestDoctorLegacySkillSymlinkDirs|TestProjectRuntimePreflight" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
After `ddx update --force` refreshed Helix's DDx skill package, `/Users/erik/Projects/helix` still reports a degraded skill layout. `ddx doctor` exits with `legacy skill symlinks detected`, and every Helix `ddx work --watch` restart emits:

```
preflight warning: project-local skill layout is degraded (project root: /Users/erik/Projects/helix)
  legacy skill symlinks under .agents/skills
  legacy skill symlinks under .claude/skills
  run: ddx update --force
  run: ddx doctor
```

The symlinks are Helix's own project skills (`.agents/skills/helix -> ../../skills/helix` and `.claude/skills/helix -> ../../skills/helix`), not stale DDx-managed skill installs. This leaves every Helix `ddx work --watch` cycle with a false remediation loop even after the DDx skill directories are real files.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight.go:35-40` calls `legacySkillSymlinkDirs(projectRoot)` and treats any returned parent directory as a degraded work/try/server preflight. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:375-384` uses the same helper to fail full-scope doctor. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:406-431` scans every entry in `.agents/skills` and `.claude/skills` and returns the parent directory once it sees any symlink, without checking whether the symlink is actually the DDx-managed `ddx` skill or another project-owned skill. `cli/cmd/project_runtime_preflight_test.go:153-177` currently encodes the over-broad behavior by expecting an arbitrary `helix-align` symlink to be flagged.

PROPOSED FIX
Restrict the legacy-symlink detector to DDx-managed installs and make both doctor and runtime preflight use that narrower result. It should still flag `.agents/skills/ddx` and `.claude/skills/ddx` when those entries are symlinks, but it must ignore unrelated project skill symlinks such as Helix's `.agents/skills/helix` and `.claude/skills/helix`. Update remediation text and test names so the warning clearly targets stale DDx skill installs, not every symlink in a project skill directory.

NON-SCOPE
Do not rewrite Helix's skill layout from DDx. Do not make `ddx update --force` remove non-DDx project skills. Do not suppress unrelated doctor warnings such as Vale, git config health, missing bead-lifecycle, or real DDx skill symlink installs.
title: doctor/preflight: scope legacy skill symlink check to DDx installs

acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.
title: agent: wrap artifact bodies with untrusted-data delimiters

Its acceptance criteria are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt: - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)" - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)" - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5" - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate - "CHANGELOG entry merged" Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed, and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses. Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`): - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill) - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5 The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap — not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed. Recommended next action for the human triager: 1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section. 2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`. 3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied. No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):

  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap

  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

acceptance:
1. `ddx doc prose --changed` exists in CLI help and runs against changed Markdown files in a git repo.
2. Command output includes file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, and suggested edit for fixture-backed findings.
3. Tests prove default findings are advisory and do not use a blocking exit code unless an explicit config/flag from TD-036 enables it.
4. Tests prove config vocabulary accept/reject and mode settings affect findings.
5. Missing external-checker behavior, if applicable, is covered by a test and produces a clear infrastructure diagnostic without panic.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/docprose/... -run "Prose|Doc"` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
CONTEXT
The first executable surface for Prose Quality Support is an advisory command over changed Markdown files. This should give users value without blocking ddx work, ddx bead review, or ddx doc validate by default.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/cmd/doc*.go or existing doc command files
- cli/internal/docprose/ checker integration code
- config schema/types for prose settings if not already landed
- command tests under cli/cmd and checker tests under cli/internal/docprose

COMMAND BEHAVIOR
- Add `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Optionally support explicit paths if TD-036 includes them, but changed Markdown is required.
- Findings include file, line, rule id, severity, rationale, and suggested edit.
- Exit behavior is advisory by default; do not break unrelated workflows on findings.
- Respect project config for mode, vocabulary, includes/excludes, and severity threshold.
- If an external checker is selected by TD-036 and missing, emit a clear advisory infrastructure message per the TD rather than panicking.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- Bead review integration.
- CI blocking policy.
- AI-authorship scoring.
title: Add advisory ddx doc prose --changed command

acceptance:
1. POST /api/agent/workers/work decodes directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec (no intermediate private struct).
2. ProjectRoot resolved via existing resolveRequestedProject, written back to spec before ApplyDefaults and Validate.
3. HTTP 400 returned for unsupported future SpecVersion with JSON capability list; unknown JSON fields tolerated.
4. Existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior preserved.
5. TestRESTWorkerStart_DecodeIntoExecuteLoopSpec verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields survive the POST decode path.
6. TestRESTWorkerStart_UnknownSpecVersion returns 400 with capability list.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The REST worker start endpoint drops execution fields because it decodes into a handler-local request struct that is narrower than the CLI/runtime spec.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 defines a private request struct missing opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version. cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 then manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, creating another silent omission point.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode POST /api/agent/workers/work directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Resolve ProjectRoot using existing resolveRequestedProject behavior, write it back to the spec, then call ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop.
- Add a shared server-side start helper if needed so REST and GraphQL use the same ApplyDefaults -> Validate -> StartExecuteLoop lifecycle.
- Return HTTP 400 for unsupported future SpecVersion with a JSON capability list; keep unknown JSON fields tolerated for newer-server/older-client compatibility.
- Preserve existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rework HTTP transport or endpoint path.
- Do not modify GraphQL dispatch; a separate child owns that parallel path.
- Do not change worker runtime consumption beyond what is necessary to pass the unified spec to StartExecuteLoop.
title: server: decode REST work workers from ExecuteLoopSpec

description:
PROBLEM
The efficacy page filter test is timing out before it can exercise the URL-binding logic. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:226-240` looks for a combobox named `powerClass`, but the page renders a labeled select whose accessible name is `Power class` in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.svelte:250-330`. The spec therefore never reaches the filter interactions it is supposed to verify.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:226-240` targets the wrong accessible name for the power-class control.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.svelte:250-330` renders the actual filter form with `Power class`, `Label`, and `Spec ID` controls.

PROPOSED FIX
Align the Playwright selector and the rendered control contract so the efficacy filter test can reach the existing select/input widgets and verify URL encoding. Keep the change limited to the efficacy page and its e2e spec; do not alter the overall efficacy aggregation or comparison flow.

NON-SCOPE
- Efficacy aggregation data shape.
- Comparison dispatch behavior.
- Other project pages or screenshots.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts` US-096.b passes against the current efficacy page.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/efficacy.spec.ts --grep "US-096.b"` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: align efficacy filter controls with accessible labels

acceptance: 1. Default --poll-interval = 30s in all four call sites: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. 2. execute_bead_loop.go:440-441 no longer returns early when poll-interval > 0; sleeps + retries instead. Per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps reset on no-candidate path. 3. execute_bead_loop.go:483-508 preflight rejection becomes per-bead continue, not loop exit. 4. loop.end event includes structured exit_reason field. Recognized values include 'sigterm', 'sigint', 'fatal_config', 'once_complete', 'explicit_poll_zero'; 'providers_exhausted' slot reserved for ddx-aede917d. 5. Server-managed workers report no_ready_work as transient (worker stays in 'running' state with 'idle' substate); existing 'exited' status reserved for true exits per AC #4. 6. Tests: TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue (poll-interval > 0; verify loop stays alive across N empty polls); TestLoop_PreflightRejectionContinues (one bead's preflight failure doesn't abandon siblings); TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits (back-compat); TestLoop_ExplicitPollZeroExits (back-compat). 7. Manual verification: spawn worker via 'ddx work --harness claude'; let queue drain; verify worker stays running > 5 min after queue empty (vs current behavior of immediate exit). 8. Update FEAT-006 / FEAT-010 docs noting the new long-running default + the explicit opt-out flags.
description:
Workers exit prematurely even when ready beads remain in the queue. Operators have to manually relaunch, defeating the point of a long-running drain.

## Root causes (from sub-agent investigation)

1. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:440-441**: when nextCandidate returns no eligible bead AND runtime.PollInterval <= 0, the loop returns immediately. Combined with the per-Run 'attempted' map (line 388, 476), once each currently-ready bead has been *attempted once* the loop exits even though those same beads (or new arrivals) are still queue-ready.

2. **cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:483-508**: routing-preflight rejection on a single bead exits the entire loop ('return result, nil'), abandoning all other ready beads.

3. **Default --poll-interval=0 everywhere**: cli/cmd/work.go:66, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1568, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:115, cli/internal/server/server.go:2281. All three recent server-spawned workers in .ddx/workers/ show poll_interval: '0s', so cause #1 fires the first empty poll.

## Fix shape (locked decisions baked in)

- Flip default --poll-interval from 0 → **30s** everywhere (CLI + server adapter). Operator can override with --once for one-shot or explicit --poll-interval=N.
- Reset per-Run 'attempted'/'hookFailed' maps when the loop falls through to 'no candidate', before sleeping. Prevents the 'attempted-once = exit' trap.
- Convert preflight early-exit (483-508) to per-bead 'continue' so one bead's routing failure doesn't abandon the rest.
- Add structured exit_reason to loop.end event; reserve 'providers_exhausted' slot for ddx-aede917d (quota-pause work).
- Server-managed workers expose 'no_ready_work' as a **transient 'running (idle)' substate**, not terminal. Worker stays alive; UI shows running (idle).
- Preserve --once and explicit --poll-interval=0 for back-compat (legacy 'drain-and-exit' semantics).

Acceptable exit conditions remain (per existing beads):
- SIGTERM / SIGINT (operator-driven)
- All providers durably out (per ddx-aede917d quota pause/resume — workers pause+probe, not exit)
- No providers configured at all (fatal config error)
- --once explicitly requested
- --poll-interval=0 explicitly requested
title: ddx work / agent work: stay alive while queue has ready work and viable providers exist

acceptance:
1. Gate bead ddx-3c4618e3 is closed after DDx upgrades to a Fizeau release without ServiceConfig ModelRoute* compatibility stubs.
2. Child cleanup beads are ordered so canonical progress adoption precedes opaque forwarding, and opaque forwarding precedes deletion of legacy transcript/session-log code.
3. Cleanup children cover canonical progress consumption, no-op compaction breaker removal, opaque service-event forwarding, direct Claude stream parser deletion, session-log formatter/tailer deletion, worker subprocess boundary cleanup, and ResolveRoute execution preflight removal.
4. When the epic closes, rg -n 'parseClaudeStream|runClaudeStreamingFn|FormatSessionLogLines|TailSessionLogs|serviceNoopCompaction|buildRoutePreflight|ExecuteBeadWithConfig|worker.Run\(' cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server has no production hits except intentionally retained tests or status/debug-only helpers.
5. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Fizeau now owns transcript, progress, routing-service event semantics, and session rendering for agent execution. DDx still has cleanup debt that parses Claude streams, renders Fizeau session logs, infers runtime status from Fizeau internals, and calls route/service APIs from DDx execution paths.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go still drains and interprets Fizeau service events for DDx-specific runtime policy.
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go still contains legacy direct stream handling.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go and cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go still render/tail inner agent logs.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go still mixes DDx worker lifecycle output with inner Fizeau progress/session surfaces.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate child cleanup beads so DDx keeps only worker/bead lifecycle, opaque Fizeau event forwarding, artifact links, and final DDx Result projection. Fizeau owns inner transcript/progress/session semantics.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau schema or API changes.
- DDx queue lifecycle state, bead state transitions, worker watchdog policy, or final Result storage outside the specific boundary cleanup children.

description:
PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent work and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent work and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing work/try behavior.
notes: Superseded by ddx-f088a3fd / ddx-b194d20f and audited by ddx-bdc0065e. This cleanup keeps ddx agent work / ddx agent execute-bead as compatibility aliases, which conflicts with the remove-not-deprecate decision.
title: C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported

acceptance:

2. reviewSessionEvents subscription in schema.graphql.
3. All four resolver implementations present in graphql_resolver.go.
4. Delta streaming optional; final-message required (verified by test).
5. TestGraphQL_ReviewSessionStart verifies mutation creates a ReviewSession with correct fields.
6. TestGraphQL_ReviewSessionRespond verifies turn appended to session.

10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
No GraphQL mutations or subscription exist for review sessions. The review panel (Story 18) cannot start, respond to, or cancel a review session through the GraphQL API, and there is no way to stream review response deltas to the frontend.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: no reviewSessionStart, reviewSessionRespond, reviewSessionCancel mutations defined.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql: no reviewSessionEvents subscription defined.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go: no resolver implementations for the above.
- ddx-f7c3d512 (turn dispatcher, a dep) must land first — it provides the dispatch path that these mutations invoke.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add to schema.graphql:
  - Mutation.reviewSessionStart(sessionId, artifactId, artifactSha, templateRef, promptRef, maxBillableUSD): ReviewSession
  - Mutation.reviewSessionRespond(sessionId, turn): ReviewSession
  - Mutation.reviewSessionCancel(sessionId): Boolean
  - Subscription.reviewSessionEvents(sessionId): ReviewSessionEvent (streaming deltas optional; final-message required)
- Add resolver implementations in graphql_resolver.go for each mutation + subscription.
- Streaming deltas: optional (loosened per codex from strict streaming AC); final-message is required.

NON-SCOPE
- Reviewer routing and cost cap (ddx-f1e12904).
- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).
title: review: GraphQL mutations + reviewSessionEvents (status + optional deltas)

PROPOSED FIX

- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.

9. shouldSuppressNoProgress (execute_bead_loop.go:1522) extends to short-circuit on OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict}. Test TestSuppressNoProgress_HonorsTransientReasons.

10. cli/cmd/try.go wires both hooks into Runtime construction at try.go:327. Test TestTry_HooksWired.

11. WIRED-IN: 'git grep PreDispatchLintHook cli/cmd/' shows the wire site; 'git grep PostAttemptTriageHook cli/cmd/' shows same.

12. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.

13. Manual verification: 'ddx try <retrofit-bead-id>' (after epic child #2 ships sub-skill) emits bead-quality.lint event in the bead's event stream; 'ddx try <bead-with-bad-AC>' under BLOCK mode refuses dispatch with operator-readable message.

14. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go + cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go + cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go + cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go + cli/cmd/try.go + cli/internal/agent/*_test.go + cli/cmd/try_test.go only.
description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 + sub-skill bead-lifecycle/: ddx try needs to invoke the sub-skill at two points (pre-dispatch lint, post-attempt triage) and act on the structured output. Today ddx try has hook precedents (PreClaimHook, RoutePreflight) but no LLM-invoking hook + no outcome classification carrier.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:24-38 defines ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with PreClaimHook + RoutePreflight closures; no slot for lint or triage hooks.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:657 RoutePreflight runs between pick and claim — exactly where lint should fire.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:45 ExecuteBeadResult already has reason + failure_mode; ExecuteBeadReport (loop event payload) does NOT — only status/detail/rationale at execute_bead_loop.go:1317.
- shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 already respects Disrupted bool. Codex amendment: KEEP Disrupted; ADD OutcomeReason beside it (do not replace).
- ddx try at cli/cmd/try.go:327 wires PreClaimHook today; sibling fields will go through the same Runtime construction.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime additions (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 30):
   PreDispatchLintHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
   PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
   LintResult struct { Score int; Rationale string; SuggestedFixes []string; WaiversApplied []string }
   TriageResult struct { Classification string; RecommendedAction string; Rationale string; SuggestedAmendments string; SuggestedFollowupBeads []FollowupBead }

2. PreDispatchLintHook fires AFTER nextCandidate, BEFORE Claim (sibling to RoutePreflight at execute_bead_loop.go:662).
   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in lint mode (per FEAT-001/FEAT-010 layer-1 invocation; NOT subprocess via ddx agent run per codex).
   - WARN-ONLY behavior (default): record lint result as bead event (kind=bead-quality.lint); proceed with claim regardless of score.
   - BLOCK mode (opt-in via config flag in .ddx/config.yaml): refuse claim when score < threshold; emit operator-readable explanation + cite bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance for fixes.
   - FAIL-OPEN per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P1: infrastructure failure (skill missing, harness unavailable, bad JSON, timeout) → emit warning event + proceed. Only valid low-score response blocks (and only in BLOCK mode).

3. PostAttemptTriageHook fires AFTER outcome adjudication, BEFORE cooldown decision (read execute_bead_loop.go ~line 880-1100 for the outcome class chain).
   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in triage mode with (bead + outcome event + session log excerpt).
   - Triage classification populates new ExecuteBeadReport.OutcomeReason field (string).
   - shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 extends to also short-circuit when OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict} (transient classes per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P6). Disrupted check stays in place beside it.
   - FAIL-OPEN: triage hook failure → no classification → fall through to existing cooldown logic (today's behavior, no regression).

4. cli/cmd/try.go integration: wire PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook into ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime construction at try.go:327. Operator can disable via --no-lint / --no-triage flags (defer if not needed; defaults are warn-only so explicit disable is rare).

5. Hook invocation cost: lint runs once per dispatch attempt (cheap sonnet call); triage runs once per non-clean outcome. Both invocations cite the bead-lifecycle skill by name in the prompt; harness auto-discovers from filesystem (no special skill-loading code needed).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work integration (this bead is ddx try only; ddx work integration is a separate follow-up if desired)
- BLOCK mode threshold tuning (operator chooses post-baseline; no code change)
- ddx work plan quality column (deferred per epic non-scope)
- GraphQL state.OutcomeReason exposure (defer; today's GraphQL maps status only at state_graphql.go:493; if exposure needed, separate bead per codex)
- --force --reason CLI flag (add only if BLOCK mode rollout reveals need; defer)

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 claim/heartbeat methods (`Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh`). Workers in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, `cli/internal/server/workers.go`, and the bead CLI hold `*Store` concretely for these. Per TD-027 §12 + §14, claim semantics belong in `ops/claim/` helper + typed `Operation` values, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, ClaimOp{...})`. JSONLBackend's sidecar-write optimization for heartbeats (per `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go`) MUST be preserved through the optional `OperationApplier` interface added in Foundation.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Heartbeats are written via a sidecar (`claim_liveness.go`) to avoid corpus churn at high frequency. The Foundation bead added `*Store.Apply` + `JSONLBackend.Apply` with an empty type-switch + generic fallback. The sidecar optimization needs the `SetClaimHeartbeat` case wired into `JSONLBackend.Apply`'s switch.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §11.5 + §12 + §14 for the claim concern:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `ClaimOp{Owner, Session, Worktree string}` — CAS via `Apply` error return; sets claim fields and `claim.at = time.Now()`. Returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` from `Apply(*Bead)` if claim is held by different owner.
   - `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner string}` — clears claim; returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` if RequireOwner non-empty and doesn't match.
   - `SetClaimHeartbeat{At time.Time}`, `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}` — heartbeat field mutators.
2. Wire `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat` and `ClearClaimHeartbeat` to use `writeClaimSidecar` / `removeClaimSidecar` (preserving today's optimization). All other ops fall through to generic load-mutate-save.
3. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/liveness.go`:
   - `Acquire(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, id string, opts AcquireOptions) error` — wraps `ClaimOp`.
   - `Release(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{}` (unconditional).
   - `ReleaseAs(ctx, l, id, requiredOwner string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner: requiredOwner}`.
   - `Touch(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `SetClaimHeartbeat{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}`.
   - `IsFresh(b *Bead, maxAge time.Duration) bool` — pure predicate.
4. Migrate callers from `*Store.Claim`/`ClaimWithOptions`/`Unclaim`/`Heartbeat`/`TouchClaimHeartbeat`/`RemoveClaimHeartbeat`/`ClaimHeartbeatFresh` to `ops/claim/` helpers:
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` (worker claim/heartbeat path)
   - `cli/internal/server/workers.go`
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (operator `ddx bead update --claim`/`--unclaim`)
   - `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`, related agent files
5. Remove `*Store.Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Stale-claim sweep policy — TD-031 §6.2 owns that operational concern; storage exposes the field and helpers; sweep policy lives in the drain loop.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, cancel, cooldown, etc.) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Modifying claim semantics (TD-027 §12).

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 4 claim Operation types. `ClaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` on owner mismatch. `UnclaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` when `RequireOwner` is set and doesn't match. Tests: `TestOperation_ClaimOp_RejectsConflict`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Honored`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Empty_Unconditional`.
2. `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` write only the sidecar; main corpus untouched. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_SetClaimHeartbeat_UsesSidecar` (compare mtime of `beads.jsonl` before/after).
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/` package exists with the helpers + tests: `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_ReturnsErrAlreadyClaimed`, `TestOpsClaim_Release_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_ReleaseAs_RequiresMatchingOwner`, `TestOpsClaim_Touch_BumpsHeartbeat`, `TestOpsClaim_IsFresh_RespectsMaxAge`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package. Verified by grep.
6. Workers continue to claim, heartbeat, and unclaim beads correctly. Test: end-to-end smoke `TestWorkerLifecycle_ClaimHeartbeatUnclaim` exercising a fake worker through `ops/claim/` helpers.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:workflow, area:claim, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (Foundation — depends on Operation pattern, BeadLifecycle, *Store.Apply, JSONLBackend.OperationApplier).
title: C: Claim + Heartbeat helpers (sidecar-preserved) + remove 7 *Store claim methods (TD-027 §12, §14)

2. TestConfigPrecedence_GlobalOverridesHardcoded asserts the global tier overrides hardcoded defaults for any key it defines.
3. TestConfigPrecedence_PartialOverlayPreservesUntouchedKeys asserts a layer that defines only library.branch (but not library.repo) leaves repo coming from the lower layer.
4. TestConfig_UnknownKeyWarnsAndContinues asserts an unknown top-level key in any layer emits a single warning and does not abort startup.

description:
PROBLEM
Convention defaults and the --global tier from ddx-15c71970 cover the common cases. Some projects need to override: a different library repo, a non-default plugin set, project-specific persona bindings, a custom meta-prompt path. Today, .ddx/config.yaml exists and is used; this bead documents and tests it as an explicit per-project overlay on top of the precedence chain established by ddx-15c71970.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/config/ has loaders today, but the precedence rules (in-tree > global > hardcoded) are not explicitly tested or documented. After the epic, the contract needs to be clear so operators can rely on it.

PROPOSED FIX

1. Document the precedence chain in cli/internal/config/CONFIG-PRECEDENCE.md (or in the package godoc):
   - Hardcoded defaults (baked into the binary).
   - Overlaid by ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml if present.
   - Overlaid by <project>/.ddx/config.yaml if present (only when in-tree mode is active).
   - Each layer is a delta on the one below. Keys present in higher layers override; keys absent fall through.

2. Define the supported config keys explicitly. The minimum set:
   - library.repo (URL)
   - library.branch
   - library.version (pin)
   - plugins (list of {name, version})
   - personas.bindings (role → persona map)
   - meta_prompt.path (relative to library or absolute)
   - meta_prompt.inject_into (target file, default CLAUDE.md)

3. Add validation: unknown keys at the top level surface a non-fatal warning ("DDx config: unknown key 'foo' in <path>; ignored"). Typos should be discoverable; unknown keys should not break operation.

4. Tests assert each layer's overlay behavior and the visibility of values from each layer at command time.

NON-SCOPE
- Config format migration (none expected; .ddx/config.yaml exists today).
- Multi-machine config sync (operator's responsibility; the XDG dir can be a git repo with a remote).
- Config-driven plugin install (covered by ddx-15c71970's global install — both modes use the same registry).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1).
ddx-ad338ec3 (E2).
ddx-15c71970 (E3).
ddx-cd29c518 (E4).
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.

description:
PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement in coordinator topology. When the hub forwards a mutation to a spoke, there is no signed attestation of origin identity, and the spoke cannot distinguish a legitimate coordinator-forwarded request from a hub-originated forgery. Without dual authorization on both origin and coordinator identity, any compromised hub can submit mutations on behalf of any operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: no attestation metadata structure defined; no signing of identity claims.
- cli/internal/server/server.go: requireTrusted checks only immediate transport peer (hub); does not validate origin identity independently.
- wake-on-approve routing (the 'wake-on-approve targets owning client node's loop' requirement) has no implementation path in the current federation layer.
- ddx-f171a715 (federation foundation, a dep) and ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define attestation metadata structure: signed structured metadata carrying origin identity (WhoIs peer at coordinator edge) and coordinator identity (hub's own attested ID). Signing mechanism: HMAC or similar per plan-locked decisions.
- Spoke validates BOTH: origin identity (verified against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND hub identity (verified against registered federation peers).
- wake-on-approve: when spoke receives an approve mutation, it routes wake signal to the owning client node's work (project-ownership authoritative on the spoke).

NON-SCOPE
- Transport-level encryption (handled by tailnet).
- Key management or certificate infrastructure beyond HMAC shared secret.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior.
title: operator-prompts: multi-node trust attestation forwarding (gates non-localhost write)
