description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
- TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
- Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
notes: decomposed into ddx-fd98ffaa (registry CLI/tests), ddx-b6fea19a (cmd/config/git audit), ddx-3bbf066b (agent/exec audit), ddx-16fcc557 (server/graphql audit), ddx-2f93f5d9 (test sweep + pre-commit audit)
title: ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 4

description:
# PROBLEM
The bead interface refactor (`docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-11-bead-backend-interfaces.md`) opens 5 categories of inadvertent regression that compile-time and test-time checks alone cannot catch:

1. **Direct file I/O against `.ddx/beads.jsonl`** outside the documented allowlist — encodes the file format into another package; silently breaks on schema changes.
2. **New types implementing `RawBackend`** for new backends — recreates the catastrophic whole-corpus-per-mutation problem that AxonBackend hit (see audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`).
3. **Untyped `ctx.Value(...)` calls in `cli/internal/bead/`** — `Context` becomes a god object, undermining the typed-accessor discipline.
4. **New callers holding `*bead.Store` as a struct field** when a narrower sub-interface would suffice — interface segregation (ISP) benefits never realized; future migration toward non-`*Store` impls (AxonStore) is harder.
5. **New `bead.NewStore(...)` calls** in production code outside the configured factory — bypasses capability validation and backend selection (Mock/prod divergence risk).

These categories are enumerated as side-doors S2, S3, S9, S1, S5+S6 in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-11-bead-interface-integration.md`. The plan specifies enforcement via lint rules. This bead implements those lints.

# ROOT CAUSE
No lint coverage today. The integration plan calls for:

- `cli/tools/lints/direct-bead-jsonl-io` — flag `os.ReadFile/WriteFile/OpenFile/Create` against `beads.jsonl` or `beads-archive.jsonl` paths outside an allowlist.
- `cli/tools/lints/no-new-rawbackend-impls` — flag new types implementing `bead.RawBackend` outside the allowlist (`JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`).
- `cli/tools/lints/typed-context-accessors-only` — flag `ctx.Value(...)` calls in `cli/internal/bead/` that don't go through a typed accessor.
- `cli/tools/lints/concrete-store-methods` — warn on new files in `cli/{cmd,internal/agent,internal/server,internal/escalation}` that introduce a `*bead.Store` field type when a narrower sub-interface would suffice.
- `cli/tools/lints/no-internal-store-construction` — flag `bead.NewStore(...)` calls outside test packages and the configured factory.

None of these exist today.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement five Go analyzers (`golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis`) under `cli/tools/lints/`:

```
cli/tools/lints/
  Makefile                              # build the bead-lints binary
  cmd/bead-lints/main.go                # composes the 5 analyzers into a single vettool
  internal/
    directio/         analyzer.go       # direct-bead-jsonl-io
    rawbackend/       analyzer.go       # no-new-rawbackend-impls
    ctxaccessors/     analyzer.go       # typed-context-accessors-only
    concretestore/    analyzer.go       # concrete-store-methods
    nointernalstore/  analyzer.go       # no-internal-store-construction
  allowlist.yaml                        # maintained allowlist (paths + types + rationale)
```

Each analyzer:

- Reads `cli/tools/lints/allowlist.yaml` to know which files / types / packages are grandfathered.
- Reports violations as `analysis.Diagnostic` with `path:line: <message>`.
- Has at least three test cases: one positive (caught), one negative (allowlist match → not caught), one regression (allowlist precision check).

`allowlist.yaml` shape:

```yaml
direct-bead-jsonl-io:
  - file: cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go
    rationale: "Repair tool operates on the file as data, not entity"
  - file: cli/cmd/bead.go
    rationale: "Git auto-commit + conflict resolution; operates on file as file"
  - file: cli/cmd/sync.go
    rationale: "Sync tool synchronizes the file across machines"
  # ...

no-new-rawbackend-impls:
  - type: cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:JSONLBackend
  - type: cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go:ExternalBackend

typed-context-accessors-only:
  # allowlist entries for legitimate raw ctx.Value calls if needed
  []

concrete-store-methods:
  # files allowed to hold *bead.Store as a field (grandfathered)
  - file: cli/cmd/bead.go
  - file: cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go
  # ... (initial pass: every existing file that holds *bead.Store)

no-internal-store-construction:
  # callers allowed to construct *Store directly (production use only — test files exempt automatically)
  - file: cli/cmd/init.go
    rationale: "Initial store creation during ddx init"
  - file: cli/internal/server/server.go
    rationale: "Server bootstrap creates per-project stores"
  # ...
```

Integration:

- `lefthook.yml` pre-commit hook adds `bead-lints` command: glob `cli/**/*.go`, run `cli/tools/lints/cmd/bead-lints/main.go` against staged files.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` adds a step: `go run ./cli/tools/lints/cmd/bead-lints ./...`.
- `make lint` in `cli/Makefile` invokes `bead-lints` alongside the existing `golangci-lint`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Migrating existing code to satisfy the lints (the allowlist grandfathers existing patterns).
- Generic Go-lint rules outside the 5 categories.
- Replacement of the existing `golangci-lint` setup.
- Lint rules for the website autogen work, the Axon work, or any other domain.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/tools/lints/cmd/bead-lints/main.go` exists; composes all 5 analyzers as a single vettool. `go run ./cli/tools/lints/cmd/bead-lints --help` lists each analyzer.
2. Each of the 5 analyzers has its own package (`internal/directio/`, etc.) with:
   - A `var Analyzer = &analysis.Analyzer{...}` declaration.
   - At least one positive test case (`TestAnalyzer_DetectsViolation`).
   - At least one negative test case (`TestAnalyzer_AllowlistSuppresses`).
   - At least one precision-regression test (`TestAnalyzer_DoesNotFalsePositiveOn...`).
3. `cli/tools/lints/allowlist.yaml` exists with documented entries for every existing pattern that would otherwise be flagged. Each entry has a `rationale:` field.
4. `lefthook.yml` runs `bead-lints` on pre-commit against staged Go files in `cli/`.
5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` adds a `bead-lints` step that runs against all Go files in `cli/`.
6. `cli/Makefile` `lint` target invokes `bead-lints`.
7. Running `bead-lints` against the current `cli/` codebase produces ZERO violations (the allowlist grandfathers everything).
8. Adding a new file with a deliberate violation produces a clear `path:line: <message>` diagnostic.
9. Tests: `TestDirectIO_CatchesNewViolation`, `TestRawBackend_CatchesNewImpl`, `TestCtxAccessors_CatchesUntypedValue`, `TestConcreteStore_CatchesNewFieldType`, `TestNoInternalStore_CatchesNewConstruction`.
10. `cd cli && go test ./tools/lints/...` is green.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes (including the bead-lints itself running against the new lint code).

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:tooling, area:ci, kind:tooling, spec:FEAT-004

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which defines the patterns these lints enforce).
notes: REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). TD-027 is now the canonical acceptance criteria source — its §28 (Acceptance Criteria) supersedes any AC enumerated in the prior plan doc. TD-031 also folded into TD-027. The bead's design hold (cooldown until 2027-01-01 for ddx-c6317784) remains in effect until the consolidated spec package is reviewed and committed.
title: Bead interface integrity lint suite — 5 Go analyzers + CI gate (interface-refactor enforcement)

acceptance:
1. library/checks/go-production-reachability/ exists with: README.md (usage), check.yaml (template), implementation (binary or script).
2. Implementation reads DIFF_BASE/DIFF_HEAD/PROJECT_ROOT/EVIDENCE_DIR from env; computes new symbols from diff; runs deadcode-rooted reachability; writes result.json conforming to REACH-PROTO schema.
3. // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation parsed; bead existence + open-status verified against .ddx/beads.jsonl; invalid annotation = violation.
4. _test.go files excluded from both root selection and reachability calculation (so a unit-test-only reference does NOT satisfy the check).
5. Self-test: synthetic project with one unwired exported function returns status=block with that symbol in violations.
6. Self-test: same project with the function wired into cmd handler returns status=pass.
7. Self-test: same project with // wiring:pending annotation citing an open bead returns status=pass; annotation citing a closed/missing bead returns status=block.
8. This project installs it: .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml committed; ddx ac run on any current bead succeeds (or BLOCKs with the known unwired functions — see REACH-BACKFILL for resolution).
9. cd cli && go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence file at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/production-reachability.json shows the check ran and its findings.
description:
Ship the Go-specific production-reachability check as a library resource conforming to the REACH-PROTO check protocol. NOT installed by default for arbitrary projects (Go-opinion would leak through ddx init). This project (ddx itself) opts in by adding .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml.

DESIGN
- Location: library/checks/go-production-reachability/ (alongside library/personas/, library/patterns/)
- Implementation: a small Go binary or shell script invoking golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode rooted at configurable entry packages.
- Default entry roots for ddx self-use: cli/cmd/..., cli/internal/server/graphql/..., cli/internal/server/http/...
- Logic:
  1. Read DIFF_BASE..DIFF_HEAD from env. Compute set of new Go symbols (functions, methods, types) added by the diff via AST parse of changed files.
  2. Run deadcode (or callgraph) rooted at the configured entry packages, EXCLUDING _test.go from roots and from reachability.
  3. For each new symbol not reachable from any entry root → violation { file, line, symbol, kind: "unreachable", detail: "no path from entry roots: <list>" }
  4. Recognize // wiring:pending <bead-id> annotation as escape hatch for individual symbols. The check verifies the named bead exists and is open via local beads.jsonl read; otherwise the annotation is rejected.
  5. Write ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/production-reachability.json with the protocol-defined schema.
- Config: library/checks/go-production-reachability/check.yaml template that operators copy to .ddx/checks/ and customize entry_roots for their project.

DOGFOOD
- This project installs it: write .ddx/checks/production-reachability.yaml referencing the library check + cli's entry roots.
- The next ddx try on any bead touching cli/**/*.go will exercise the check.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Auto-install during ddx init (would leak Go-opinion). Operators opt in manually or via ddx install check (future, separate bead).
- Coverage-based "exercised via CLI" check (entered_via flavor). Static reachability is sufficient for the immediate problem.
- Python/TS/Rust analogues. Future contributions; protocol supports them.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (above)
title: checks: ship Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability)

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (or landing_chaos_test.go) contains TestTrackerLock_LandingPushBlocksTrackerCommit asserting CommitTracker completes during a slow push.
2. The same file contains TestTrackerLock_ConcurrentLandDuringPushRaceRecovery exercising worker B arriving during worker A's landPushAutoRecover window.
3. The same file contains TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLand exercising local-ahead recovery after breakStaleTrackerLock fires.
4. Each test uses a fake LandingGitOps with deterministic latency injection (no real wall-clock sleeps in CI; synctest or channel barriers).
5. With the sister fix landed, all three pass; without it, at minimum test #1 fails (proving the regression coverage is real).
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Existing chaos tests (cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go)
  exercise only the bead.Store JSONL/axon file-lock layer. None of them
  exercise the higher-level withMainGitLock / withTrackerLock locks in
  cli/internal/agent/, and none model the multi-process race conditions
  that have produced real-world failures (ddx-08e90869's preserved-ref
  pile-up; ddx-15a51f8c's push-race recovery commit). When the sister bead
  splits Land()'s critical section, a regression suite is needed that would
  fail before that fix and pass after.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go have zero coverage
    of withMainGitLock or withTrackerLock.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go exists but covers basic
    acquire/release/break-stale; no concurrent-landing scenarios.
  - The chaos suite under cli/internal/bead/ is single-process
    (goroutine-level); the agent-layer locks defend against multi-process
    contention (worker A and worker B in different processes), which the
    bead-layer chaos suite does not model.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three new chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go
  (or a new file cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go) using a fake
  LandingGitOps that introduces deterministic latency:

  1. TestTrackerLock_LandingPushBlocksTrackerCommit:
     - Worker A calls Land() with PushFFOnly that sleeps 5s.
     - Worker B (goroutine) calls CommitTracker (which uses
       withTrackerLock) immediately after A acquires.
     - Assert: B completes in <1s (proves push runs OUTSIDE the lock
       after the sister fix; would fail today since the lock spans push).

  2. TestTrackerLock_ConcurrentLandDuringPushRaceRecovery:
     - Worker A's Land() hits a non-FF push rejection and enters
       landPushAutoRecover.
     - Worker B's Land() arrives during A's recovery.
     - Assert: B does not exhaust MaxElapsed; B's landing either
       proceeds to push or detects A's advance via FetchOriginAncestryCheck
       and does no-op merge correctly.

  3. TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLand:
     - Simulate a dead worker that advanced UpdateRefTo locally but was
       killed before PushFFOnly.
     - Next worker arrives, finds stale lock (PID dead), breaks it via
       breakStaleTrackerLock at tracker_lock.go:196-218.
     - Assert: next worker detects local-ahead in FetchOriginAncestryCheck
       and pushes cleanly without re-merging.

  Use a per-test sandbox (t.TempDir() or testutils.NewFixtureRepo) plus a
  bare-repo origin so push/fetch is real but local-only. Use synctest or
  channel-based barriers to make the timing deterministic.

NON-SCOPE
  - The fix itself (sister bead handles the implementation refactor).
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps).
  - bead.Store-layer chaos (chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go already
    cover the JSONL/axon layer).
notes: Cancelled per architectural reframe 2026-05-09: with network I/O removed from Land() (sister bead ddx-1f774edf rewritten), push-race scenarios no longer exist in single-node mode. The single useful chaos test that was here (stale-lock-after-partial-land) lives in sister bead ddx-c019e48a.
title: agent/chaos: cover landing-push lock-hold and push-race-recovery races

acceptance:
1. Fixture-based test: 2 workers + server up + restart server mid-flight; assert workers continue + reconnect + backfill in <60s.
2. Same scenario with NO server; assert identical bead outcomes.
3. Operator-cancel test: cancel issued; preserved_for_review reported within 30s with reason operator_cancel.
4. LIVE OBSERVABILITY: start a real ddx work worker against fixture repo with server running; observe via workers panel that worker appears within 30s, freshness state transitions to 'connected', current_bead updates within 60s of bead pickup. Kill worker process; observe transition to 'disconnected' within 2× probe interval.
5. Soak: ≥7 days of operator use without manual intervention; summary at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/soak-summary.md.
6. SOAK ABORT CRITERIA: rollback if any of: (a) any worker's mirror_failures_count exceeds 5 in a 24h window; (b) any silent dropped backfill (had_dropped_backfill flag set on any worker); (c) any operator-initiated SIGTERM of a worker process during soak (workers should not need killing); (d) any bead requires manual git-reset to recover; (e) any 'preserved_orphaned' outcome on a bead the worker actually finished. If any of these fire during soak, file a regression bead, halt the soak clock, and resume after fix lands.
7. CHANGELOG entry merged describing the worker-server architecture change.
description: Uses ddx-50da9674 clean fixture repo. Multi-worker drain WITH server up; restart server mid-flight; verify worker continues + reconnects + backfills. Then drain with NO server; verify identical bead outcomes. Operator-cancel test (cancel issued; preserved_for_review with operator_cancel reason within 30s). Soak: ≥1 week of normal operator use. Final bead.
title: ADR-022 step 9: acceptance + 1-week soak using clean fixture repo

description:
PROBLEM
The screenshot suite is failing across the dashboard, beads, documents, graph, agent, project-overview, plugins, and efficacy pages because the current rendered pages are taller than the stored baselines. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts:133-144` repeatedly reports 1280x720 expectations versus 1280px-tall actuals ranging from 729px to 1113px, which indicates baseline drift in the current shell/layout contract rather than a single-page crash.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts:133-144` captures the stale screenshots and reports the height mismatch.
- The affected pages are the current shell-level routes exercised by the screenshot matrix: dashboard, beads-kanban, documents, graph, agent, project-overview, plugins, and efficacy.

PROPOSED FIX
Investigate the current rendered output and either stabilize the shared shell layout or refresh the affected snapshots to the current contract, keeping the change limited to the screenshot test and snapshot artifacts. Do not expand the scope to unrelated page features.

NON-SCOPE
- Functional route or data-model changes unless they are required to stabilize the screenshot contract.
- Non-screenshot e2e suites.
- Backend data or queue changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` passes for the affected pages.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: refresh screenshot baselines for the current project shell

acceptance: 1. TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md exists with all sections. 2. depends_on: [ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004]. 3. All five locked decisions reflected exactly. 4. Wire transport explicitly GraphQL-only with rationale. 5. Cross-references the plan at /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md as 'background' AND axon-82b6f7b2 as the cross-repo coordination tracker. 6. Adoption gates (alpha/beta/GA) documented with which axon-side beads gate each. 7. ddx doc audit clean. 8. TD picks a concrete TD-NNN ID.
description:
Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

Output: TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md with sections for: GraphQL schema mapping (DDx bead → Axon entity); operation mapping (DDx CLI → GraphQL queries/mutations/subscriptions); deployment + config; concurrency model (OCC via expectedVersion); migration plan; test plan; cross-repo coordination notes (links to axon-82b6f7b2 + FEAT-013/017).
title: TD: axon-backend for bead tracker — schema mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestPreClaimIntakePromptIncludesEstimatedDifficultyGuidance|TestReadinessClassification_DecodesEstimatedDifficulty|TestReadinessClassification_LegacyDecodesEstimatedDifficulty|TestReadinessDifficultyDoesNotPersistBeadMetadata|TestReadinessUsesBeadDifficultyPrecedence' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesFractionalScore|TestPreClaimReadiness_AcceptsStringSuggestedFixes|TestPreClaimIntakeHook_DispatchesWithStandardProfileNoStrongPowerTrick' -count=1` passes.
3. `rg -n 'difficulty MUST be an object with power_class|PowerHint|applyPreClaimPowerHint|triage\.power_hint' cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake* cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go` shows no active readiness persistence path.
4. `rg -n 'SelectStrongestProfile\(|MinPowerOverride:|MaxPower:|score.*power|power.*score' cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake* cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go` shows no readiness dispatch escalation or score-to-power path.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Readiness should estimate bead difficulty using the bead-facing vocabulary from TD-037 (`easy`, `medium`, `hard`). It currently asks for route-shaped `power_class` values and persists the result as bead metadata. The readiness model call itself must run as ordinary lifecycle work: default/standard profile, no strongest-profile selection, no min/max power trick, and no route escalation merely because readiness is important.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:87` models readiness difficulty as `PowerClass`.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:92` treats readiness score as a parsed field; score must remain quality/readiness signal only and must never feed power inference.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:268` is the readiness dispatch routing selector; it must select the standard/default lifecycle profile, not the strongest/smart profile.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:428` asks for `difficulty.power_class` with `cheap|standard|smart`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3517` writes readiness output into `TriagePowerHintKey`.

PROPOSED FIX
Change readiness schema and prompt to `difficulty.estimated_difficulty` with `easy|medium|hard`. Decode canonical and legacy readiness payloads into an in-memory estimated difficulty. Apply precedence in memory: authored bead `triage.estimated_difficulty` first, readiness estimate second, default `medium` last. Do not mutate bead `Extra` during ordinary readiness checks. Keep readiness dispatch on the standard/default lifecycle profile with zero min/max power overrides; the readiness model estimates difficulty, but DDx does not use readiness score as a power input.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add a second durable readiness hint field.
- Do not persist readiness difficulty to `triage.power_hint`, `triage.estimated_difficulty`, Fizeau profile names, or numeric power floors.
- Do not use readiness score as an input to power inference.
- Do not alter retry escalation policy in this bead.
- Do not change Fizeau request feature forwarding in this bead; `EstimatedPromptTokens`/`RequiresTools` is a separate routing-boundary bug bead.

acceptance:
1. WorkerManager.runWorker resolves the ddx binary (os.Executable by default, with a test-only override) and executes ddx work as a child process for bead execution.
2. Spawned ddx work receives MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, FromRev, profile/model/power, and bead selection fields from the validated worker spec.
3. Production runWorker no longer calls agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig, worker.Run, agent.ExecuteBeadWorker, or a production BeadWorkerFactory execution seam; rg -n 'ExecuteBeadWithConfig|ExecuteBeadWorker|worker.Run\(|BeadWorkerFactory' cli/internal/server/workers.go has no production hits except tests or non-execution docs.
4. TestRunWorker_ExecsDDxWorkSubprocess proves the server launches the subprocess with the expected args/env or spec file.
5. TestRunWorker_ProgressBridge_FromChild proves child progress/status output updates worker state without parsing Fizeau inner transcript logs.
6. TestStopExecuteLoopWorker_KillsSubprocess proves stop/watchdog cancellation signals the child process and records terminal worker state.
7. TestServerSpawnWorker_HonorsAllSpecFields verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields reach the subprocess.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestRunWorker|TestStopExecuteLoopWorker|TestWorkerManager|TestServerSpawnWorker' -count=1 passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx server workers still execute bead work in-process instead of spawning the canonical ddx work command. That leaves an alternate execution path inside the server process, duplicates worker lifecycle behavior, and weakens both ADR-022's worker/server boundary and the DDx/Fizeau execution boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:735-969 builds server-local execution configuration and runs bead execution inline instead of delegating to a ddx work subprocess.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go uses BeadWorkerFactory as a production seam around the in-process executor, so server worker behavior can diverge from the installed ddx work CLI.
- Previous attempts parked on missing unified ExecuteLoopSpec wiring; the cleanup still needs to make the subprocess contract explicit and preserve MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev.

PROPOSED FIX
Make WorkerManager.runWorker spawn a ddx subprocess for worker execution, using the current ddx executable by default and a test override for deterministic tests. Translate the validated ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec/ExecuteLoopSpec into ddx work arguments or a spec file, stream child output/events into existing worker status channels, and ensure stop/watchdog behavior signals the child process.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing Fizeau internals.
- Changing bead state semantics.
- Rewriting REST/GraphQL request decoding except as needed to preserve the validated worker spec into the subprocess.

Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates." ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) "server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work. ## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo) Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable): ### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID) Scope: - Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env). - Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works. - Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl. - Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess. - Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path. - Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection. - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green. Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC. ### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec Scope: - handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct. - Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling. - Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal. Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green. Estimated: ~300–500 LOC. ### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude) Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires: - Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port. - Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead. - POST /api/agent/workers/work with --once. - Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status). - Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH. Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented. Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra. ### Dependencies - 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec) - 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape) ## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree. ## Bookkeeping A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing

- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/work with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

2. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers the close-prevention, event body, repair context, and one-cycle scheduling behavior.
3. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins proves the repair attempt raises MinPower above the implementer actual power while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and leaving MaxPower unset unless the operator explicitly set it.

description:
PROBLEM
Fixable review findings currently stop the bead after review instead of scheduling a bounded repair cycle. The old escalation bead ddx-cfedee8e is marked superseded because this scope belongs in C9, but C9 was too broad. This split slice owns exactly one review_fixable_gap repair cycle plus the MinPower bump needed for that repair attempt.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-277 records REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK and applies review triage, but it does not schedule a repair attempt with the review context.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:283-353 reduces the review group to one verdict but does not return a repair_context_from_review_group payload to the attempt path.
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:40-68 escalates MinPower for repeated implementation failures, not for review_fixable_gap repair attempts.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1699-1715 already rebuilds per-attempt config with a requested MinPower while preserving explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins; the repair path needs to reuse that property instead of mutating pins.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:69-94 records reviewer slots and review_group_id, which is the source data the repair context should reference.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a review_fixable_gap classification distinct from terminal review classes.
- When review_fixable_gap is returned, prevent close, append review-fixable-gap evidence, and schedule exactly one bounded repair cycle using repair_context_from_review_group.
- Raise MinPower for the repair attempt above the implementer's actual power using the available ladder/model catalog, but preserve explicit harness/model/provider/profile pins and do not add a MaxPower cap.
- Ensure spec_gap, missing_acceptance, too_large at depth cap, unsafe/out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error remain terminal operator-required states from ddx-c2a66f45 rather than repair attempts.
- Stop after one repair cycle per candidate/result unless a later bead explicitly extends the policy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement candidate refs or same-worktree repair persistence; candidate ref retention is tracked by ddx-7cb3153d and the candidate-cycle epic.
- Do not change the reviewer prompt beyond schema fields needed to classify fixable vs terminal findings.
- Do not change general implementation-failure escalation beyond the repair attempt.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-c2a66f45 for terminal review classification. This bead supersedes the executable scope of ddx-cfedee8e.
title: repair: schedule one bounded review-fixable repair cycle

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work status --all-projects` currently reports live watcher processes but prints `bead=- worktree=-` while those same watchers have active execute-bead children. During the live 2026-05-15 monitor loop, process inspection showed `/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx work --watch ... --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` with child `codex exec -C /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-b6fea19a-20260515T120840-2c0aa694`, but `ddx work status --all-projects` only printed the parent with empty bead/worktree fields.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/work_status.go:79-89` builds the report solely from `workerstatus.Scanner`. `cli/cmd/work_status.go:149-158` prints `-` whenever the scanner row has no inferred bead/worktree. `cli/internal/workerstatus/workerstatus.go:52-88` can infer a bead only from the scanned process argv/cwd, so a long-lived parent `ddx work --watch` has no active bead signal even though its child process and liveness sidecar do. The existing sidecar already records the missing fields: `cli/internal/agent/work/liveness.go:52-67` records current bead/attempt/child PID, `cli/internal/agent/work/liveness.go:87-96` writes `.ddx/workers/<worker-id>/status.json`, and `cli/internal/agent/worker_liveness_sidecar_test.go:150-204` verifies those operator-facing fields are persisted.

PROPOSED FIX
Enrich `ddx work status` rows from fresh worker liveness sidecars, using process scan rows for PID/command/project scope and sidecar records for current bead, attempt, child PID, phase, and last activity when the scan cannot infer an execution worktree. Preserve helper-subcommand filtering and project scoping. For all-projects output, include active bead/worktree/attempt where available for each project instead of requiring operators to run `pgrep` manually.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change worker scheduling, route selection, readiness, or execute-bead landing. Do not make stale sidecar files count as live workers without a matching live process or TTL-safe liveness check. Do not hand-edit `.ddx` tracker files.
title: work status: enrich active bead fields from liveness sidecars

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `ddx init` (or the equivalent template-update path) writes .gitignore entries for `.ddx/attachments/`, `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/`, `.ddx/workers/`.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1 passes.
- CHANGELOG.md notes the reaper + retention behavior + env knobs.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 3

Child implementation beads cover watch/idle CLI semantics with `--poll-interval` removed, loop stop + queue snapshot fields, renderer foundation, DDx lifecycle lines, Fizeau route/progress lines, watch idle/next-ready transition output, and final summaries without exceeding ~3 in-scope files each.

4. `ddx bead dep tree ddx-8b7ed960` shows watch-idle rendering depends on the spec, renderer, watch-mode, and queue-snapshot beads.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` stdout and mode behavior currently mix DDx lifecycle messages, Fizeau route/progress rows, and final summaries without one human-facing contract. Operators see repeated bead ids, unlabeled model/route lines, vague `pre-claim intake` wording, ambiguous `--poll-interval` semantics, and terminal output that does not explain whether work changed code, why the worker exited, or what queue state remains.

CURRENT STATE / ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:251-265 says layer 3 owns queue-drain records and stop disposition, but it does not define the human stdout grammar for a live drain.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:685-702 describes long-running behavior in terms of `--poll-interval`, conflating mode selection with idle sleep duration.
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 exposes `--poll-interval` as the public control for long-running behavior, but the intended operator concepts are `watch` and `idle interval`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:656-699 emits idle/active progress signals but does not print human idle queue status or next-ready-bead transitions.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:722-737 writes bead headers and readiness-start text directly with `fmt.Fprintf`, including repeated bead IDs and legacy `pre-claim intake` wording.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:185-195 writes Fizeau route/progress lines directly to the same writer without DDx phase context.
- cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:331-349 prefixes tool progress with task_id, which often equals the already-scoped bead id.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1885-1937 formats terminal summaries without stop reason, changed/already-satisfied breakdown, queue-state buckets, or human-review blocker explanation.

PROPOSED FIX
Break the work into a spec update plus focused implementation beads:
- Define public `once`, `drain`, `watch`, and `idle interval` semantics.
- Add explicit `--watch` / `--idle-interval` CLI semantics while preserving `--once`.
- Remove `--poll-interval`; do not retain it as a compatibility alias in CLI help, generated docs, or new server/API worker-start inputs.
- Expose stop reason and queue snapshot data, including human-review dependency pressure.
- Add a reusable work-log renderer.
- Route DDx lifecycle lines through it.
- Render Fizeau service events with scoped phase context.
- Render watch idle queue status and next-ready-bead transitions.
- Clean up final summaries.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the structured JSONL event schema or RFC3339 timestamps stored in logs.
- Do not change Fizeau routing policy, ResolveRoute boundaries, retry policy, or actual model selection.
- Do not complete broader terminology cleanup covered by ddx-1c0de0af except where required for `ddx work` stdout.

PARENT / DEPS
Root epic. Child beads carry the executable details and dependency order.
title: EPIC: improve ddx work stdout sequence

description:
PROBLEM
The runs/[runId] page is a single-panel flat view with no tab navigation, no URL-driven tab state, and no separation between Overview, Prompt, Response, Tools, and Session data. Users cannot deep-link to a specific view. Shared sub-components between the runs/[runId] and executions/[executionId] pages are duplicated.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte is a monolithic flat page without tab structure.
- Subcomponents for prompt/response/tools/session panels are inlined or missing; no lib/runDetail/ shared library exists.
- URL query parameter ?tab= is not implemented; tab state is not preserved on navigation.
- Depends on ddx-9228a484 (C9) to stabilize the StopCondition / run record shape before building the Session tab.

PROPOSED FIX
- Promote shared subcomponents from cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte into cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/runDetail/ (tab shell, prompt panel, response panel, tools panel, session panel).
- Rebuild cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed: Overview / Prompt / Response / Tools / Session.
- Implement URL-driven tab state via ?tab=<name> query parameter; deep-link preserves tab on refresh.
- Overview tab: status + last-update timestamp + run/try/work layer chip + base/result rev + link to worker page for running runs.
- Tools tab: paginated 50/page with Load more; lazy-loads on activation.
- Session tab: tokens (prompt/completion/total/cached), billingMode, base/result revs, stderr/stderrPath link.

NON-SCOPE
- Evidence tab (ddx-4cd64068, a child bead).
- New subscription infrastructure (reuse existing workerProgress).
title: Story 16.1: Frontend rebuild runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed run-detail page

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has overlapping work queued for `ddx agent` removal, try/work refactors, Fizeau transcript cleanup, and routinglint guardrails, but several existing beads still preserve deprecated aliases or target `cli/internal/agent` as the future package. That conflicts with the operator decision: remove, do not deprecate; DDx owns task execution; Fizeau owns routing; and `agent` must disappear from DDx-owned internals unless it literally refers to an external AI agent.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 currently still has an `Agent Passthrough` section describing `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still names the upstream boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:19-23. FEAT-010 still describes layer 1 as consuming upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. Open beads ddx-387a0178 and ddx-3ee4ddcf still preserve deprecated aliases or target `cli/internal/agent`, while upstream Fizeau changes are now tracked in ../fizeau as fizeau-06524f43, fizeau-61668fd3, and fizeau-400b57fe.

PROPOSED FIX
Use this epic as the governing DDx-side sequence: update specs first, wait for/consume the upstream Fizeau contract cleanup release, remove the `ddx agent` public surface with no alias, rename DDx-owned execution packages/types away from `agent`, update skills/generated docs, then add lint guardrails that prevent routing or vocabulary regressions.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement Fizeau model matching or native harness rename in the DDx repo. Do not retain compatibility aliases for `ddx agent`. Do not add DDx-side model/provider/harness defaulting or fuzzy matching. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; all sequencing changes go through `ddx bead` commands.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-06524f43: Align Fizeau contract with DDx routing-boundary cleanup
- fizeau-61668fd3: Rename Fizeau native harness identity from agent to fiz
- fizeau-400b57fe: Define Fizeau-owned fuzzy model constraint resolution

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-e34994e2. Dependencies: child beads define the execution order.

acceptance:

Add `TestExecuteBeadWorker_UnmergedTaskSucceededResultDoesNotRetry` (or the closest existing execute-loop test name if the file's naming convention requires it) that simulates a successful attempt with `ExitCode=0`, `Outcome=task_succeeded`, and `ResultRev != BaseRev` while the bead remains worker-ready, then asserts the work loop does not dispatch a second agent attempt for that bead.
2. Add or extend `TestRecoverDanglingSuccess_EmitsEventWhenResultRevUnreachable` so the unreachable successful `result_rev` path no longer returns a silent retry decision; it must land/close, preserve with explicit ref, or stop with a terminal operator-visible result.

description:
PROBLEM
During live monitoring on 2026-05-15, `ddx work --watch` ran bead `ddx-5baa6a15` successfully twice but did not land or close either successful attempt. Attempt `20260515T222727-7b672c7c` produced worker commit `dabf77e68`; attempt `20260515T223843-6eb42169` produced worker commit `2776da59a`. Both had successful cost records / exit_code=0 evidence. Instead of treating that success as terminal, the supervisor made additional checkpoint/tracker commits (`66f879766`, `259577d7c`, `0c5bea300`, `b0f986426`) and dispatched a third attempt `20260515T224956-e89ea1e0` for the same bead. The operator had to terminate the watcher, manually merge `2776da59a` via merge commit `a14abb332`, verify, close the bead, and commit `.ddx/attachments/ddx-5baa6a15/events.jsonl`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1355-1363` only invokes dangling-success recovery when the reclaimed candidate status is `in_progress`. If the successful attempt left the bead open/unclaimed, the next loop iteration can re-execute without first reconciling the prior `task_succeeded` result. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_dangling_success.go:200-207` documents recovery for prior successes, but `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_dangling_success.go:230-243` treats an unreachable/unmerged successful `result_rev` as a diagnostic-only condition and returns `(false, nil)`, allowing a fresh attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:165-176` says successful non-base results are a landing obligation (`LandBeadResult` should merge or preserve), but the work loop does not enforce that terminal obligation before retrying the same bead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the success reconciliation path so any prior `task_succeeded` result with `ResultRev != BaseRev` for the same bead is handled before the worker dispatches another attempt, regardless of whether the bead is currently `open` or stale `in_progress`.
- For an unreachable/unmerged successful `result_rev`, either land and close it, preserve it under a durable `refs/ddx/iterations/...` ref with an explicit non-retry outcome, or stop with an operator-visible failure. It must not silently checkpoint and run the bead again.
- Emit an operator-visible event/log for every successful commit that cannot be landed, including bead id, attempt id, result rev, preserve ref or failure reason.
- Keep the existing reachable-result idempotent close behavior, but add coverage for the observed `ddx-5baa6a15` shape: success evidence exists while the bead remains worker-ready.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change worker prompt/staging behavior; `ddx-213d7e95` covers serialized worker git commands and staged hook gates.
- Do not change false dirty-preservation handling; `ddx-b5039a2c` covers pre-dispatch dirty preservation after land.
- Do not change tracker auto-commit locking; `ddx-3f9a89a6` covers tracker git write serialization.
- Do not rewrite existing execute-bead history or manually edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

DEPS
No deps. This is a supervisor correctness bug exposed by the `ddx-5baa6a15` incident.
title: work: land successful detached-head attempts before retrying

acceptance:
1. `*Store.AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind` take `ctx context.Context` first. Compile-time assertions `var _ BeadEventReader = (*Store)(nil)` and `var _ BeadEventWriter = (*Store)(nil)` compile.
2. All non-test callers that invoke these methods on `*Store` pass `ctx` through. Test: `TestStore_AppendEvent_AcceptsContext`, `TestStore_Events_AcceptsContext`, `TestStore_EventsByKind_AcceptsContext`.
3. At least 3 caller files have field types narrowed from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadEventReader` or `bead.BeadEventWriter` (or composite) — concrete examples: `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go` (uses `EventsByKind` + `AppendEvent`; narrow to `BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter`). Remaining caller narrowing happens in BL.
4. Existing event tests (`cli/internal/bead/events_test.go` if present, `chaos_test.go` event-ordering assertions) continue to pass.
5. Event monotonicity invariant (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8) holds — appended events have monotonically non-decreasing `created_at`. Test: `TestStore_AppendEvent_MaintainsMonotonicTimestamps`.
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 event-log methods (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) used widely across the agent/server/cmd layers. Per TD-027 §6 these belong on `BeadEventReader` and `BeadEventWriter` sub-interfaces; per TD-027 every interface method takes `ctx context.Context` first. The Foundation bead declares those interfaces but deliberately leaves `*Store`'s event methods unchanged so event caller migration remains a focused, testable slice here.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/bead/events*.go` define `AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error`, `Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)`, `EventsByKind(id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error)` — no ctx. After the Foundation bead, `BeadEventReader` and `BeadEventWriter` interfaces have ctx-first signatures, but `*Store` does not satisfy them until this bead updates the event methods and callers.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind` to take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter.
2. Add compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadEventReader = (*Store)(nil)` and `var _ BeadEventWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Migrate callers that hold `*Store` and use event methods — thread ctx through the existing call sites:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go`, `bead_evidence.go` (operator evidence commands)
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go`, `resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `resolver_sub_bead.go`
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` (event appends on state transitions)
   - Other event-emitting paths
4. Where the caller narrows naturally to event-only access (e.g., a resolver that only reads/appends events), update the field type from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadEventReader` or `bead.BeadEventWriter` (or a composite). This is opportunistic narrowing; full narrowing pass happens in BL.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event vocabulary (`kind` values) is documented in TD-027 §13; not modified here.
- Event externalization to attachments (TD-027 §17) — separate concern; not modified.
- Non-event foundational method ctx threading — F bead `ddx-c6317784`.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, claim, etc.) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:events, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (Foundation — BeadEventReader/Writer interfaces declared).
title: E: Event log — add ctx to AppendEvent/Events/EventsByKind, satisfy BeadEventReader/Writer (TD-027 §6)

description:
PROBLEM
The factory workflow needs to improve or split poor beads before implementation is attempted. Current queue work only covers a Step 0 prompt hint inside the implementer prompt, which is too late: an agent can claim an ambiguous or feature-sized bead, fail, and burn retry budget before the system improves the input.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 invokes PreDispatchLintHook before claim, but the hook is lint-oriented and does not expose the required intake outcomes actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, or intake_error.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:86 constructs NewPreDispatchLintHook, which emits lint results but does not safely rewrite bead text or create child beads.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds Step 0 decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, so decomposition happens after dispatch rather than before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1399 still says auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2, while docs/triage/decomposition.md now defines the intake gate and default max_decomposition_depth policy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a PreClaimIntakeHook/guard that runs after ready selection and routing preflight but before Claim.
- Intake returns one of: actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, intake_error.
- For actionable_but_rewritten, mutate the bead only when the rewrite preserves intent, narrows wording, adds missing file/test references already inferable from repo evidence, and does not invent behavior or scope.
- For too_large_decomposed, create child beads with standalone descriptions/AC, dependency wiring, and a parent triage-decomposed event containing child_ids, rationale, and ac_map. The parent moves to blocked/decomposed instead of being executed.
- For ambiguous_needs_human, block with needs_human/triage:spec-gap labels and an operator-readable explanation.
- Intake errors fail open only when the hook infrastructure fails; a valid ambiguous/too-large decision must block/decompose, not proceed.
- No intake-only outcome counts as no-progress implementation failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementer prompt Step 0 cleanup beyond removing contradictions after the pre-claim gate exists.
- Human UI for editing rewritten beads.
- Full skill rubric redesign beyond the outcomes required here.
notes: DECOMPOSED after worker no_changes_needs_investigation: ddx-12638823 (typed hook), ddx-07206d47 (safe rewrite), ddx-008d288f (AC-map decomposition), ddx-a5696aac (no-progress accounting). Do not claim the parent directly.
title: agent: add pre-claim intake gate for actionability and AC-preserving decomposition

description:
PROBLEM
Durable DDx audit outputs still appear as uncommitted local dirt after normal worker attempts. Today the live monitor saw `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` appended and `.ddx/beads.jsonl` outcome rows changed, while the worker continued. The operator-facing behavior must be simple: if DDx writes durable tracked audit state, it commits it; if the state is volatile, it never writes it to tracked files.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:548-571` appends attempt rows to `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` through `attemptmetrics.AppendRow` without staging or committing the metrics file.
- `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:19` defines `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` as the durable per-attempt analytics table, so ignoring it would contradict ADR-026.
- `cli/internal/agent/commit_outcome.go:34-60` handles store mutation errors after outcomes, but successful durable tracker mutations are not wrapped in a transaction that commits the resulting `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, and related attachment writes.
- Existing bead `ddx-051500ff` covers attachment auto-commit; this bead covers the remaining metrics/outcome tracker writes so the system has one coherent tracked-output policy.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a single DDx-managed tracked-output commit path for worker attempts. After an attempt appends attempt metrics or applies a terminal durable tracker mutation, stage only DDx-managed durable paths that changed (`.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`, and attachment files covered by `ddx-051500ff`) and create one audit commit such as `chore: update tracker (execute-bead <attempt-id>)` or a more specific metrics/outcome message. If the commit fails, stop the worker with operator attention instead of continuing to accumulate dirty tracked state.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not auto-commit arbitrary implementation files.
- Do not change attempt metrics schema.
- Do not duplicate attachment reconciliation work owned by `ddx-051500ff`; compose with it or depend on it.
- Do not hide commit failures behind best-effort warnings.
title: work: auto-commit durable worker audit outputs

acceptance:
1. The default DDx plugin includes a human-writing-support skill with valid agentskills.io frontmatter and a trigger description under the project convention limit.
2. `rg -n "not an AI detector|detector bypass|specific|voice|technical|planning|public|ddx doc prose"` against the skill file returns matches for required guidance.
3. Existing skill validation command passes, or if this repo uses EXEC-011, the focused validation for shipped skills passes.
4. The skill does not require agents to remove legitimate technical lists, headings, or DDx terminology.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
CONTEXT
DDx should guide agents while they write or edit prose, separate from deterministic post-write checks. This bead ships a default-plugin skill named human-writing-support that adapts the stronger advisory guidance from human-writing-style approaches to DDx: preserve voice, prefer specific detail, avoid generic AI-like tropes, and do not trade away technical precision.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- library/.agents/skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md or the default-plugin skill path established by FEAT-011/TD-036
- skills/ddx/human-writing-support/SKILL.md and .agents/.claude materialized copies only if this repo maintains generated checked-in mirrors
- skill validation/eval fixtures under the existing skills validation surface

REQUIRED SKILL BEHAVIOR
- Trigger for writing, rewriting, editing, or reviewing DDx prose, specs, ADRs, beads, docs, website copy, and public prose.
- Explicitly says it is not an AI detector or detector-bypass tool.
- Preserves user voice and technical precision.
- Applies different expectations for technical, planning, and public prose.
- Encourages concrete/checkable detail over generic importance language.
- Directs agents to run deterministic prose checks when available.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- CLI checker implementation.
- Rule pack implementation.
- Bead review integration.
title: Ship human-writing-support skill in the default DDx plugin

description:
PROBLEM
The run-detail page and evidence browser need a single end-to-end regression test that exercises prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence together, plus the p95 dataset size check. The current Playwright coverage only checks the evidence tab slice.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs-evidence.spec.ts:241-305 currently covers evidence tab behavior, but it does not assert the complete prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence flow in one scenario or the 200-tool-call/30-file render-frame budget.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add one Playwright scenario named `full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence`.
- Cover prompt visible, response visible, tool trace visible, and evidence list visible states in a single run-detail flow.
- Add the p95 dataset fixture/check for 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files and keep the tabs interactive within one render frame.

NON-SCOPE
- Server audit semantics.
- Spec prose edits.
- Any new auth or RBAC behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...)` exists in the Playwright suite.
2. The scenario asserts prompt visible, response visible, tool trace visible, and evidence list visible in the same flow.
3. The p95 dataset check covers 200 tool calls and 30 evidence files and preserves one-frame interactivity.
4. `bun run test:e2e` green.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
