acceptance:
1. `ddx work --help` documents `--watch` and `--idle-interval` as the primary long-running worker surface, with examples for `ddx work`, `ddx work --once`, `ddx work --watch`, and `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s`.
2. `ddx work --help` and generated CLI docs no longer contain `--poll-interval`; `ddx work --poll-interval=30s` fails with an unknown-flag error.
3. `ddx work` with no watch flag keeps drain-and-exit behavior on an empty execution-ready queue; `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` covers the default.
4. `ddx work --watch` keeps running across an empty queue until context cancellation or signal; `TestWorkWatchModeStaysAliveWithEmptyQueue` covers the watch path.
5. `ddx work --once` still processes at most one ready bead and exits; existing `TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits` remains green.
6. `ddx work --once --watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--once` and `--watch` are mutually exclusive.
7. `ddx work --idle-interval 15s` without `--watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--idle-interval` only applies in watch mode.
8. Server-managed workers created through REST and GraphQL default to watch mode with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly selects once/drain behavior. New request/input shapes use `mode` and `idle_interval`, not `poll_interval`.
9. The loop/runtime no longer relies on raw `PollInterval > 0` as the only semantic mode test; mode and idle interval are represented explicitly at the command/runtime boundary.
10. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWork(DefaultDrain|Watch|Once|IdleInterval|PollIntervalRemoved)|TestLoop_(StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|OnceFlagStillExits|ExplicitDrainExits)|TestStartExecuteLoop'` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently uses `--poll-interval` as the public way to request a long-running worker. That name is ambiguous because drain mode also scans the queue between attempts; the real operator choice is whether the process exits when execution-ready work dries up or keeps watching for newly arriving work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 defines `--poll-interval` with default 0 and documents it as the continuous scanning control.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:649-682 treats `PollInterval > 0` as the semantic switch from drain-and-exit to idle/watch behavior.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2425-2437 and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:112-124 default server-managed workers to a 30s poll interval, but the persisted/request vocabulary still names only the interval, not the worker mode.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md will define the intended vocabulary in ddx-7d9e1e82: `once`, `drain`, `watch`, and `idle interval`, with no `--poll-interval` compatibility alias.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement explicit watch/drain semantics in the CLI and server worker entry points:
- Add `ddx work --watch` for long-running behavior: drain ready work, then remain idle and rescan for newly arriving execution-ready beads.
- Add `ddx work --idle-interval <duration>` for the sleep between empty-queue scans in watch mode, defaulting to 30s when watch mode is active.
- Keep direct CLI `ddx work` as drain-and-exit unless `--watch` is supplied. `ddx work --once` remains the one-bead mode.
- Treat `--once` and `--watch` as incompatible and return a usage error when both are supplied.
- Treat `--idle-interval` without `--watch` as a usage error. The interval tunes watch mode; it does not implicitly select a mode.
- Remove `--poll-interval` from `ddx work` entirely. It is not retained as a compatibility alias. Help and generated docs must not list it.
- Replace new server-managed worker request/input vocabulary with `mode` (`once`, `drain`, `watch`) and `idle_interval`; do not accept `poll_interval` for new worker starts. Existing persisted worker records may be read best-effort if necessary, but this bead must not expose `poll_interval` as a supported input alias.
- Keep server-managed workers in watch mode by default with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly asks for once/drain semantics.
- Use one internal representation for mode + idle interval so callers do not branch directly on raw `PollInterval > 0`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign route/provider/model passthrough.
- Do not change success/failure classification, bead closing policy, cost accounting, or retry cooldown rules.
- Do not implement final human summary wording; ddx-dc300be9 consumes this once loop state is explicit.
- Do not perform ExecuteLoopSpec unification; that remains owned by ddx-29058e2a and its server children.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. Depends on ddx-7d9e1e82 for the documented public semantics.
title: work: add explicit watch mode and idle interval semantics

acceptance:
1. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_XDGPath asserts Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`.
2. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_LocalFallback covers the no-remote `local/<basename>-<sha>/` form.
3. TestDDxRoot_URLParsing covers https, ssh, custom-host, missing-remote cases (table-driven).
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_XDGPath asserts Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`.
- TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_LocalFallback covers the no-remote `local/<basename>-<sha>/` form.
- TestDDxRoot_URLParsing covers https, ssh, custom-host, missing-remote cases (table-driven).

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 2

acceptance:
1. Audit report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md exists, covers at minimum: ddx agent run (CLI + REST + MCP variants), ddx work passthrough behavior, GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (and any other GraphQL adapters), server worker variants, ddx bead create.
2. For each command: programmatically-generated field tables for each layer (cobra / REST request struct / GraphQL adapter / spec / consumer); diff between layers; explicit drop count per command.
3. Drop-risk score (count of fields dropped between any two layers) explicit per command.
4. Summary table at top of report.
5. For each high-drop-risk command: a follow-up bead filed with ExecuteLoopSpec refactor as template; bead ID listed in the report.
6. For commands judged clean: explicit rationale ("only 1 layer" or "fields collapse via X" — not just "looks fine").
7. ddx try and ddx run, if not shipped, recorded as N/A with one-line rationale; not chased.
8. Report committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ and bead closed once review confirms coverage.
description:
The ExecuteLoopSpec unification (ddx-29058e2a) addresses one instance of the parallel-struct smell. The same anti-pattern likely exists in other ddx commands where flags travel cobra → function param → loose map → HTTP request struct → spec struct → consumer. Find them and either:
- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR
- Document them as already-clean (and why).

COMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)

1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.
2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent work with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)
3. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) — covered by ddx-29058e2a explicitly, but audit any OTHER GraphQL adapters with similar patterns
4. Server-side worker variants in cli/internal/server/workers.go — anything else that takes a Spec struct
5. ddx bead create / update — flag set is small but check whether server endpoint mirrors
6. ddx server commands (ddx server workers list/show/log) — read-only, less risk but verify
7. ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. The runTry function constructs config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in parallel to runWork's runAgentExecuteLoopImpl (see comment at try.go:155 acknowledging the duplication). Audit whether the same parallel-struct smell that plagues ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec also exists at the cobra-→-spec boundary for try, and if so file a follow-up unification bead. (Stale 2026-05-04 note "ddx try NOT shipped" was corrected 2026-05-07.)
8. ddx run (cli/cmd/run.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. Layer-1 invocation primitive; flags forward to Fizeau Execute. Lower drop-risk than try/work but verify cobra → service request shape parity.

AUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)

For each command:
1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).
2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).
3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).
4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).
5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.
6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.

OUTPUT

Report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md:
- One section per command audited
- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)
- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation

If unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.

DEP CHANGE (per codex)
- DEP on ddx-29058e2a is RELAXED to "may begin read-only command discovery + diff generation in parallel; only the implementation-pattern recommendations need ddx-29058e2a as reference." Update dep semantics so this can start now.
title: audit: parallel-struct smell — find other ddx commands with multi-layer flag wiring

description:
PROBLEM
Both execute-bead prompt strings (Claude: 1078 words, Agent: 1020 words) contain prose paragraphs that consume more tokens than equivalent bullet checklists. 19 load-bearing guardrails are embedded in prose without explicit enumeration, making omission hard to detect. No invariant tests verify that selectors and the missing-governing path work correctly.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package): the shared blocks (extracted by ddx-e2171a2c, a dep) are prose paragraphs averaging ~25 words each. Equivalent checklists would cut to ~10 words per guardrail.
- No FEAT-022-referencing comment block exists that enumerates all 19 guardrails inline.
- No invariant tests exist for prompt selectors (which harness-specific block is chosen) or missing-governing path (behavior when governing doc is absent).
- ddx-e2171a2c (shared block extraction, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Tighten shared blocks: convert prose paragraphs to bullet checklists targeting >= 30% word reduction on both Claude and Agent variants (~400 words saved on Claude, ~450 on Agent).
- Verify all 19 guardrails preserved: enumerate each guardrail in a comment or test.
- Add FEAT-022-referencing comment block listing all 19 guardrails inline (with FEAT-022 cross-reference).
- Add invariant tests:
  - TestPromptSelector_CorrectBlock: verifies each harness gets its specific block.
  - TestPromptSelector_MissingGoverning: verifies missing-governing path uses fallback or errors predictably.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing prompt semantics or dropping any of the 19 guardrails.
- Adding new guardrails (those go in the rubric update).

description:
PROBLEM
The current bead mixes a focused worktree-registry behavior change with a repo-wide path-audit sweep. That blocks execution because the registry work is small and local, while the audit spans hundreds of call sites.

ROOT CAUSE

There is no operator command surface for `ddx project worktree master <path>`, so operators cannot re-pin `worktrees.json.master` even though the design requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go`: expose safe registry read/update helpers for `worktrees.json` and reuse the bootstrap lock for operator writes.
- `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go`: add `TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster` covering a second checkout that registers without replacing master.

acceptance:
1. AC of the form '<command>' returns/exits/passes/fails/outputs <expr>. is scored verifiable=true.
2. 'ls foo | wc -l returns 7' (no quotes) is also detected.

description:
The pre-claim AC-quality review scores quoted-command ACs as kind:prose, verifiable:false even when they ARE runnable commands. Repro from fizeau bead fizeau-e08d8228 (commit 96691937 of fizeau):

AC text (markdown prose with embedded commands):
  1. 'ls scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/*.yaml | wc -l' returns 7.
  2. 'python -c "import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in __import__(\"glob\").glob(\"scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/*.yaml\")]"' exits 0.
  3. 'python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(\"scripts/benchmark/concurrency-groups.yaml\"))"' exits 0.
  4. Each bench-set's task list is a subset of the current terminalbench-2-1-sweep.yaml subsets entries.

Scoring event captured on the bead:
  kind:ac-quality-low
  source:preclaim-ac-quality
  summary:score=0.00 threshold=0.50 verifiable=0/4

All four ACs got kind:prose, verifiable:false. ACs 1-3 are clearly runnable; the parser is missing the heuristic that 'command' as a single-quoted string at the start of an AC line is verifiable.

Likely fix: in the AC-quality parser, treat lines beginning with a single- or backtick-quoted shell-like token followed by 'returns', 'exits', 'passes', 'fails', or 'outputs' as verifiable=true regardless of surrounding prose.

Impact: every bead created with my AC convention gets a needs-refinement label and a low pre-claim score, which may eventually cause beads to be deferred or skipped. ACs that ARE verifiable should not be scored as prose just because they have a sentence wrapper.
title: ac-quality: command-in-prose ACs misclassified as unverifiable

2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWorkDirtyTreeCheckpointReturnsOperatorAttention' -count=1` passes.
3. The dirty-tree test fixture with two ready beads proves only the first bead is claimed then released; the second bead is never claimed while the same dirty-tree blocker remains.
4. The emitted event/result includes the project root and dirty path list, and the operator-facing message includes `commit or clean` guidance.

description:
PROBLEM
When the project worktree has uncommitted implementation changes, the pre-execute checkpoint correctly refuses to absorb them, but `ddx work --watch` keeps retrying ready beads from the same dirty tree. This produces repeated failed attempt events and gives the operator the appearance of active worker progress even though no actual build can start until the local tree is committed or cleaned.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:567` computes dirty paths before dispatch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:586` returns `checkpoint refused to absorb implementation changes outside DDx bookkeeping` for non-DDx-bookkeeping dirty paths.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1713` handles that report in watch mode by unclaiming, logging, sleeping, and continuing, so the same project-level blocker recurs across the queue.

PROPOSED FIX
Treat pre-execute dirty-tree checkpoint refusal as a project-level operator attention condition, not an ordinary retryable bead failure. `ddx work` must release the candidate bead, emit one structured event naming the project root and dirty paths, and stop the worker before claiming another bead. `ddx work --watch` must not loop forever on this condition; it should exit or park in an explicit operator-attention terminal result that does not claim additional beads. The stdout/stderr message must tell the operator to commit or clean the listed implementation files before restarting the worker.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not allow `ddx work` to auto-commit arbitrary implementation files.
- Do not add a hidden force flag that bypasses dirty-tree protection.
- Do not change allowed DDx-bookkeeping checkpoint paths.
- Do not mark the bead itself blocked; the blocker is the local project tree, not the bead content.
title: work: stop retry churn after pre-execute dirty-tree checkpoint refusal
